174.description_ja_short: 畑薙湖の東に立つ静かな山。赤石岳や荒川三山を一望する。
174.description_en_long: Aonagiyama is a shapely 2,406 m mountain rising east of Lake Hatanagi in the southern Southern Alps. Overshadowed by higher neighbors and visited by few, its summit nonetheless commands a sweeping view of Akaishidake and the Arakawa Sanzan.
174.description_en_short: A quiet mountain east of Lake Hatanagi, with views of Akaishidake and the Arakawa Sanzan.

175.description_ja_long: 北八ヶ岳の一角、針葉樹の立ち枯れが白い横縞を描く縞枯現象が名の由来の標高2,403mの山。枯れ木の根元では幼木が育ち、森は世代交代を繰り返している。
175.description_ja_short: 立ち枯れの白い縞模様が名の由来。北八ヶ岳の森深い山。
175.description_en_long: Shimagareyama, a 2,403 m peak in northern Yatsugatake, takes its name from the shimagare phenomenon, in which dead standing conifers form white horizontal stripes. Saplings grow at the feet of the dead trees, and the forest keeps renewing itself in cycles.
175.description_en_short: Named for the white stripes of dead standing trees; a deeply forested peak in northern Yatsugatake.

176.description_ja_long: 頸城三山の一峰をなす新潟県唯一の活火山で標高2,400m。釣鐘状の山容は上高地の焼岳と形も高さも名前もよく似ていて、今も活動を続けている。
176.description_ja_short: 新潟県唯一の活火山。焼岳に似た釣鐘状の頸城三山の一峰。
176.description_en_long: Yakeyama, one of the Kubiki Sanzan, is Niigata Prefecture's only active volcano at 2,400 m. Its bell-shaped form closely resembles Yakedake above Kamikochi in shape, height and even name, and the volcano remains active to this day.
176.description_en_short: Niigata's only active volcano, a bell-shaped peak of the Kubiki Sanzan resembling Yakedake.

177.description_ja_long: 白山の主峰群の南に少し離れて立つことが名の由来とされる標高2,399mの峰。美濃禅定道の要衝として登拝者を迎え、直下の別山平にはお花畑が広がる。
177.description_ja_short: 白山から離れて立つのが名の由来。禅定道が通う信仰の峰。
177.description_en_long: Bessan, a 2,399 m peak, is said to take its name from standing slightly apart to the south of Hakusan's main summits. It welcomed pilgrims as a key point on the Mino Zenjodo pilgrimage route, and alpine flower fields spread across Bessandaira just below the top.
177.description_en_short: Named for standing apart from Hakusan; a peak of worship on the Zenjodo pilgrimage route.

178.description_ja_long: 八ヶ岳連峰の南端近く、主稜線から西へ張り出した尾根上に立つ標高2,398mの山。三角錐の整った頂は隣の編笠山と似た趣で、八ヶ岳や南アルプスを望む。
178.description_ja_short: 編笠山と似た三角錐の頂をもつ、八ヶ岳南端付近の展望峰。
178.description_en_long: Gongendake stands at 2,398 m on a ridge jutting west from the main crest near the southern end of the Yatsugatake range. Its well-formed triangular summit has a character much like neighboring Amigasayama, and it looks out over Yatsugatake and the Southern Alps.
178.description_en_short: A viewpoint peak near the southern end of Yatsugatake, with a triangular summit like Amigasayama's.

179.description_ja_long: 南アルプス深南部に天を突く姿でそびえる標高2,392mの双耳峰。長野側の池口集落にちなむ名で、静岡側では二つの頂の姿から十釈迦とも呼ばれてきた。
179.description_ja_short: 深南部に天を突く双耳峰。静岡側では十釈迦とも呼ばれる。
179.description_en_long: Ikeguchidake is a 2,392 m twin-peaked mountain soaring skyward in the deep southern reaches of the Southern Alps. Named after the village of Ikeguchi on the Nagano side, it has also long been called Toshaka on the Shizuoka side for the look of its two summits.
179.description_en_short: A twin-peaked mountain piercing the sky in the deep southern Alps, also called Toshaka in Shizuoka.

180.description_ja_long: 富山県魚津市と黒部市の境、立山連峰の北に連なる毛勝三山の一峰。片貝川の源流をなし、山麓の雪渓近くには山菜が豊かに自生する。標高2,378m。
180.description_ja_short: 毛勝三山の一峰で片貝川の源流。残雪と山菜の恵み豊かな山。
180.description_en_long: Nekomatayama is one of the Kekachi Sanzan, ranging north of the Tateyama mountains on the border of Uozu and Kurobe in Toyama. Rising to 2,378 m, it forms the headwaters of the Katakai River, and edible wild plants grow richly near the snow patches at its foot.
180.description_en_short: One of the Kekachi Sanzan and source of the Katakai River, blessed with lingering snow and wild plants.

181.description_ja_long: 男体山の北東に立つ日光火山群の溶岩円頂丘。山体を家族に見立てる日光連山では母にあたるとされ、頂に御嶽神社を祭る山岳信仰の中心の山。
181.description_ja_short: 日光連山で母と見立てられた信仰の峰。頂に御嶽神社を祭る。
181.description_en_long: Nyohosan is a lava dome of the Nikko volcanic group standing northeast of Nantaisan. In the Nikko range, whose peaks are likened to a family, it is regarded as the mother, and with Mitake Shrine enshrined on its summit it has been a center of mountain worship.
181.description_en_short: Seen as the mother of the Nikko range; a peak of worship with Mitake Shrine on its summit.

182.description_ja_long: 中央アルプスの将棊頭山から北へ延びる尾根上の標高2,375mの山。塩尻市と木曽町の境で分水嶺をなし、雨水は信濃川と木曽川へ分かれ下る。
182.description_ja_short: 中央アルプス北部の分水嶺。信濃川と木曽川に水を分ける。
182.description_en_long: Otanairiyama is a 2,375 m mountain on the ridge running north from Shogigashirayama in the Central Alps. Sitting on the border of Shiojiri and Kiso Town, it forms a watershed divide, sending rainwater down to the Shinano and Kiso rivers on either side.
182.description_en_short: A watershed peak in the northern Central Alps, dividing waters between the Shinano and Kiso rivers.

183.description_ja_long: 戦場ヶ原の北東にどっしり構える日光火山群の溶岩円頂丘。男体山と女峰山の長男とされ、頂に太郎山神社を祭り、四方の展望に恵まれる。
183.description_ja_short: 男体山と女峰山の長男と伝わる日光の峰。展望は四方に開く。
183.description_en_long: Tarosan is a lava dome of the Nikko volcanic group sitting solidly northeast of Senjogahara. Regarded as the eldest son of Nantaisan and Nyohosan, it enshrines Tarosan Shrine on its summit and is blessed with views opening in every direction.
183.description_en_short: A Nikko peak held to be the eldest son of Nantaisan and Nyohosan, with views opening on all sides.

184.description_ja_long: 中央アルプス南部、飯田市と大桑村の境に横たわる標高2,363mの山。雅で都風な名の由来は不詳といい、頂は樹林とクマザサに深く覆われる。
184.description_ja_short: クマザサに包まれた中ア南部の静峰。雅な名の由来は謎。
184.description_en_long: Anpeijiyama lies at 2,363 m on the border of Iida City and Okuwa Village in the southern Central Alps. The origin of its elegant, courtly-sounding name is said to be unknown, and its summit is deeply covered in forest and kumazasa bamboo grass.
184.description_en_short: A quiet peak in the southern Central Alps wrapped in bamboo grass; its graceful name remains a mystery.

185.description_ja_long: 尾瀬沼の北にそびえる東北以北の最高峰。この火山が只見川を堰き止めて尾瀬を生んだとされ、名は残雪を火打ちばさみに見立てたという。
185.description_ja_short: 尾瀬を生んだ火山。東北以北の最高峰として湿原を見守る。
185.description_en_long: Hiuchigatake, rising north of Lake Ozenuma, is the highest peak from the Tohoku region northward. The volcano is said to have dammed the Tadami River and given birth to Oze, and its name reportedly comes from a snow patch likened to fire-striking tongs.
185.description_en_short: The volcano that created Oze; the highest peak from Tohoku northward, watching over the marshland.

186.description_ja_long: 長野・群馬県境の標高2,354m峰。屋根の棟に似た山容が名の由来とされ、日本武尊が「あずまはや」と嘆いた伝説が嬬恋村の名にも残る。
186.description_ja_short: 屋根の棟に似た姿が名の由来。日本武尊伝説を秘める百名山。
186.description_en_long: Azumayasan is a 2,354 m peak on the border of Nagano and Gunma. Its name is said to come from a silhouette resembling the ridge of a roof, and the legend of Yamato Takeru lamenting "Azuma haya" here also survives in the name of Tsumagoi Village.
186.description_en_short: Named for its roof-ridge shape; a celebrated mountain steeped in the legend of Yamato Takeru.

187.description_ja_long: 戸隠連峰の最高峰で、三角錐の姿から戸隠富士とも呼ばれる標高2,353mの峰。修験の道の名残で、一不動から十阿弥陀まで石仏が稜線に続く。
187.description_ja_short: 戸隠富士と呼ばれる連峰最高峰。稜線に修験の石仏が続く。
187.description_en_long: Takatsumayama, the highest peak of the Togakushi range, rises to 2,353 m and is also called the Togakushi Fuji for its triangular form. As a legacy of the shugendo ascetic path, stone Buddhist figures from Ichi-Fudo to Ju-Amida line the ridge toward the top.
187.description_en_short: The Togakushi range's highest peak, called the Togakushi Fuji; stone figures of shugendo line its ridge.

189.description_ja_long: 井川湖の西に構える南アルプス深南部の重鎮。小無間山と吊尾根で結ばれた大きな山容を見せ、広い草地の頂には一等三角点が置かれている。
189.description_ja_short: 南アルプス深南部の重鎮。一等三角点の広い頂をもつ。
189.description_en_long: Daimugenzan is a heavyweight of the deep southern Southern Alps, standing west of Lake Ikawa. It presents a massive form linked to Shomugenzan by a suspended ridge, and a first-order triangulation point sits on its broad, grassy summit.
189.description_en_short: A giant of the deep southern Southern Alps, its broad summit bearing a first-order triangulation point.

190.description_ja_long: 日光連山の一峰で、深山の趣を今に残す静かな山。頂は樹林に包まれるが、そのすぐ先には展望のよい場所があり、日光の山々を見渡せる。
190.description_ja_short: 深山の趣を残す日光連山の静かな峰。頂の先に展望が開ける。
190.description_en_long: Nyohosan is a peak of the Nikko range, a quiet mountain that preserves the atmosphere of remote deep forest to this day. Though the summit itself is wrapped in trees, a spot with fine views lies just beyond it, offering a panorama of the mountains of Nikko.
190.description_en_short: A quiet peak of the Nikko range with a deep-mountain feel; views open up just beyond the wooded summit.

191.description_ja_long: 志賀高原を代表する山で、日本海と太平洋の分水嶺にあたる。山腹を横に絡む道を「横手」と呼んだことが名の由来とされ、樹氷でも名高い。
191.description_ja_short: 志賀高原の代表峰。太平洋と日本海を分ける樹氷の山。
191.description_en_long: Yokoteyama is a representative mountain of the Shiga Highlands, sitting on the divide between the Sea of Japan and the Pacific. Its name is said to come from "yokote," a word for paths winding sideways across a mountainside, and it is also famed for frost-covered trees.
191.description_en_short: A leading peak of the Shiga Highlands on the Pacific-Japan Sea divide, famed for frost-covered trees.

192.description_ja_long: 中央アルプス北端に横たわるなだらかな山。慈覚大師が霊木に観音像を刻み、経を納めたのが名の由来と伝わる。頂は南アルプスの好展望。
192.description_ja_short: 経を納めた伝承が名に残る、中央アルプス北端の穏峰。
192.description_en_long: Kyogatake is a gentle mountain lying at the northern end of the Central Alps. Its name is said to derive from the priest Jikaku Daishi, who carved a Kannon image from a sacred tree and buried sutras here. The summit offers fine views of the Southern Alps.
192.description_en_short: A gentle peak at the north end of the Central Alps, named for a legend of sutras buried on the mountain.

193.description_ja_long: 志賀高原を代表する標高2,295mの山。かつて岩巣護山と書かれた。一等三角点の頂からは魚野川源流の手つかずの原生林が見事に広がる。
193.description_ja_short: 志賀高原の雄峰。頂から魚野川源流の原生林を見渡す。
193.description_en_long: Iwasugeyama is a 2,295 m mountain representing the Shiga Highlands, once written with characters read Iwasugoyama. From its summit, which holds a first-order triangulation point, spreads a superb view of untouched virgin forest at the headwaters of the Uono River.
193.description_en_short: A proud peak of the Shiga Highlands, overlooking virgin forest at the headwaters of the Uono River.

194.description_ja_long: 北海道の屋根と呼ばれる大山塊で、主峰旭岳は道内の最高峰。旭の名はアイヌ語「日の川」の忠別川に由来し、旭川市の名も同じ流れを汲む。
194.description_ja_short: 主峰旭岳が道内最高峰の大山塊。名はアイヌ語の川に由来。
194.description_en_long: Daisetsuzan is a great mountain mass called the roof of Hokkaido, and its main peak Asahidake is the island's highest point. The name Asahi derives from the Chubetsu River, meaning "river of the sun" in Ainu, the same stream that gave Asahikawa City its name.
194.description_en_short: A vast massif whose main peak Asahidake is Hokkaido's highest; the name comes from an Ainu river name.

195.description_ja_long: 常念山脈の前衛に富士山型の端整な姿でそびえ、有明富士や信濃富士とも呼ばれる。江戸期に修験者が開山し、多くの信者が登拝した霊山。
195.description_ja_short: 信濃富士と呼ばれる端整な峰。修験者が開いた登拝の山。
195.description_en_long: Ariakeyama rises in a trim, Fuji-like form in front of the Jonen range and is also called the Ariake Fuji or Shinano Fuji. A sacred mountain opened by shugendo ascetics in the Edo period, it drew many believers on pilgrimage climbs to its heights.
195.description_en_short: A shapely peak called the Shinano Fuji, a sacred mountain opened by ascetics and climbed by pilgrims.

196.description_ja_long: 旭岳に次ぐ北海道第2の高峰・北鎮岳を指す。黒岳から望む白鳥と千鳥の雪形で知られ、名は旭川に置かれた陸軍の鎮台にちなむという。
196.description_ja_short: 旭岳に次ぐ道内第2の高峰。白鳥と千鳥の雪形で知られる。
196.description_en_long: This peak, Hokuchindake, is Hokkaido's second highest summit after Asahidake. It is known for the swan and plover snow patterns seen from Kurodake, and its name is said to derive from the army garrison once stationed in Asahikawa.
196.description_en_short: Hokkaido's second highest peak after Asahidake, known for its swan and plover snow patterns.

197.description_ja_long: 日本海に裾を引く秀麗な成層火山で出羽富士と呼ばれる。噴火を繰り返す荒ぶる神と畏れられ、頂に大物忌神社を祭る。固有の高山植物も多い。
197.description_ja_short: 出羽富士と呼ばれる東北の名峰。固有の高山植物が咲く。
197.description_en_long: A graceful stratovolcano trailing its skirts to the Sea of Japan, known as Dewa Fuji. Feared as a raging deity for its repeated eruptions, it bears Omonoimi Shrine on its summit and is also home to many endemic alpine plants.
197.description_en_short: A famed peak of Tohoku called Dewa Fuji, where endemic alpine plants bloom.

198.description_ja_long: 表大雪の南端にどっしり座る白雲岳を指す。岩塊が積み重なる頂の周りにはナキウサギの声が飛び交い、対岸に旭岳の雄姿を望む展望台となる。
198.description_ja_short: 表大雪南端の展望峰。岩塊の間にナキウサギの声が響く。
198.description_en_long: This is Hakuundake, sitting solidly at the southern end of the Omote-Daisetsu group. Pika calls fly among the boulders piled around its summit, which serves as a viewpoint commanding the imposing figure of Asahidake across the way.
198.description_en_short: A scenic peak at the south end of Omote-Daisetsu, where pika calls echo among the boulders.

199.description_ja_long: 奥秩父の西端に花崗岩の岩峰が林立する異観の山。明治の県知事が瑞牆の字を当てたと伝わり、初夏はアズマシャクナゲの群落が岩を彩る。
199.description_ja_short: 花崗岩の岩峰が林立する奥秩父の異峰。シャクナゲの名所。
199.description_en_long: A strikingly unusual mountain at the western edge of Okuchichibu, bristling with granite rock spires. The name Mizugaki is said to have been chosen by a Meiji-era governor, and in early summer colonies of Azuma rhododendrons color the rocks.
199.description_en_short: An unusual Okuchichibu peak bristling with granite spires, famed for its rhododendrons.

200.description_ja_long: 浅間山の西に東西二峰を並べる上信国境の山。一等三角点の東峰は360度の展望を誇り、山腹には全国でも貴重な天然カラマツ林が茂る。
200.description_ja_short: 東西二峰が並ぶ上信国境の山。頂は360度の展望を誇る。
200.description_en_long: A mountain on the Joshin border west of Asamayama, with twin peaks aligned east and west. The eastern peak, holding a first-order triangulation point, commands a 360-degree panorama, and rare natural larch forest grows on its slopes.
200.description_en_short: Twin east-west peaks on the Joshin border; the summit boasts a 360-degree panorama.

201.description_ja_long: 尾瀬ヶ原の西端に燧ヶ岳と向かい合うなだらかな名峰。山名は仏教でなく渋ッ沢に由来するといい、蛇紋岩の斜面に希少な高山植物が咲く。
201.description_ja_short: 尾瀬西端の花の名山。蛇紋岩の斜面に希少な花が咲く。
201.description_en_long: A gentle, celebrated peak facing Hiuchigadake across the western end of Ozegahara. Its name is said to derive not from Buddhism but from the Shibussawa stream, and rare alpine flowers bloom on its serpentinite slopes.
201.description_en_short: A famed flower mountain at Oze's western edge, where rare blooms dot serpentinite slopes.

202.description_ja_long: 菅平高原の東にそびえる標高2,207mの峰。花の百名山に数えられ、夏から秋の稜線を彩る花々、とりわけ白く可憐なウメバチソウで名高い。
202.description_ja_short: 菅平高原に立つ花の百名山。可憐なウメバチソウで名高い。
202.description_en_long: A 2,207 m peak rising east of the Sugadaira highlands. Counted among the 100 Famous Flower Mountains, it is renowned for the blossoms that adorn its ridgeline from summer to autumn, above all the dainty white grass-of-Parnassus.
202.description_en_short: A flower mountain above the Sugadaira highlands, famed for dainty grass-of-Parnassus.

203.description_ja_long: 苗場山と白砂山を結ぶ信越国境稜線の最高峰。里から見えぬ奥深さゆえ長く登路がなく、残雪期の縦走でしか近づけない秘峰として知られた。
203.description_ja_short: 信越国境稜線の最高峰。長く登路のなかった奥深い秘峰。
203.description_en_long: The highest point on the Shin'etsu border ridge linking Naebasan and Shirasunayama. So deep that it cannot be seen from any village, it long had no trail and was known as a hidden peak approachable only by traverses on lingering snow.
203.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Shin'etsu border ridge, a remote hidden summit long without a trail.

204.description_ja_long: 中央アルプス最南端に構える美濃の最高峰。船を伏せた山容から船伏山とも呼ばれ、天照大神の胞衣を埋めたという伝説が山名の由来とされる。
204.description_ja_short: 船を伏せたような姿の美濃最高峰。胞衣伝説が名に残る。
204.description_en_long: The highest peak of Mino, standing at the southernmost end of the Central Alps. Its upturned-boat form earned it the alternate name Funafuseyama, and its name is said to come from a legend that Amaterasu's placenta was buried here.
204.description_en_short: Mino's highest peak, shaped like an upturned boat; a placenta legend lives in its name.

205.description_ja_long: 南アルプス深南部の只中、黒法師岳から続く尾根に形よくそびえる。樹林のない頂は360度の大展望で、傍らに鹿ノ平の草原が広がっている。
205.description_ja_short: 深南部の只中に立つ好展望の峰。鹿ノ平の草原を従える。
205.description_en_long: Rising handsomely on the ridge running from Kurobushidake, deep in the southernmost reaches of the Southern Alps. Its treeless summit offers a sweeping 360-degree panorama, with the grassland of Shikanodaira spreading out nearby.
205.description_en_short: A fine viewpoint deep in the southern Southern Alps, attended by the Shikanodaira grassland.

206.description_ja_long: 群馬県草津町と嬬恋村にまたがる標高2,171mの活火山で、草津白根山の最高峰。旧火口の鏡池では秋に池底の石が描く亀甲模様が現れる珍しい景観で知られる。
206.description_ja_short: 草津白根山の最高峰。鏡池の亀甲模様とコマクサで知られる。
206.description_en_long: A 2,171 m active volcano straddling Kusatsu and Tsumagoi in Gunma, the highest peak of Kusatsu-Shiranesan. At the old crater pond Kagamiike, stones on the bottom trace a rare tortoiseshell pattern that emerges in autumn.
206.description_en_short: The highest peak of Kusatsu-Shirane, known for Kagamiike's tortoiseshell pattern and komakusa.

208.description_ja_long: 北アルプス南部、笠ヶ岳の南に立つ標高2,168mの岩峰。幾重にも重なる岩壁が僧の錫杖に似ることが名の由来とされ、クライマーを惹きつけてきた。
208.description_ja_short: 屏風のような岩壁が連なる奥飛騨の岩峰。名は錫杖に由来。
208.description_en_long: A 2,168 m rock peak standing south of Kasagatake in the southern Northern Alps. Its tiered walls of rock, said to resemble a monk's shakujo staff, gave the mountain its name and have long drawn climbers to their heights.
208.description_en_short: An Okuhida rock peak of screen-like tiered walls, named for a monk's shakujo staff.

210.description_ja_long: 群馬県草津町にそびえる活火山で、草津白根山と呼ばれる。山頂の火口湖・湯釜はエメラルド色の水をたたえ、その酸性度は世界一とされることで名高い。
210.description_ja_short: 山頂にエメラルド色の湯釜を抱く活火山。名湯草津の母胎。
210.description_en_long: An active volcano rising in Kusatsu, Gunma, known as Kusatsu-Shiranesan. Yugama, the crater lake at its summit, holds emerald-colored water famed for an acidity said to be the highest of any lake in the world.
210.description_en_short: An active volcano cradling the emerald crater lake Yugama; mother of the famed Kusatsu springs.

211.description_ja_long: 群馬県北部、七つの峰を連ねる古い火山。山名は日本武尊の東征の故事に伝わり、古来信仰を集めた。深い谷は武尊九十九谷と呼ばれ、初夏は花々に彩られる。
211.description_ja_short: 日本武尊の故事に名を負う上州の名峰。初夏は花に彩られる。
211.description_en_long: An old volcano in northern Gunma linking seven peaks. Its name is tied to the legend of Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign, and it has drawn worship since antiquity. Its deep valleys, the ninety-nine valleys of Hotaka, fill with flowers in early summer.
211.description_en_short: A famed Joshu peak bearing Yamato Takeru's name, colored with flowers in early summer.

212.description_ja_long: 新潟・長野県境に立つ標高2,145mの火山。山頂には池塘を散りばめた広大な高層湿原が広がり、それを天然の苗田に見立てたことが名の由来と伝わる。
212.description_ja_short: 山頂に池塘散る広大な湿原。苗田に見立てた名を持つ。
212.description_en_long: A 2,145 m volcano on the Niigata-Nagano border. Its summit spreads into a vast high moor scattered with small ponds, and the name is said to come from likening this moorland to natural paddies of rice seedlings.
212.description_en_short: A vast pond-dotted moor crowns its summit, named for its likeness to seedling paddies.

213.description_ja_long: 栃木・群馬県境、足尾の山深くに立つ標高2,144mの古い火山。笄山が皇開山と宛字され、スカイと読まれたという山名の変遷を持つ。修験の道が通った秘峰。
213.description_ja_short: 笄山が転じたという奇妙な山名を持つ足尾山地の秘峰。
213.description_en_long: A 2,144 m old volcano deep in the Ashio mountains on the Tochigi-Gunma border. Its name changed curiously over time: Kogaiyama came to be written with characters read as Sukai. A hidden peak once threaded by paths of mountain ascetics.
213.description_en_short: A hidden peak of the Ashio mountains with a curious name said to derive from Kogaiyama.

214.description_ja_long: 大雪山国立公園の中央に構える標高2,141mの山。神々の遊ぶ庭カムイミンタラと崇められ、山上には庭園のような沼とお花畑、岩の造形が広がる。
214.description_ja_short: 沼とお花畑が広がる「神々の遊ぶ庭」。大雪の奥座敷。
214.description_en_long: A 2,141 m mountain at the heart of Daisetsuzan National Park. Revered as Kamuy Mintar, the playground of the gods, its heights spread with garden-like ponds, fields of alpine flowers, and sculpted formations of rock.
214.description_en_short: The playground of the gods, spread with ponds and flower fields; Daisetsuzan's inner sanctum.

215.description_ja_long: 群馬・新潟県境に穏やかな平頂を広げる標高2,141mの山。頂は池塘と花の高層湿原で、昭和に登山道が開かれるまで幻の山と呼ばれた秘峰。
215.description_ja_short: 名の通り平らな山頂に高層湿原を抱く、かつての幻の山。
215.description_en_long: A 2,141 m mountain spreading a gentle flat summit on the Gunma-Niigata border. Its top is a high moor of ponds and flowers, and until a trail was opened in the Showa era it was a hidden peak called the phantom mountain.
215.description_en_short: True to its name, a flat summit holding a high moor; once called the phantom mountain.

216.description_ja_long: 栃木・群馬県境、奥鬼怒の山懐にある標高2,141mの山。山頂近くには日本一の高所とされる高層湿原・鬼怒沼が広がり、大小の池塘に高山植物が咲く。
216.description_ja_short: 日本最高所級の高層湿原・鬼怒沼を頂に抱く奥鬼怒の山。
216.description_en_long: A 2,141 m mountain in the Okukinu recesses on the Tochigi-Gunma border. Near its summit spreads Kinunuma, a high moor said to be the loftiest in Japan, where alpine plants bloom around its ponds large and small.
216.description_en_short: An Okukinu mountain holding Kinunuma, one of Japan's highest moors, near its summit.

217.description_ja_long: 群馬・長野・新潟の三県境にそびえる標高2,140mの山。群馬側へ下る白砂川の源にちなむ名で、静かな頂は山また山の奥深い展望に恵まれる。
217.description_ja_short: 三県境の静峰。白砂川の源にちなみ、展望は山また山。
217.description_en_long: A 2,140 m mountain rising where Gunma, Nagano, and Niigata meet. Named for the Shirasuna River that descends the Gunma side, its quiet summit is blessed with deep views of mountains upon mountains stretching away.
217.description_en_short: A quiet peak on a three-prefecture border, named for the Shirasuna River; views of endless mountains.

218.description_ja_long: 福島県檜枝岐村にそびえる標高2,133mの山で、会津駒の名で親しまれる。山腹に黒い駒形が現れるという名の由来を持ち、頂稜の湿原には花々が咲き競う。
218.description_ja_short: 山腹に黒駒が現れると伝わる、湿原と花の会津の名峰。
218.description_en_long: A 2,133 m mountain rising in Hinoemata, Fukushima, affectionately known as Aizu-Koma. Its name comes from a black horse figure said to appear on its flanks, and flowers vie in bloom across the moorland of its summit ridge.
218.description_en_short: A famed Aizu peak of moors and flowers, where a black horse is said to appear on its flanks.

219.description_ja_long: 飯豊連峰の最高峰で標高2,128m。盛夏も残雪を光らせる巨体は連峰の奥ノ院として崇められ、直下の文平ノ池は山影を映すお花畑の静寂境と讃えられる。
219.description_ja_short: 盛夏も残雪を輝かせる飯豊連峰の最高峰。信仰の奥ノ院。
219.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Iide range at 2,128 m. Its massive body, glinting with snow even in midsummer, is revered as the range's innermost sanctuary, and Bunpeinoike just below, a flower-fringed pool mirroring the peak, is praised for its stillness.
219.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Iide range, gleaming with snow even in midsummer; a sanctuary of faith.

220.description_ja_long: 乗鞍岳と御嶽の中間、長野・岐阜県境に立つ標高2,121mの山。お釜を思わせる山容が名の由来と伝わり、北隣には女工哀史の舞台・野麦峠が横たわる。
220.description_ja_short: お釜に似た山容が名の由来。野麦峠を隣に置く静かな山。
220.description_en_long: A 2,121 m mountain on the Nagano-Gifu border midway between Norikuradake and Ontake. Its form, said to resemble a cooking pot, gave the mountain its name, and just to the north lies Nomugi Pass, stage of the tragic tale of the mill girls.
220.description_en_short: Named for its pot-like form; a quiet mountain with Nomugi Pass lying to its north.

221.description_ja_long: 大雪山系の北端に岩肌黒々とそびえる標高2,113mの岩峰。椀を伏せたような穏やかな峰が多い山群にあって、ひときわ男性的な鋭い姿を見せる異色の存在。
221.description_ja_short: なだらかな大雪山系にあって異彩を放つ黒々とした鋭鋒。
221.description_en_long: A 2,113 m rock peak towering dark at the northern end of the Daisetsuzan range. Among a mountain group of mostly gentle domes like upturned bowls, it is a singular presence, showing a strikingly sharp and masculine profile.
221.description_en_short: A dark, sharp spire that cuts an unusual figure in the otherwise gentle Daisetsuzan range.

222.description_ja_long: 長野県南佐久、北相木村と南相木村の境に立つ標高2,112mの孤峰。黒木の森の上に岩壁の頂だけを突き出し、大島亮吉や深田久弥が魅力を綴った佐久の名山。
222.description_ja_short: 森の上に岩峰を突き出す佐久の名山。シャクナゲの山。
222.description_en_long: A 2,112 m solitary peak between Kitaaiki and Minamiaiki villages in Nagano's Minamisaku district. Thrusting only its rock-walled crown above dark conifer forest, it is a famed Saku mountain whose charms Ryokichi Oshima and Kyuya Fukada wrote of.
222.description_en_short: A famed Saku peak thrusting a rocky crown above the forest; a mountain of rhododendrons.

223.description_ja_long: 埼玉・山梨県境、奥秩父主脈に静かに連なる標高2,109mの山。多摩川の集水域では最高峰にあたり、シャクナゲに覆われた頂は初夏に花の彩りを見せる。
223.description_ja_short: 多摩川の水源域で最も高い、シャクナゲ咲く静かな峰。
223.description_en_long: A quiet 2,109 m peak strung along the main Okuchichibu ridge on the Saitama-Yamanashi border. It is the highest point in the Tama River watershed, and its rhododendron-covered summit shows the colors of the flowers in early summer.
223.description_en_short: The highest peak in the Tama River watershed, a quiet summit where rhododendrons bloom.

224.description_ja_long: 飯豊連峰の主峰で標高2,105m。頂へ続く尾根だけが福島県として細長く延びる特異な県境を持ち、白装束の若者が登った飯豊詣の歴史が息づく信仰の山。
224.description_ja_short: 飯豊詣の歴史を刻む連峰の盟主。県境が細長く走る山。
224.description_en_long: Mt. Iide, 2,105 m, is the principal peak of the Iide Range. A curiously narrow strip of Fukushima Prefecture runs along the ridge to its summit, and it remains a mountain of faith, once climbed by white-clad youths on the Iide pilgrimage.
224.description_en_short: Chief peak of the Iide Range, steeped in pilgrimage history, with a strangely narrow prefectural border running to its top.

225.description_ja_long: 長野・群馬県境に牛が寝たような姿を横たえる溶岩円頂丘。初夏、山腹を染めるレンゲツツジの大群落は天然記念物で、山名は麓に湧く鹿沢温泉に由来する。
225.description_ja_short: レンゲツツジが山腹を染める、なだらかな高原の名峰。
225.description_en_long: A lava dome lying like a resting ox on the Nagano-Gunma border. In early summer a vast colony of Japanese azaleas, designated a natural monument, dyes its slopes, and the name comes from Kazawa Onsen, the hot spring at its foot.
225.description_en_short: A gentle highland peak whose slopes are dyed with Japanese azaleas in early summer.

227.description_ja_long: 富山市南東部、立山連峰の前衛にそびえる標高2,090mの独立峰。富山城主佐々成政が黄金を埋めたという伝説が残り、今も探索に訪れる人がいるという。
227.description_ja_short: 佐々成政の黄金伝説を秘める、立山連峰前衛の三角峰。
227.description_en_long: A 2,090 m free-standing peak rising in southeastern Toyama City before the Tateyama Range. Legend holds that Sassa Narimasa, lord of Toyama Castle, buried gold here, and treasure seekers are said to visit even today.
227.description_en_short: A triangular outlier of the Tateyama Range, said to hide the buried gold of Sassa Narimasa.

228.description_ja_long: 越後三山の最高峰で標高2,085m。豪雪に磨かれた山は盛夏も雪渓を残し、険谷と絶壁に囲まれて容易に人を寄せ付けない。静けさに満ちた魚沼の奥山。
228.description_ja_short: 豪雪が磨く越後三山の最高峰。盛夏も雪渓を残す奥山。
228.description_en_long: The highest of the Echigo Sanzan three peaks at 2,085 m. Polished by heavy snows, it keeps snow patches even in midsummer, and steep gorges and sheer cliffs hold visitors at bay - a deep, silent mountain in the Uonuma backcountry.
228.description_en_short: The highest of the Echigo Sanzan, sculpted by heavy snow, keeping snow patches even in midsummer.

229.description_ja_long: 大雪山国立公園の南西部、十勝連峰の主峰をなす標高2,077mの活火山。今も噴煙を上げ、火山礫に覆われた荒涼たる山頂からは北の大地の大観が広がる。
229.description_ja_short: 今も噴煙を上げる十勝連峰の主峰。荒涼たる火山の頂。
229.description_en_long: Mt. Tokachi is a 2,077 m active volcano, principal peak of the Tokachi Range in the southwest of Daisetsuzan National Park. Still venting smoke, its desolate summit of volcanic gravel commands a sweeping panorama of northern Hokkaido.
229.description_en_short: Chief peak of the Tokachi Range, an active volcano still venting smoke from its desolate summit.

230.description_ja_long: 埼玉・山梨県境に立つ標高2,077mの山で、飛龍山とも呼ばれる。雲取山を凌ぐ高さと堂々たる山容を持ちながら訪れる人は少なく、深い静けさを保つ。
230.description_ja_short: 雲取山を凌ぐ高さを持ちながら静けさを保つ奥秩父の山。
230.description_en_long: A 2,077 m mountain on the Saitama-Yamanashi border, also known as Hiryuzan. Though it exceeds Kumotoriyama in height and shows an imposing form, it sees few visitors and preserves a deep quiet.
230.description_en_short: An Okuchichibu peak taller than Kumotoriyama, yet quiet and little visited.

231.description_ja_long: 志賀高原の南、長野県山ノ内町と高山村の境に立つ標高2,076mの溶岩円頂丘。ダルマのように頭をもたげた姿は高原のシンボルで、巌頭の展望が広い。
231.description_ja_short: ダルマのように頭を持ち上げた志賀高原のシンボル峰。
231.description_en_long: A 2,076 m lava dome standing south of the Shiga Kogen highlands on the border of Yamanouchi Town and Takayama Village, Nagano. Raising its head like a daruma doll, it is a symbol of the highland, with wide views from its rocky summit.
231.description_en_short: A symbol of Shiga Kogen, lifting its head like a daruma doll, with wide views from the rocky top.

232.description_ja_long: 南アルプス深南部、黒木に覆われた三角形の山で標高2,068m。山頂の一等三角点には全国唯一という「×」印が刻まれ、謎めいた存在として注目される。
232.description_ja_short: 「×」印の一等三角点が謎を呼ぶ、深南部の黒い秀峰。
232.description_en_long: A 2,068 m triangular peak cloaked in dark conifers in the deep southern reaches of the Minami Alps. The first-order triangulation point on its summit bears an 'x' mark said to be the only one in Japan, drawing attention as a mystery.
232.description_en_short: A dark peak of the deep southern Alps, its first-order triangulation point bearing a puzzling 'x' mark.

233.description_ja_long: 岐阜県高山市、平湯峠を隔てて乗鞍岳と向き合う標高2,063mの山。南東面に太陽がよく当たることが名の由来ともいわれ、頂から槍・穂高連峰を望む。
233.description_ja_short: 陽に照る山肌が名の由来とも。乗鞍岳と対峙する静かな山。
233.description_en_long: A 2,063 m mountain in Takayama, Gifu, facing Norikuradake across Hirayu Pass. The name is said to come from the sunlight that falls full on its southeastern face, and from the summit one looks out to the Yari and Hotaka ranges.
233.description_en_short: A quiet mountain facing Norikuradake, said to be named for its sunlit slopes.

234.description_ja_long: 福島・栃木県境に連なる帝釈山脈の主峰。山深く「幻の名山」と呼ばれた頂の周辺は、初夏に日本固有種オサバグサが白く群れ咲く。標高2,060m。
234.description_ja_short: 帝釈山脈の主峰。かつて幻の名山と呼ばれ、オサバグサが群れ咲く。
234.description_en_long: The principal peak of the Taishaku Mountains along the Fukushima-Tochigi border, 2,060 m. So remote it was once called a 'phantom famous mountain,' its summit area turns white in early summer with Osabagusa, a flower endemic to Japan.
234.description_en_short: Chief peak of the Taishaku Mountains, once called a phantom mountain, where endemic Osabagusa bloom.

235.description_ja_long: 白山の東、岐阜県白川村に立つ標高2,059mの山。天正の白山大地震で山頂部が三方へ大崩壊したことが名の由来で、天狗の爪跡という伝説も残る。
235.description_ja_short: 三方に崩壊地を開く白山東方の山。天狗の爪跡の伝説も残る。
235.description_en_long: A 2,059 m mountain in Shirakawa Village, Gifu, east of Hakusan. Its name records the great Hakusan earthquake of the Tensho era, when the summit collapsed on three sides, and legend calls the scars the claw marks of a tengu.
235.description_en_short: A mountain east of Hakusan with collapse scars on three sides, said in legend to be a tengu's claw marks.

236.description_ja_long: 山梨県東部、大菩薩連嶺の最高峰で標高2,057m。名は軍神に導かれた新羅三郎義光の故事に由来すると伝わり、富士を望む草原の稜線が続く。
236.description_ja_short: 中里介山の小説で名高い大菩薩連嶺の最高峰。富士を望む草原の稜線。
236.description_en_long: The highest point of the Daibosatsu Range in eastern Yamanashi at 2,057 m. The name is said to derive from the tale of Shinra Saburo Yoshimitsu, who was guided by the god of war, and grassy ridgelines run on with views of Mt. Fuji.
236.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Daibosatsu Range, famed through Nakazato Kaizan's novel, with grassy ridges facing Mt. Fuji.

237.description_ja_long: 白山主峰・御前峰の西に連なる標高2,053mの峰。鎧状の山肌から鎧岳と呼ばれた古名を持ち、中腹には白山系随一と謳われたブナ林が野鳥を育む。
237.description_ja_short: 御前峰の西に連なる峰。古名は鎧岳。ブナ林が野鳥を育む。
237.description_en_long: A 2,053 m peak trailing west of Gozengamine, the main summit of Hakusan. Its armor-like rock face earned it the old name Yoroidake, and the beech forest on its middle slopes, praised as the finest in the Hakusan area, shelters wild birds.
237.description_en_short: A peak west of Gozengamine, once called Yoroidake, whose beech forest nurtures wild birds.

238.description_ja_long: 野尻湖の西にどっしり構える火山で、信濃富士とも呼ばれる。大蛇に懸想された美しい黒姫が山頂の池に身を投げたという伝説を秘める。標高2,053m。
238.description_ja_short: 信濃富士と呼ばれる火山。黒姫の悲恋伝説を山頂の池に秘める。
238.description_en_long: A volcano standing massively west of Lake Nojiri, also called the Shinano Fuji. It holds the legend of the beautiful Princess Kurohime, who, courted by a giant serpent, threw herself into the pond on its summit. 2,053 m high.
238.description_en_short: A volcano called the Shinano Fuji, holding Princess Kurohime's tragic legend in its summit pond.

239.description_ja_long: 日高山脈の最高峰で標高2,052m。名はアイヌ語で「大きい山」を意味し、氷河期のカールを悠々と抱く山頂部の周りにお花畑が広がる。
239.description_ja_short: アイヌ語で「大きい山」。カールを抱く日高山脈の最高峰。
239.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Hidaka Mountains at 2,052 m. The name means 'great mountain' in the Ainu language, and fields of alpine flowers spread around a summit that calmly cradles glacial cirques carved in the ice age.
239.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Hidaka Mountains, 'great mountain' in Ainu, cradling glacial cirques.

240.description_ja_long: 甲府盆地の西に横たわる標高2,052mの山。櫛の形に見える姿が名の由来で、山頂一帯のアヤメ大群落と、雨乞いの高僧を伝える滝の物語で知られる。
240.description_ja_short: 櫛の形の姿が名の由来。山頂一帯にアヤメが大群落をつくる。
240.description_en_long: A 2,052 m mountain lying west of the Kofu Basin, named for its profile resembling a comb. It is known for the great colony of irises spreading across its summit area and for a waterfall tale of a high priest who prayed for rain.
240.description_en_short: A mountain named for its comb-shaped profile, where irises form a great colony near the summit.

241.description_ja_long: 十勝岳連峰の一峰で標高2,052m。名は「油ぎった川」を指すアイヌ語に由来し、南西面に荒々しい火口を開く。山頂近くにはチングルマが咲く。
241.description_ja_short: 南西面に火口を開く十勝岳連峰の一峰。名はアイヌ語に由来。
241.description_en_long: A 2,052 m peak of the Tokachi volcanic range. Its name derives from an Ainu word meaning an 'oily river,' and a rugged crater opens on its southwestern face, while Aleutian avens bloom near the summit.
241.description_en_short: A Tokachi Range peak with a crater open on its southwest face, its name drawn from an Ainu word.

242.description_ja_long: 岩手県の最高峰となる複式火山で標高2,038m。富士に似た姿から南部片富士と呼ばれ、石川啄木が「ふるさとの山」と詠んだ郷土の象徴でもある。
242.description_ja_short: 南部片富士と呼ばれる岩手県最高峰。啄木が詠んだ郷土の象徴。
242.description_en_long: Mt. Iwate, a 2,038 m compound volcano, is the highest peak in Iwate Prefecture. Its Fuji-like form earned it the name Nanbu Katafuji, and it stands as a symbol of home country that the poet Takuboku Ishikawa celebrated in verse.
242.description_en_short: Iwate's highest peak, called Nanbu Katafuji, a homeland symbol sung by the poet Takuboku.

243.description_ja_long: 信州の秘境・秋山郷にそびえる標高2,038mの火山。鷲が翼を広げたような山容で、東面には「第二の谷川岳」と称された険しい絶壁が切れ落ちる。
243.description_ja_short: 秋山郷にそびえる火山。東面は第二の谷川岳と称された絶壁。
243.description_en_long: A 2,038 m volcano towering over Akiyamago, a secluded corner of Shinshu. Its form suggests an eagle with wings outspread, and down its east face plunges a sheer wall of cliffs once styled 'a second Tanigawadake.'
243.description_en_short: A volcano above Akiyamago whose eastern cliffs were once styled a second Tanigawadake.

245.description_ja_long: 奥秩父主脈から外れて静けさを保つ標高2,036mの秘峰で、秩父側では和名倉山と呼ぶ。かつてコメツガの原生林の美しさが讃えられた深い山。
245.description_ja_short: 和名倉山とも呼ぶ奥秩父の秘峰。原生林の美しさで知られた。
245.description_en_long: A secluded 2,036 m peak standing apart from the main Okuchichibu ridge and keeping its quiet; on the Chichibu side it is called Wanakurayama. A deep mountain once praised for the beauty of its virgin forest of northern Japanese hemlock.
245.description_en_short: A quiet Okuchichibu peak also called Wanakurayama, once praised for its beautiful virgin forest.

246.description_ja_long: 山形・福島県境に横たわる吾妻連峰の最高峰で標高2,035m。なだらかな山上に高層湿原が点在し、夏はチングルマやワタスゲが大群落をつくる。
246.description_ja_short: 吾妻連峰の最高峰。山上の湿原に夏の花が大群落をつくる。
246.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Azuma Range lying along the Yamagata-Fukushima border, 2,035 m. High moors dot its gently rounded summit area, where Aleutian avens and cottongrass form great colonies of bloom in summer.
246.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Azuma Range, its summit moors filled with great colonies of summer flowers.

247.description_ja_long: 長野県中部に広がる標高2,034mの溶岩台地。比類ない北アルプスの展望台と讃えられ、詩人尾崎喜八は「世界の天井が抜けた」と感嘆を残した。
247.description_ja_short: 北アルプスの大展望が広がる溶岩台地。詩人も感嘆した高原。
247.description_en_long: A lava plateau spreading across central Nagano at 2,034 m. Celebrated as an unrivaled viewing platform for the Northern Alps, it moved the poet Kihachi Ozaki to marvel that 'the ceiling of the world has come off.'
247.description_en_short: A lava plateau with a grand panorama of the Northern Alps, a highland that moved a poet to awe.

248.description_ja_long: 奥秩父前衛に立つ標高2,031mの岩峰。恵林寺を開いた夢窓国師が座禅修行した山と伝わり、寺の山号にもなった。巨岩の頂は一点360度の展望。
248.description_ja_short: 夢窓国師が修行したと伝わる岩峰。頂は一点360度の展望。
248.description_en_long: A 2,031 m rocky peak in the front ranges of Okuchichibu. Muso Kokushi, founder of Erin-ji temple, is said to have practiced Zen meditation here, and the mountain became the temple's honorific name. Its crag summit commands a full 360-degree view.
248.description_en_short: A rocky peak where Muso Kokushi is said to have trained, with a full 360-degree summit view.

249.description_ja_long: 群馬・新潟県境に連なる谷川連峰の最高峰で標高2,026m。平標山へ続くたおやかな稜線に見渡す限りのお花畑と笹原が広がり、楽園の景をなす。
249.description_ja_short: 谷川連峰の最高峰。平標山へ続く稜線にお花畑が広がる。
249.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Tanigawa Range along the Gunma-Niigata border, 2,026 m. On the gentle ridgeline leading toward Tairappyoyama, flower fields and seas of bamboo grass spread as far as the eye can see, a paradise-like scene.
249.description_en_short: The Tanigawa Range's highest peak, with flower fields along the gentle ridge to Tairappyoyama.

250.description_ja_long: 新潟・山形県境、飯豊連峰主稜の中央に座す標高2,025mの峰。名はマタギが呼んだ「加治川の北股」にちなみ、鞍部の近くでクロユリが見つかった。
250.description_ja_short: 飯豊連峰主稜の中央に座す峰。名はマタギの呼び名にちなむ。
250.description_en_long: A 2,025 m peak sitting at the center of the main ridge of the Iide Range on the Niigata-Yamagata border. Its name comes from 'Kita-mata of the Kajikawa River,' as matagi hunters called it, and kuroyuri lilies were found near the saddle.
250.description_en_short: A peak at the center of the Iide Range's main ridge, named after what matagi hunters called it.

251.description_ja_long: 奥秩父の東端にそびえる東京都の最高峰で標高2,017m。三都県の境に位置し、山頂からは山並みのはるか彼方に富士山を望む大展望が開ける。
251.description_ja_short: 東京都の最高峰。山頂から富士を望む奥秩父東端の名峰。
251.description_en_long: Rising at the eastern end of Okuchichibu, this 2,017 m mountain is the highest peak in Tokyo. Standing on the border of three prefectures, its summit opens a sweeping view with Mt. Fuji far beyond the ranks of mountains.
251.description_en_short: Tokyo's highest peak at the eastern end of Okuchichibu, with a summit view of Mt. Fuji.

252.description_ja_long: 大菩薩嶺の南へ続く小金沢連嶺の主峰で標高2,014m。名は源流の沢に由来し、富士の眺めに優れる秀麗富嶽十二景の一つに数えられている。
252.description_ja_short: 小金沢連嶺の主峰。富士を望む秀麗富嶽十二景の一座。
252.description_en_long: The 2,014 m principal peak of the Koganezawa ridge running south from Daibosatsurei. Named after a stream at its headwaters, it is counted among the Twelve Superb Views of Mt. Fuji for its fine prospect of the mountain.
252.description_en_short: Principal peak of the Koganezawa ridge, counted among the Twelve Superb Views of Mt. Fuji.

253.description_ja_long: 十勝連峰の最北に立つ標高2,013mの鋭峰。名は「槍がそりかえっている山」などの意とされ、雌阿寒岳と槍を投げ合ったという伝説を伝える。
253.description_ja_short: 十勝連峰最北の鋭峰。名は槍にまつわるアイヌ語に由来する。
253.description_en_long: A sharp 2,013 m peak standing at the northern end of the Tokachi Range. Its Ainu name is said to mean 'mountain where a spear bends back,' and legend tells of it hurling spears back and forth with Meakandake.
253.description_en_short: A sharp peak at the northern end of the Tokachi Range, its Ainu name tied to a spear legend.

254.description_ja_long: 東大雪に孤高を誇る標高2,013mの鋭峰。名はシナノキにちなむアイヌ語の川名に由来し、山上の岩場にはエゾナキウサギが声を響かせる。
254.description_ja_short: 東大雪に孤高を誇る鋭峰。岩場にエゾナキウサギが棲む。
254.description_en_long: A sharp 2,013 m peak standing in proud isolation in the East Daisetsu area. Its name derives from an Ainu river name referring to the linden tree, and northern pikas call out among the rocks near its summit.
254.description_en_short: A proudly isolated sharp peak of East Daisetsu, where northern pikas live among the rocks.

255.description_ja_long: 安倍川流域の最高峰で標高2,013m。ササに覆われた明るくなだらかな山頂はヤナギランの群生地で、南アルプス南部の峰々や富士を一望する。
255.description_ja_short: 安倍川流域の最高峰。ヤナギラン咲く頂から富士を望む。
255.description_en_long: The highest peak in the Abe River basin at 2,013 m. Its bright, gentle summit covered in bamboo grass is a colony of fireweed, commanding a panorama of the southern peaks of the Southern Alps and Mt. Fuji.
255.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Abe River basin, its fireweed-covered summit looking out to Mt. Fuji.

256.description_ja_long: 尾瀬ヶ原の北に立つ標高2,004mの山。密生するササを「這いずる」ように登ったことが名になったと伝わり、今は原から静かに仰がれる。
256.description_ja_short: 尾瀬ヶ原の北の山。名は「這いずる」の訛りと伝わる。
256.description_en_long: A 2,004 m mountain standing north of the Ozegahara marshland. Its name is said to come from climbers having to 'crawl' through the dense bamboo grass, and today it is quietly admired from the marsh below.
256.description_en_short: A mountain north of Ozegahara, its name said to be a corruption of a word meaning 'to crawl.'

257.description_ja_long: 越後三山の一座で標高2,003m。上越線の車窓から天馬が空を翔けるようと讃えられた雄姿で、上部の尾根は高山植物と大きな展望に恵まれる。
257.description_ja_short: 天馬が空を翔けるようと讃えられた雄姿。越後三山の一座。
257.description_en_long: One of the Three Mountains of Echigo at 2,003 m. Its noble figure, praised from the windows of the Joetsu Line as a heavenly horse galloping across the sky, carries upper ridges blessed with alpine flowers and great views.
257.description_en_short: One of the Three Mountains of Echigo, praised as a heavenly horse galloping across the sky.

258.description_ja_long: 身延山地の霊山で標高1,989m。家康の側室お万の方が滝に打たれ女人禁制を解いたと伝わり、彼岸の中日には富士山頂からの日の出を拝める。
258.description_ja_short: 身延山地の霊山。彼岸には富士山頂からの日の出を拝む。
258.description_en_long: A sacred 1,989 m mountain of the Minobu range. Oman no Kata, a consort of Ieyasu, is said to have bathed under its waterfall and ended the ban on women, and at the equinox the sunrise can be seen rising from the summit of Mt. Fuji.
258.description_en_short: A sacred peak of the Minobu range; at the equinox the sun rises from the summit of Mt. Fuji.

259.description_ja_long: 層雲峡の奥、大雪山の北東部に位置する黒岳の頂。名は頂まで続く深い緑に由来し、夏の稜線は夢のように美しい花の道となる。標高1,984m。
259.description_ja_short: 大雪山北東部の峰・黒岳。緑深く、夏は花の道が続く。
259.description_en_long: The summit of Kurodake in the northeastern part of the Daisetsuzan massif, beyond Sounkyo Gorge. Its name comes from the deep green reaching to the top, and in summer the ridgeline becomes a dreamlike trail of flowers. It stands 1,984 m.
259.description_en_short: Kurodake, a peak of northeastern Daisetsuzan, deep green with a summer trail of flowers.

260.description_ja_long: 山形県中央にそびえる出羽三山の主峰で標高1,984m。頂に月山神社を戴く古い信仰の山で、芭蕉も白衣の道者に交じって登った。豊富な残雪が高山の花園を育む。
260.description_ja_short: 出羽三山の最高峰。信仰と残雪と高山植物に彩られる霊山。
260.description_en_long: The 1,984 m principal peak of the Dewa Sanzan, rising in central Yamagata. An old mountain of faith crowned by Gassan Shrine, it was climbed by the poet Basho among white-robed pilgrims, and its abundant lingering snow nurtures alpine flower gardens.
260.description_en_short: Highest of the Dewa Sanzan, a sacred mountain adorned with faith, lingering snow and alpine flowers.

261.description_ja_long: 長野・岐阜県境に横たわる阿寺山地の最高峰で標高1,982m。笹と矮木が点在する高原状の頂は日本庭園の趣で、眼前に御嶽山の雄大な姿を仰ぐ第一級の展望地。
261.description_ja_short: 阿寺山地の最高峰。御嶽山を眼前に仰ぐ日本庭園風の頂。
261.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Atera Mountains lying on the Nagano-Gifu border, at 1,982 m. Its plateau-like summit, dotted with bamboo grass and dwarf trees, has the air of a Japanese garden and offers a first-class view of Ontakesan looming close at hand.
261.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Atera Mountains, a Japanese-garden-like summit facing Ontakesan.

262.description_ja_long: 愛媛県にそびえる西日本最高峰、標高1,982m。役小角が開いたと伝わる日本七霊山の一つで、垂直に立つ柱状節理が天狗岳など峻険な岩峰を連ねる修験の山。
262.description_ja_short: 西日本最高峰の霊山。峻険な岩峰が天を突く修験の道場。
262.description_en_long: Rising in Ehime, at 1,982 m the highest peak in western Japan. One of Japan's Seven Sacred Mountains, said to have been opened by En no Ozunu, it is a mountain of Shugendo asceticism where vertical columnar joints form precipitous rock peaks such as Tengudake.
262.description_en_short: Western Japan's highest peak, a sacred Shugendo mountain of precipitous rock spires.

263.description_ja_long: 山形・福島県境に横たわる吾妻連峰の西部に座る峰。山頂に立てば磐梯山と裏磐梯に散らばる湖沼群が眼前に広がり、連峰でも指折りの展望をほしいままにする。
263.description_ja_short: 吾妻連峰西部の峰。磐梯山と裏磐梯の湖沼群を一望する頂。
263.description_en_long: A peak in the western part of the Azuma Range lying on the Yamagata-Fukushima border. From the summit, Bandaisan and the lakes and ponds scattered across Urabandai spread out before the eyes, giving one of the finest views in the range.
263.description_en_short: A peak of the western Azuma Range, overlooking Bandaisan and the lakes of Urabandai.

264.title_ja: カムイエクウチカウシ山
264.description_ja_long: 日高山脈の中央部、幌尻岳に次ぐ第二の高峰で標高1,979m。名はアイヌ語「熊の転げ落ちる所」に由来し、氷河が刻んだカール群の上に男性的な姿で君臨する。
264.description_ja_short: 日高山脈第二の高峰。カール群の上に君臨する主脈の雄。
264.description_en_long: The second-highest peak of the Hidaka Range after Poroshiridake, at 1,979 m in its central section. Its Ainu name means 'where bears tumble down,' and it reigns with a masculine bearing above a cluster of glacier-carved cirques.
264.description_en_short: Second-highest peak of the Hidaka Range, reigning over glacier-carved cirques.

265.description_ja_long: 群馬・新潟県境に連なる谷川連峰のほぼ中央に座る頂。県境稜線の山々や馬蹄形の山並みを見渡す展望に恵まれながら訪れる人は少なく、静かな山旅が味わえる。
265.description_ja_short: 谷川連峰中央の静かな頂。県境の稜線を見渡す展望に富む。
265.description_en_long: A summit sitting near the center of the Tanigawa Range along the Gunma-Niigata border. Blessed with views over the border-ridge peaks and the horseshoe-shaped skyline, it sees few visitors and offers a quiet mountain journey.
265.description_en_short: A quiet summit at the center of the Tanigawa Range, with wide views of the border ridgeline.

266.description_ja_long: 群馬・新潟県境に猫の耳のような双耳峰を掲げる上越の名峰。二つの頂はトマの耳、オキの耳と呼ばれ、元は隣の俎嵓の名が測量の誤記で定着したものという。
266.description_ja_short: 猫の耳のような双耳峰を掲げて立つ、上越国境の名峰。
266.description_en_long: A famous peak of the Joetsu border raising twin summits like a cat's ears on the Gunma-Niigata line. The two tops are called Toma no Mimi and Oki no Mimi, and the name is said to have settled through a surveying error, originally that of neighboring Manaitagura.
266.description_en_short: A famous peak on the Joetsu border, raising twin summits like a cat's ears.

267.description_ja_long: 福島県、吾妻連峰の一座をなすなだらかな火山。オオシラビソやコメツガの深い樹林と湿原の花に彩られ、ハイマツ帯の頂からは連峰の峰々をぐるりと見渡す。
267.description_ja_short: 樹林と湿原に包まれたおおらかな峰。吾妻連峰の一座。
267.description_en_long: A gentle volcano forming one peak of the Azuma Range in Fukushima. Graced by deep forests of Maries' fir and northern Japanese hemlock and by marshland flowers, its summit in the creeping-pine zone looks around at the peaks of the whole range.
267.description_en_short: A gentle peak of the Azuma Range, wrapped in forest and marshland.

268.description_ja_long: 上越国境に連なる谷川連峰の一峰。東面の一ノ倉沢は剱岳・穂高岳と並ぶ日本三大岩場に数えられ、その登攀の歩みは日本の近代登山史そのものと語られる。
268.description_ja_short: 一ノ倉沢の大岩壁を東面に擁する峰。近代登山史の舞台。
268.description_en_long: A peak of the Tanigawa Range on the Joetsu border. Ichinokurasawa on its east face ranks with Tsurugidake and Hotakadake among Japan's three great rock walls, and the history of its ascents is said to be the very history of modern Japanese climbing.
268.description_en_short: A peak holding the great rock walls of Ichinokurasawa, a stage of modern climbing history.

269.description_ja_long: 福島・栃木県境の帝釈山脈に位置する標高1,971mの山。平坦な頂に名の由来となった広大な湿原をのせ、木道の先に会津駒ヶ岳や日光の山々が浮かぶ。
269.description_ja_short: 頂に広大な湿原をのせる山。花と展望の木道が延びる。
269.description_en_long: A 1,971 m mountain in the Taishaku Mountains on the Fukushima-Tochigi border. Its flat summit carries the vast marshland that gave the mountain its name, and beyond the boardwalk float Aizu-Komagatake and the mountains of Nikko.
269.description_en_short: A mountain bearing a vast marshland on its summit, with a boardwalk of flowers and views.

270.description_ja_long: 新潟県魚沼、銀山平を見下ろす標高1,969mの鋭峰。三角形の頂から両翼を広げた均整の姿で、深田久弥がついに登れなかった山としても語り継がれる。
270.description_ja_short: 銀山平にそびえる均整の鋭峰。深田久弥が登り残した山。
270.description_en_long: A sharp 1,969 m peak looking down on Ginzandaira in Uonuma, Niigata. With a balanced form spreading both wings from its triangular summit, it is also remembered as the mountain that Kyuya Fukada never managed to climb.
270.description_en_short: A finely balanced sharp peak above Ginzandaira, the mountain Kyuya Fukada left unclimbed.

271.description_ja_long: 新潟・群馬県境、三国山脈の標高1,967m峰。頂上一帯で美女が機を織っていたという伝説が名の由来で、山上には復元された高層湿原が広がるおおらかな名山。
271.description_ja_short: 機織りの美女の伝説が名に残る、山上湿原のおおらかな名山。
271.description_en_long: A 1,967 m peak of the Mikuni Mountains on the Niigata-Gunma border. Its name comes from a legend of a beautiful woman weaving on a loom around the summit, and a restored high moor spreads across the top of this gentle, celebrated mountain.
271.description_en_short: A gentle famous mountain with a summit moor, named for the legend of a weaving beauty.

272.description_ja_long: 北海道中央部、東大雪の主峰で標高1,967m。大河石狩川の水源と確かめられてこの名を得た。彫りの深いアルプス的な山容と奥深さで登山者の憧れを集める。
272.description_ja_short: 石狩川の水源をなす東大雪の主峰。彫りの深い山容を誇る。
272.description_en_long: The 1,967 m principal peak of East Daisetsu in central Hokkaido, which gained its name when confirmed as the source of the great Ishikari River. Its deeply chiseled, alpine form and remoteness draw the longing of climbers.
272.description_en_short: Principal peak of East Daisetsu at the source of the Ishikari River, deeply chiseled in form.

273.description_ja_long: 山梨・静岡県境、天子山地の最高峰で標高1,964m。山麓に朝霧高原を従えて堂々とそびえ、頂の稜線からは富士山の剣ヶ峰大沢の崩壊まで間近に望まれる。
273.description_ja_short: 天子山地の最高峰。富士山を間近に仰ぐ大展望の山として名高い。
273.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Tenshi Mountains on the Yamanashi-Shizuoka border, at 1,964 m. Rising grandly with the Asagiri highlands at its foot, its summit ridge gives a close view of Mt. Fuji reaching from Kengamine to the collapsing Osawa gully.
273.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Tenshi Mountains, famed for its grand close-up views of Mt. Fuji.

274.description_ja_long: 北海道・表大雪の南部に横たわる標高1,963mの火山。東はなだらかな高根ガ原に続く一方、西面は忠別川へ切れ落ちる岩壁。トムラウシへの縦走路上の頂。
274.description_ja_short: 表大雪の縦走路に座る峰。西面は忠別川へ切れ落ちる。
274.description_en_long: A 1,963 m volcano lying in the southern part of the main Daisetsu massif in Hokkaido. While its east side continues into the gentle Takanegahara plateau, its west face drops in cliffs to the Chubetsu River, a summit on the traverse route to Tomuraushi.
274.description_en_short: A peak on the main Daisetsu traverse, its west face dropping in cliffs to the Chubetsu River.

275.description_ja_long: 新潟・長野県境に猫ノ耳と呼ばれる双耳峰を掲げる名峰。塩の道の旅人に親しまれ、山名の由来には「雨や天を祀る山」など諸説が伝わる。標高1,963m。
275.description_ja_short: 猫ノ耳の双耳峰を掲げ、塩の道を見守ってきた県境の名峰。
275.description_en_long: Amakazariyama, a celebrated 1,963 m peak on the Niigata-Nagano border, raises twin summits called the Cat's Ears. Long familiar to travelers on the old Salt Road, its name has several explanations, including 'a mountain enshrining rain and the heavens.'
275.description_en_short: A twin-peaked 'Cat's Ears' summit on the prefectural border, long watching over the old Salt Road.

277.description_ja_long: 日高山脈北部、幌尻岳と尾根続きに立つ標高1,959mの峰。七ツ沼カールなど二度の氷河期が刻んだカール群を抱き、鋭いピラミッド型が巨峰と好対照をなす。
277.description_ja_short: 幌尻岳と対をなすピラミッド型の鋭峰。氷河のカールを抱く。
277.description_en_long: Tottabetsudake is a 1,959 m peak in the northern Hidaka Range, standing on a ridge continuous with Horoshiridake. It cradles cirques carved by two glacial ages, including the Nanatsunuma Cirque, and its sharp pyramidal form contrasts finely with its massive neighbor.
277.description_en_short: A sharp pyramidal peak paired with Horoshiridake, cradling glacial cirques.

278.description_ja_long: 長野県、南アルプス最北端に位置する標高1,955mの山。頂からは南・中央・北アルプスに八ヶ岳、富士山までほぼ360度の大観が広がり、山上の湿原は花の宝庫。
278.description_ja_short: 南アルプス北端の展望峰。湿原の花と360度の眺め。
278.description_en_long: Nyukasayama, a 1,955 m mountain in Nagano at the northernmost end of the Southern Alps, offers a nearly 360-degree panorama taking in the Southern, Central and Northern Alps, Yatsugatake and even Mt. Fuji, while the marshland on the mountain is a treasury of flowers.
278.description_en_short: A viewpoint peak at the north end of the Southern Alps, with marshland flowers and a 360-degree panorama.

279.description_ja_long: 徳島県の最高峰で標高1,955m、西日本第二の高峰。名とは裏腹に女性的な山容で、頂は「平家の馬場」と呼ばれる笹原。夏はキレンゲショウマが彩る信仰の山。
279.description_ja_short: 笹原の頂をもつ徳島県の最高峰。西日本第二の高峰の霊山。
279.description_en_long: Tsurugisan, the highest peak of Tokushima at 1,955 m, is second highest in western Japan. Despite its sword-like name its form is gentle, the summit a bamboo-grass plain called Heike no Baba. A mountain of worship, it is graced by Kirengeshoma blooms in summer.
279.description_en_short: Tokushima's highest peak with a bamboo-grass summit, a sacred mountain and second highest in western Japan.

280.description_ja_long: 群馬・新潟県境、谷川岳から平標山へ延びる縦走路のほぼ中間に座る山。太平洋側と日本海側の水を分ける中央分水嶺をなし、谷川連峰縦走の要の頂である。
280.description_ja_short: 上越国境稜線のほぼ中間に立つ峰。谷川連峰縦走の要の頂。
280.description_en_long: Mantaroyama sits roughly midway along the traverse route from Tanigawadake to Tairappyoyama on the Gunma-Niigata border. Part of the central watershed dividing waters between the Pacific and the Sea of Japan, it is a key summit of the Tanigawa Range traverse.
280.description_en_short: A peak roughly midway along the Joetsu border ridge, a key summit of the Tanigawa Range traverse.

281.description_ja_long: 福島県、吾妻連峰の東部にそびえる活火山。岩礫の荒涼とした頂に立てば、眼下に「魔女の瞳」とも呼ばれる瑠璃色の五色沼が輝き、湿原と沼が彩りを添える。
281.description_ja_short: 瑠璃色の五色沼を眼下にする活火山。吾妻連峰東部の一座。
281.description_en_long: Higashiazumayama is an active volcano rising in the eastern Azuma Range in Fukushima. From its desolate, rock-strewn summit, the lapis-blue crater lake Goshikinuma, also called the "Witch's Eye," glitters below, with marshes and ponds adding further color.
281.description_en_short: An active volcano in the eastern Azuma Range, overlooking the lapis-blue Goshikinuma pond.

282.description_ja_long: 群馬・新潟県境、湯桧曽川を挟んで谷川岳と相対する標高1,945mの山。朝日を最初に受けて輝く峰の名を持ち、蛇紋岩の頂にホソバヒナウスユキソウが咲く。
282.description_ja_short: 朝日に輝く名を持つ峰。蛇紋岩の頂に高山の稀花が咲く。
282.description_en_long: Asahidake, a 1,945 m mountain on the Gunma-Niigata border, faces Tanigawadake across the Yubiso River. Bearing a name that means the peak first to catch and shine in the morning sun, its serpentinite summit blooms with the rare alpine flower Hosoba-hinausuyukiso.
282.description_en_short: A peak named for glowing in the morning sun; rare alpine flowers bloom on its serpentinite summit.

283.description_ja_long: 屋久島の中央に鎮座する九州最高峰、標高1,936m。海岸からは望めない「奥岳」の主で、花崗岩の頂をヤクザサとシャクナゲが覆う世界遺産の山である。
283.description_ja_short: 洋上アルプスと呼ばれる屋久島の頂点にして九州最高峰。
283.description_en_long: Miyanouradake, seated at the center of Yakushima, is the highest peak in Kyushu at 1,936 m. Lord of the "inner mountains" that cannot be seen from the coast, it is a World Heritage mountain whose granite summit is cloaked in Yakushima dwarf bamboo and rhododendrons.
283.description_en_short: The summit of Yakushima, called the Alps on the ocean, and the highest peak in Kyushu.

284.description_ja_long: 北海道、東大雪の石狩連峰にあって石狩岳と並び立つ標高1,932mの山。名は音更川の源頭にあることによるといい、広い台地状の頂から大雪の山々を望む。
284.description_ja_short: 石狩岳と並び立つ東大雪の峰。頂は広い台地の一角をなす。
284.description_en_long: Otofukeyama is a 1,932 m mountain standing beside Ishikaridake in the Ishikari Range of eastern Daisetsu, Hokkaido. Its name is said to come from its position at the headwaters of the Otofuke River, and its broad plateau-like summit overlooks the Daisetsu mountains.
284.description_en_short: A peak of eastern Daisetsu standing beside Ishikaridake, its summit part of a broad plateau.

285.description_ja_long: 愛媛県、石鎚山から西へ延びる尾根上の一峰で県内第二の高峰。その順位が名になったといわれ、石鎚山頂から望む三角錐の姿は堂々たる風格を漂わせる。
285.description_ja_short: 石鎚山に次ぐ愛媛県第二の高峰。三角錐の堂々たる姿を誇る。
285.description_en_long: Ninomori is a peak on the ridge extending west from Ishizuchisan and the second highest mountain in Ehime. Its name is said to derive from that ranking, and its triangular pyramid form, seen from the summit of Ishizuchisan, carries an air of stately dignity.
285.description_en_short: Ehime's second highest peak after Ishizuchisan, proud of its stately triangular form.

286.description_ja_long: 長野県中部、美ヶ原の南に草原のドームを盛り上げる標高1,929mの山。鉢を伏せた姿が名の由来で、北アルプスから富士山まで360度の大観をほしいままにする。
286.description_ja_short: 鉢を伏せたような草原の山。アルプスを一望する大展望台。
286.description_en_long: Hachibuseyama is a 1,929 m mountain raising a grassy dome south of Utsukushigahara in central Nagano. Named for its shape like an upturned bowl, it commands a sweeping 360-degree panorama that stretches from the Northern Alps all the way to Mt. Fuji.
286.description_en_short: A grassland mountain shaped like an upturned bowl, a grand viewpoint surveying the Alps.

288.description_ja_long: 吾妻連峰の中央にどっしり構える火山。山頂は針葉樹林に包まれて目立たず、北西面の弥兵衛平には池塘が点在し、ワタスゲなど湿原の花が咲き広がる。
288.description_ja_short: 吾妻連峰中央の穏やかな火山。弥兵衛平の池塘と湿原の花が美しい。
288.description_en_long: Nishiazumayama is a volcano sitting solidly at the center of the Azuma Range. Its summit, wrapped in conifer forest, is inconspicuous, while on the northwestern slope the Yaheidaira marsh is dotted with ponds and blooms widely with wetland flowers such as cotton grass.
288.description_en_short: A gentle volcano at the center of the Azuma Range, beautiful with the ponds and marsh flowers of Yaheidaira.

289.description_ja_long: 長野県中部に広がるなだらかな草原の火山。最高点の車山を中心に八島ヶ原などの湿原が点在し、夏はニッコウキスゲの群落が高原を黄色に染める。
289.description_ja_short: なだらかな草原が広がる高原の山。夏はニッコウキスゲが咲く。
289.description_en_long: Kirigamine is a gently rolling grassland volcano spreading across central Nagano. Around Kurumayama, its highest point, lie marshes such as Yashimagahara, and in summer massed blooms of Nikko day lilies dye the highland yellow.
289.description_en_short: A highland mountain of gentle grasslands, where Nikko day lilies bloom in summer.

290.description_ja_long: 十勝岳の南西に連なる主稜線上の高点。穏やかな南面に対し火口側は岩峰が立ち並ぶ荒々しい壁で、山名はアイヌ語の「逆さ川の奥の高い山」に由来する。
290.description_ja_short: 十勝岳連峰の一峰。火口側に岩峰が並び、名はアイヌ語に由来。
290.description_en_long: Kamihorokamettokuyama is a high point on the main ridge southwest of Tokachidake. In contrast to its gentle south face, the crater side is a wild wall lined with rock pinnacles; the name is Ainu for "high mountain at the back of the reverse-flowing river."
290.description_en_short: A peak of the Tokachidake Range with rock pinnacles on its crater side; its name comes from Ainu.

291.description_ja_long: 那須連峰の北端に座るどっしりとした山。三藩が領界確認のため頂に槍を立てたことが名の由来と伝わり、一等三角点の山頂は360度の展望が開ける。
291.description_ja_short: 那須連峰北端の重厚な山。槍を立てた領界確認が名の由来。
291.description_en_long: Sanbonyaridake is a solidly built mountain at the northern end of the Nasu Range. Its name is said to come from three domains planting spears on the summit to confirm their borders, and the top, holding a first-order triangulation point, opens a 360-degree view.
291.description_en_short: A massive mountain at the north end of the Nasu Range, named for spears planted to confirm borders.

292.description_ja_long: 北上山地の最高峰。『遠野物語』や宮沢賢治の作品に登場し、蛇紋岩の山肌にはハヤチネウスユキソウなど固有の高山植物が咲く、信仰と花の名山。
292.description_ja_short: 北上山地の最高峰。固有種の高山植物が咲く信仰の山。
292.description_en_long: Hayachinesan is the highest peak of the Kitakami Highlands. Appearing in the Tono Monogatari and in Kenji Miyazawa's works, it is a famed mountain of faith and flowers, its serpentinite slopes blooming with endemic alpine plants such as Hayachine-usuyukiso.
292.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Kitakami Highlands, a mountain of faith blooming with endemic alpine plants.

293.description_ja_long: 長野市の北にすそ野を広げる北信五岳の一峰。名は山中に生じた「天狗の麦飯」という菌類に由来し、修験者はこれを食料にしたと伝わる信仰の山。
293.description_ja_short: 北信五岳の一峰。「天狗の麦飯」に名が由来する修験の山。
293.description_en_long: Iizunayama spreads its skirts north of Nagano City as one of the Five Peaks of Hokushin. A mountain of faith, it takes its name from a fungus found on the mountain called "tengu no mugimeshi" (tengu's barley rice), which shugen ascetics are said to have eaten as food.
293.description_en_short: One of the Five Peaks of Hokushin, a shugendo mountain named after the "tengu's barley rice" fungus.

294.description_ja_long: 北日高の主稜からやや東に外れた岩尾根の頂。名はアイヌ語で「沼貝が多い」の意といい、山頂は戸蔦別岳を彩るカール群を望む格好の展望台となる。
294.description_ja_short: 日高山脈北部の岩の頂。カール群を望む展望台として知られる。
294.description_en_long: Pipairodake is a rocky-ridge summit lying slightly east of the main crest of the northern Hidaka Range. Its name is said to mean "abounding in marsh shellfish" in Ainu, and the summit makes an ideal viewpoint over the cirques that adorn Tottabetsudake.
294.description_en_short: A rocky summit in the northern Hidaka Range, known as a viewpoint over the cirques.

295.description_ja_long: 大峰山脈の主峰にして近畿地方の最高峰。役行者が法華経八巻を埋めたという伝説が名の由来で、稜線には天然記念物オオヤマレンゲの群落が花開く。
295.description_ja_short: 近畿最高峰。法華経八巻を埋めた伝説が名の由来と伝わる。
295.description_en_long: Hakkyogatake is the main peak of the Omine Range and the highest mountain in the Kinki region. Its name comes from the legend that En no Gyoja buried the eight scrolls of the Lotus Sutra here, and groves of Oyamarenge, a natural monument, flower along the ridge.
295.description_en_short: The highest peak in Kinki, named, tradition says, for the legend of eight buried Lotus Sutra scrolls.

296.description_ja_long: 栃木県北部にそびえる那須連山の主峰・茶臼岳を指す名。今も白い噴煙をたなびかせる活火山で、春のツツジと燃えるような秋の紅葉に山肌が彩られる。
296.description_ja_short: 噴煙を上げる那須連山の主峰・茶臼岳。紅葉の美しさで名高い。
296.description_en_long: Nasudake is the name given to Chausudake, the main peak of the Nasu mountains rising in northern Tochigi. An active volcano still trailing white plumes of smoke, its slopes are colored by azaleas in spring and by blazing autumn foliage.
296.description_en_short: Chausudake, the smoking main peak of the Nasu mountains, famed for its beautiful autumn colors.

297.description_ja_long: 十勝連峰の最南端に立つ山。古い時代に火山活動を終えたため高山植物がことのほか豊かで、夏の山頂部は連峰随一のお花畑が広がる花の名山である。
297.description_ja_short: 十勝連峰最南端の山。夏に広がるお花畑で名高い花の名峰。
297.description_en_long: Furanodake stands at the southernmost end of the Tokachi Range. Because its volcanic activity ended in ancient times, alpine flora is exceptionally rich, and in summer the summit area spreads into the range's finest flower fields, a celebrated mountain of flowers.
297.description_en_short: The southernmost mountain of the Tokachi Range, a famed flower peak with summer flower fields.

298.description_ja_long: 那須連山と高原山の間に横たわる山域の主峰。コメツガやブナの原生林に深く覆われ、明瞭な道を持たない。残雪期にのみ人を迎える奥深い秘峰である。
298.description_ja_short: 原生林に覆われた奥深い主峰。残雪期に登られる秘峰。
298.description_en_long: Osabiyama is the main peak of the mountain mass lying between the Nasu mountains and Takaharayama. Deeply covered in virgin forest of hemlock and beech, it has no clear trail — a remote, hidden peak that welcomes climbers only in the lingering-snow season.
298.description_en_short: A remote main peak covered in virgin forest, a hidden mountain climbed in the lingering-snow season.

299.description_ja_long: 天照大神が隠れた天の岩戸が飛来してできたと伝わる神話の山。屏風のような岩壁が連なり、幅狭い岩稜「蟻の塔渡り」を越えた先に山頂の八方睨がある。
299.description_ja_short: 天の岩戸伝説を宿す岩屏風の山。修験の道場として知られる。
299.description_en_long: Togakushiyama is a mountain of myth, said to have formed when the heavenly rock door behind which Amaterasu hid flew here. Screen-like rock walls range in succession, and beyond the narrow rock-ridge passage called Ari no Towatari lies Happonirami, the summit.
299.description_en_short: A mountain of screen-like rock walls bearing the heavenly rock door legend, known as a shugendo training ground.

300.title_ja: エサオマントッタベツ岳
300.description_ja_long: 日高山脈北部の奥深い頂。北から仰ぐと東西に大きなカールを抱え、翼を広げた大鷲を思わせる。登山道はなく沢と尾根をたどる者だけが立てる。
300.description_ja_short: カールを抱く日高の奥深い頂。大鷲を思わせる山容をもつ。
300.description_en_long: Esaomantottabetsudake is a deep, remote summit in the northern Hidaka Range. Seen from the north it holds great cirques on its east and west sides, recalling a giant eagle with wings spread. There is no trail; only those tracing streams and ridges can stand on top.
300.description_en_short: A remote Hidaka summit cradling cirques, its form recalling a great eagle with wings spread.

301.description_ja_long: 均整のとれた円錐形から蝦夷富士と呼ばれる成層火山。アイヌ語では「女山」を意味するマチネシリと呼ばれ、尻別岳と対をなす夫婦山とされてきた。
301.description_ja_short: 蝦夷富士と呼ばれる端正な成層火山。西南北海道の最高峰。
301.description_en_long: Yoteizan is a stratovolcano called Ezo Fuji for its well-balanced conical form. In Ainu it is known as Machineshiri, meaning "female mountain," and it has long been regarded, together with Shiribetsudake, as one of a married pair of mountains.
301.description_en_short: A shapely stratovolcano called Ezo Fuji, the highest peak of southwestern Hokkaido.

302.description_ja_long: 石鎚山脈に連なる愛媛県第3の高峰。西側に氷見二千石原と呼ぶ広大なササ原を敷き、名は湧水をたたえる甌穴「瓶壺」に由来するという古い信仰の山。
302.description_ja_short: 広大なササ原・氷見二千石原を抱く信仰の山。愛媛第3の高峰。
302.description_en_long: Kamegamori, part of the Ishizuchi Range, is the third highest peak in Ehime. On its western side spreads the vast bamboo-grass plain called Himi-Nisengokuhara, and its name is said to derive from Kametsubo, a pothole holding spring water — an old mountain of faith.
302.description_en_short: A mountain of faith with the vast bamboo-grass plain Himi-Nisengokuhara, Ehime's third highest peak.

303.description_ja_long: 大峰山脈の中央に構える標高1,895mの山。トウヒやシラベの原生林に覆われた平坦な頂に天河神社の奥宮が鎮まり、古くからの祈りの気配を漂わせる。
303.description_ja_short: 原生林に覆われた大峰の山。頂に天河神社の奥宮が鎮まる。
303.description_en_long: Misen is a 1,895 m peak standing at the heart of the Omine Range. Its flat summit, cloaked in a primeval forest of spruce and Maries' fir, holds the inner shrine of Tenkawa Shrine and carries an air of prayer handed down from ancient times.
303.description_en_short: A mountain of the Omine Range cloaked in primeval forest, with the inner shrine of Tenkawa Shrine on its summit.

304.description_ja_long: 日高山脈の主稜から東に派生した尾根上の高峰。名はアイヌ語の「乾いた川」に由来し、カムイエクウチカウシ山を正面に望む山また山の展望が魅力。
304.description_ja_short: 日高の主稜から東に外れた高峰。名はアイヌ語の乾いた川。
304.description_en_long: Satsunaidake is a lofty peak on a ridge branching east from the main crest of the Hidaka Mountains. Its name derives from an Ainu word meaning 'dry river,' and the summit offers a view of mountains upon mountains, with Kamuiekuuchikaushiyama rising straight ahead.
304.description_en_short: A lofty Hidaka peak east of the main crest, named from an Ainu word meaning 'dry river.'

305.description_ja_long: 剣山の西、四国山脈の主稜線を再び高々と持ち上げる高知県最高峰。全山を包むササ原にコメツツジが点在し、四国で最も美しい山と讃えられてきた。
305.description_ja_short: ササ原にコメツツジが点在する高知県最高峰。四国屈指の美峰。
305.description_en_long: West of Tsurugisan, Miune lifts the main crest of the Shikoku Mountains high once more; it is Kochi's highest peak. Dwarf bamboo covers the whole mountain, dotted with kometsutsuji azaleas, and it has long been praised as the most beautiful mountain in Shikoku.
305.description_en_short: Kochi's highest peak, its dwarf-bamboo slopes dotted with kometsutsuji azaleas — one of Shikoku's most beautiful mountains.

306.description_ja_long: 足尾の山中に立つ庚申信仰の霊山。勝道上人の開山と伝わり、『南総里見八犬伝』でも知られる奇岩怪石がそびえ、固有種コウシンソウが自生する。
306.description_ja_short: 奇岩怪石そびえる庚申信仰の霊山。コウシンソウの自生地。
306.description_en_long: Koshinzan is a sacred mountain of Koshin worship deep in the Ashio hills. Said to have been opened by the priest Shodo, it bristles with the strange rocks and crags known from the novel Nanso Satomi Hakkenden, and the endemic koshinso plant grows wild here.
306.description_en_short: A sacred peak of Koshin worship bristling with strange crags, home to the endemic koshinso plant.

307.description_ja_long: 南アルプスに沿って走る伊那山地の最高峰。訪れる人は少なく、麓の大鹿村は南朝の宗良親王が再起を期して籠った地。山頂には赤石岳や聖岳が迫る。
307.description_ja_short: 伊那山地の最高峰。山頂から赤石岳や聖岳の眺めがすばらしい。
307.description_en_long: Kimenzan is the highest peak of the Ina Mountains, which run alongside the Southern Alps. Few climbers visit; Oshika Village at its foot is where Prince Munenaga of the Southern Court holed up awaiting a comeback. From the summit, Akaishidake and Hijiridake loom close.
307.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Ina Mountains, with superb summit views of Akaishidake and Hijiridake.

308.description_ja_long: 北アルプス北部の静かな山。山麓には北アルプス最大といわれる風吹大池が水をたたえ、周囲の湿原はミズバショウの花や紅葉が季節ごとに彩りを変える。
308.description_ja_short: 北アルプス最大の池・風吹大池を抱く山。湿原の花が美しい。
308.description_en_long: Kazafukidake is a quiet mountain in the northern part of the Northern Alps. At its foot lies Kazafuki Oike, said to be the largest pond in the Northern Alps, and the surrounding wetlands change color with the seasons, from skunk cabbage blooms to autumn leaves.
308.description_en_short: A quiet mountain cradling Kazafuki Oike, said to be the Northern Alps' largest pond, ringed by flowering wetlands.

309.description_ja_long: 宮之浦岳の西に対峙する屋久島第2の高峰。花崗岩の荒々しい山容にロウソク岩の岩峰を従え、古くから永田集落の岳参りの対象として崇められてきた。
309.description_ja_short: 屋久島第2の高峰。花崗岩の荒々しい山容と岳参りで知られる。
309.description_en_long: Nagatadake, facing Miyanouradake from the west, is the second-highest peak on Yakushima. Its rugged granite form is attended by the rock pinnacle of Rosoku-iwa, and it has long been revered by the village of Nagata as the object of its takemairi mountain pilgrimage.
309.description_en_short: Yakushima's second-highest peak, known for its rugged granite form and the takemairi pilgrimage.

310.title_ja: ニセイカウシュッペ山
310.description_ja_long: 層雲峡の北に大きく構える山。名はアイヌ語で「断崖絶壁の上にいる者」の意で、その山容を言い当てる。お花畑の頂から大雪山の展望がすばらしい。
310.description_ja_short: アイヌ語で絶壁の上にいる者を意味する、大雪山北方の雄峰。
310.description_en_long: Niseikaushuppeyama rises grandly to the north of Sounkyo Gorge. Its name, Ainu for 'one who sits above the cliffs,' aptly captures its form, and from its flower-field summit the view of the Daisetsuzan massif is superb.
310.description_en_short: A commanding peak north of Daisetsuzan whose Ainu name means 'one who sits above the cliffs.'

311.description_ja_long: 日高山脈の主稜から日高側へ張り出した尾根に座る山。頂上一帯は広々としたお花畑で、千呂露川を隔てて幌尻岳やピパイロ岳の威容が迫り立つ。
311.description_ja_short: お花畑の頂から幌尻岳を望む、千呂露川上流の日高の山。
311.description_en_long: Chirorodake sits on a ridge jutting out from the main crest of the Hidaka Mountains toward the Hidaka side. Its broad summit area is one great flower field, and across the Chiroro River the imposing forms of Poroshiridake and Pipairodake rise before it.
311.description_en_short: A Hidaka mountain above the upper Chiroro River, its flower-field summit facing Poroshiridake.

312.description_ja_long: 石狩川の北に連なる北大雪の雄峰。どっしりした姿は石狩岳に匹敵する風格と評され、岩尾根の頂からは大雪山やオホーツク海までが見渡せる。
312.description_ja_short: 北大雪の雄峰。頂からオホーツク海までの大展望が広がる。
312.description_en_long: Muridake is a stately peak of the Kita-Taisetsu range stretching north of the Ishikari River. Its massive form is said to rival Ishikaridake in dignity, and from the rocky ridge of its summit the view sweeps from Daisetsuzan all the way to the Sea of Okhotsk.
312.description_en_short: A stately peak of Kita-Taisetsu with a vast summit panorama reaching to the Sea of Okhotsk.

313.description_ja_long: 白川郷の東にそびえる飛騨高地の最高峰で標高1,875m。平頂の広さが山名の由来とされ、前衛の帰雲山は天正の大地震で帰雲城を一夜で埋めた悲話を伝える。
313.description_ja_short: 白川郷の東に構える飛騨高地の最高峰。広く平らな頂が名の由来とされる。
313.description_en_long: Sarugababayama, at 1,875 m, is the highest peak of the Hida Highlands east of Shirakawa-go. Its broad flat top is said to explain the name, while the outlying Kaerikumoyama recalls the tragedy of Kaerikumo Castle, buried overnight by the great Tensho earthquake.
313.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Hida Highlands east of Shirakawa-go, named, it is said, for its broad flat top.

314.description_ja_long: 山梨県大月市の山で標高1,874m。雁が腹を擦るように峠を越えた姿が名の由来と伝わり、旧五百円札の富士山はこの頂で撮影された写真を原画とする。
314.description_ja_short: 旧五百円札の富士を撮った頂。雁が峠を越えた姿を名に負う。
314.description_en_long: Gangaharasuriyama is a 1,874 m mountain in Otsuki, Yamanashi. Its name is said to come from wild geese crossing the pass so low they brushed their bellies, and the Mt. Fuji on the old 500-yen note was based on a photograph taken from this summit.
314.description_en_short: The peak from which the old 500-yen note's Mt. Fuji was photographed, named for geese brushing their bellies over the pass.

315.description_ja_long: 山形県西部、豪雪の朝日連峰の主峰で標高1,871m。端正な三角錐の頂に花崗片麻岩が露われ、雪解け水が育むお花畑や池塘が稜線の山旅を彩る。
315.description_ja_short: 豪雪が育む朝日連峰の主峰。三角錐の頂と花畑の稜線が美しい。
315.description_en_long: Asahidake, at 1,871 m, is the main peak of the snowbound Asahi Range in western Yamagata. Granite gneiss lies exposed on its shapely pyramidal summit, and flower fields and ponds nourished by meltwater adorn a ridge-line journey through the range.
315.description_en_short: Main peak of the snow-rich Asahi Range, with a shapely pyramidal summit and flower-decked ridges.

316.description_ja_long: 石鎚連峰の東部、愛媛・高知県境の標高1,860m。山名通り一面のササに覆われた頂は伊予の三名山に数えられ、石鎚山より古いと伝わる霊山の歴史を持つ。
316.description_ja_short: ササに覆われたなだらかな頂。石鎚山より古い歴史を持つ霊山。
316.description_en_long: Sasagamine, 1,860 m on the Ehime-Kochi border in the eastern Ishizuchi Range, is covered in dwarf bamboo just as its name says. The summit is counted among the three famous mountains of Iyo, a sacred peak whose history is said to be older than Ishizuchisan's.
316.description_en_short: A gently rounded sacred peak blanketed in dwarf bamboo, with a history said to predate Ishizuchisan.

317.description_ja_long: 石鎚山の南東、愛媛・高知県境に立つ標高1,860mの修験の山。ドーム状の頂は山座同定の目印とされ、笹原にコメツツジが群れ咲き祠が鎮まる。
317.description_ja_short: ドーム状の頂が目を引く修験の山。笹原にコメツツジが咲く。
317.description_en_long: Tsutsujozan is a 1,860 m mountain of Shugendo asceticism on the Ehime-Kochi border southeast of Ishizuchisan. Its dome-shaped summit is a landmark for identifying peaks, and kometsutsuji azaleas bloom in drifts across the bamboo grassland where a shrine rests.
317.description_en_short: A dome-topped mountain of Shugendo practice, its bamboo grassland abloom with kometsutsuji azaleas.

318.description_ja_long: 立山連峰の北端に位置する富山県の山で標高1,855m。春の雪解けに虚無僧や馬の雪絵が現れることが名の起こりで、麓の人々は農作業の目安とした。
318.description_ja_short: 春に僧の雪絵が浮かぶ立山連峰北端の山。農事の暦とされた。
318.description_en_long: Sogadake, a 1,855 m mountain in Toyama at the northern end of the Tateyama Range, takes its name from snow patterns of a komuso monk and a horse that appear with the spring thaw, which the people at its foot once used as a guide for their farm work.
318.description_en_short: A peak at the Tateyama Range's north end where a monk's snow figure appears in spring, once a farmers' calendar.

319.description_ja_long: 長野県と静岡県の境に頂を置く標高1,851mの山。南アルプス深南部と呼ばれる山域に属し、その名の通り山深い一帯に静かなたたずまいを見せる。
319.description_ja_short: 南アルプス深南部にたたずむ、長野・静岡県境の静かな山。
319.description_en_long: Shirakurayama is a 1,851 m mountain whose summit sits on the border of Nagano and Shizuoka. Belonging to the region known as the deep southern Southern Alps, it keeps a quiet presence in these remote mountain depths, true to the area's name.
319.description_en_short: A quiet peak on the Nagano-Shizuoka border, deep in the remote southern reaches of the Southern Alps.

320.description_ja_long: 徳島県西部、祖谷川を挟んで剣山地と向き合う祖谷山地の盟主で標高1,849m。天を突く鋭い山容が名の由来を思わせ、シコクザサの頂は遮るもののない展望が広がる。
320.description_ja_short: 祖谷山地の盟主。矢筈の名の通り天を突き、笹の頂は展望絶佳。
320.description_en_long: Yahazuyama, 1,849 m, is the monarch of the Iya Mountains in western Tokushima, facing the Tsurugi Range across the Iya River. Its sharp, sky-piercing form suggests the origin of its name, and its summit of Shikoku dwarf bamboo offers an unobstructed panorama.
320.description_en_short: Monarch of the Iya Mountains, piercing the sky as its arrow-nock name suggests, with sweeping views from its bamboo summit.

321.description_ja_long: 東大雪の南部に長い頂稜を横たえる標高1,848mの火山。山名はアイヌ語で「雪解け水をどっと出してくる山」の意で、頂は西峰から糠平富士まで東西2kmに及ぶ。
321.description_ja_short: 雪解け水を出す山を意味する東大雪の火山。長い頂稜を持つ。
321.description_en_long: Upepesankeyama is a 1,848 m volcano laying its long summit crest across southern Higashi-Taisetsu. The Ainu name means 'mountain that sends forth torrents of snowmelt,' and the crest runs some 2 km east to west from the west peak to Nukabira-Fuji.
321.description_en_short: A Higashi-Taisetsu volcano with a long summit crest, its Ainu name meaning a mountain pouring out snowmelt.

322.description_ja_long: 十勝平野の西にひときわ大きく迫る日高山脈の山で標高1,846m。山名はアイヌ語で大きな山を意味するポロシリに十勝を冠したもので、頂は北日高随一の展望台となる。
322.description_ja_short: アイヌ語で大きな山の意。十勝平野に迫る北日高の展望台。
322.description_en_long: Tokachiporoshiridake, 1,846 m, is a mountain of the Hidaka Range looming exceptionally large west of the Tokachi Plain. Its name joins 'Tokachi' to poroshiri, Ainu for 'great mountain,' and its summit is the finest viewpoint in the northern Hidaka Range.
322.description_en_short: Ainu for 'great mountain,' looming over the Tokachi Plain — the finest viewpoint in the northern Hidaka Range.

324.description_ja_long: 白山の北方、三県境にそびえる登山道を持たない山で標高1,841m。深田久弥が百名山に入れ損ねたと悔やんだ峰で、頂からは修験の遺品の経筒や仏像が出土した。
324.description_ja_short: 深田久弥が選び損ねたと悔やんだ、道なき残雪期の秘峰。
324.description_en_long: Oizurugatake, 1,841 m, is a trail-less mountain rising at the junction of three prefectures north of Hakusan. Kyuya Fukada lamented failing to include it among his 100 Famous Mountains, and sutra cases and Buddhist statues left by ascetics were unearthed on its summit.
324.description_en_short: A pathless hidden peak for the lingering-snow season, which Kyuya Fukada regretted leaving off his hundred.

325.description_ja_long: 山形・宮城県境に連なる活火山群の総称で、最高峰は熊野岳の標高1,841m。吉野の蔵王権現を祀って名を得た信仰の山で、火口湖の御釜が山上の象徴となる。
325.description_ja_short: 御釜を抱く活火山群の総称。蔵王権現を祀った東の霊山。
325.description_en_long: Zaozan is the collective name for a group of active volcanoes along the Yamagata-Miyagi border, topped by Kumanodake at 1,841 m. A mountain of faith named for enshrining Zao Gongen of Yoshino, it takes as its symbol the crater lake Okama high on the range.
325.description_en_short: A cluster of active volcanoes cradling the crater lake Okama, a sacred range of the east enshrining Zao Gongen.

326.description_ja_long: 関東平野に裾野を広げる群馬の名山で、上毛三山の一つ。最高峰黒檜山は標高1,828m。カルデラに大沼・小沼をたたえる複式火山で、初夏は多彩なツツジに彩られる。
326.description_ja_short: 大沼を抱く上毛三山の一峰。初夏はツツジが山を彩る。
326.description_en_long: Mt. Akagi, one of the Three Mountains of Jomo, spreads its long skirts over the Kanto Plain in Gunma; its highest point, Kurobisan, stands 1,828 m. A compound volcano holding lakes Onuma and Konuma in its caldera, it glows with varied azaleas in early summer.
326.description_en_short: One of the Three Mountains of Jomo, cradling Lake Onuma, its slopes colored by azaleas in early summer.

327.description_ja_long: 宮城県側の蔵王連峰南部にそびえる屏風岳を最高点とし標高1,825m。東面が屏風のように切れ落ちる山容に対し西面はなだらかで、高層湿原の芝草平が花に埋まる。
327.description_ja_short: 東面が屏風と切れ落ちる宮城最高峰の屏風岳。湿原の花も見事。
327.description_en_long: This Zao rises on the Miyagi side of the southern Zao Range, culminating in Byobudake at 1,825 m, the prefecture's high point. Its east face drops away like a folding screen while the west side is gentle, and the alpine wetland of Shibakusadaira is buried in flowers.
327.description_en_short: Byobudake, Miyagi's highest point, falls away screen-like on its east face; the wetland of Shibakusadaira brims with flowers.

328.description_ja_long: 石川・富山県境に端正な三角形を見せる両白山地の山で標高1,822m。金沢平野から広く望まれ、江戸期の絵図では犀川の源流と誤って描かれたほど目立つ存在だった。
328.description_ja_short: 金沢平野から望む端正な三角錐。絵図に犀川源流と描かれた山。
328.description_en_long: Ogasayama, a 1,822 m mountain of the Ryohaku Mountains, shows a shapely triangle on the Ishikawa-Toyama border. Widely visible from the Kanazawa Plain, it stood out so much that Edo-period pictorial maps mistakenly drew it as the source of the Saigawa River.
328.description_en_short: A shapely triangular peak seen from across the Kanazawa Plain, once mistakenly mapped as the source of the Saigawa.

330.description_ja_long: 山梨・長野県境、奥秩父の西端に横たわる標高1,818mの山。東西に長い尾根が名を思わせ、笹原の稜線は太平洋と日本海を分ける分水嶺で、初夏レンゲツツジが咲く。
330.description_ja_short: 東西に長い笹の稜線が展望よし。二つの海を分ける分水嶺の山。
330.description_en_long: A 1,818 m mountain lying at the western edge of Okuchichibu on the Yamanashi-Nagano border. Its long east-west ridge suggests the name, and the bamboo-grass crest forms the watershed dividing the Pacific from the Sea of Japan. Renge azaleas bloom in early summer.
330.description_en_short: A long east-west bamboo-grass ridge with fine views, on the watershed dividing two seas.
