331.description_ja_long: 飛騨市河合町と高山市清見町の境に立つ標高1,816mの山。御前の名は信仰の山を拝む遥拝所を意味し、この峰は中世に栄えた白山信仰にちなむと伝えられる。
331.description_ja_short: 白山を拝む遥拝所に名を由来すると伝わる飛騨の静かな山。
331.description_en_long: A 1,816 m mountain on the border of Kawai in Hida City and Kiyomi in Takayama City. The name Gozen denotes a place of worship facing a sacred mountain, and this peak is said to derive from the Hakusan faith that flourished in medieval times.
331.description_en_short: A quiet Hida mountain whose name is said to come from a site for worshipping Mt. Hakusan from afar.

332.description_ja_long: 猪苗代湖の北にそびえる会津のシンボルで標高1,816m。天へ通じる岩の梯子が名の由来といわれ、明治の大噴火は裏磐梯に五色沼など無数の湖沼を生んだ。
332.description_ja_short: 会津富士と呼ばれる端正な活火山。明治の噴火が湖沼群を生んだ。
332.description_en_long: Rising 1,816 m north of Lake Inawashiro, Mt. Bandai is the symbol of Aizu. Its name is said to mean a rock ladder reaching to heaven, and its great Meiji-era eruption created countless lakes and ponds in Urabandai, including the Goshikinuma ponds.
332.description_en_short: A shapely active volcano called the Aizu Fuji; its Meiji-era eruption created a host of lakes and ponds.

333.description_ja_long: 祖谷の南、剣山地の西部に整った三角錐を立てる標高1,812mの山。シコクザサとコメツツジに包まれた頂は展望絶佳で、西に広がる笹の台地・牛ノ背が心を引く。
333.description_ja_short: どこから見ても三角錐の笹の峰。西に牛ノ背の台地が広がる。
333.description_en_long: A 1,812 m mountain raising a neat triangular pyramid in the western Tsurugi Range, south of Iya. The summit, wrapped in Shikoku bamboo grass and kome azaleas, commands superb views, and the grassy plateau of Ushinose spreading to the west is captivating.
333.description_en_short: A bamboo-grass peak that looks a triangular pyramid from every angle, with the Ushinose plateau to the west.

334.description_ja_long: 阿寺山地の南部、岐阜・長野県境の標高1,811mの山。三界山のさらに奥に立つことが名の由来で、江戸期は尾張藩がヒノキを守るため立ち入りを禁じた御料林だった。
334.description_ja_short: 三界山の奥に立つ阿寺山地の山。かつて尾張藩が入山を禁じた。
334.description_en_long: A 1,811 m mountain on the Gifu-Nagano border in the southern Atera Mountains. It is named for standing beyond Mt. Sangaisan, and in the Edo period it was a domain forest where the Owari clan banned entry to protect its hinoki cypress.
334.description_en_short: An Atera Mountains peak standing beyond Mt. Sangaisan; the Owari clan once banned entry here.

337.description_ja_long: 栃木県北部に裾を広げる那須火山帯の成層火山で、主峰釈迦ヶ岳は標高1,795m。谷文晁の日本名山図会にも描かれた名山で、静かな山歩きとヤシオツツジで知られる。
337.description_ja_short: 釈迦ヶ岳を主峰とする成層火山。谷文晁が名山図会に描いた。
337.description_en_long: A stratovolcano of the Nasu volcanic belt spreading its skirts across northern Tochigi, its main peak Shakagatake rising 1,795 m. A noted mountain depicted in Tani Buncho's illustrated book of famous Japanese mountains, it is loved for quiet hiking and yashio azaleas.
337.description_en_short: A stratovolcano whose main peak is Shakagatake, depicted by Tani Buncho in his book of famous mountains.

338.description_ja_long: 河口湖の北にそびえる御坂山塊の最高峰で標高1,793m。樹林の頂から南へわずかに下ると岩の展望地が開け、眼下の湖越しに富士山や南アルプスを一望できる。
338.description_ja_short: 御坂山塊の最高峰。頂の南の岩場から富士と河口湖を望む。
338.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Misaka Mountains, rising 1,793 m north of Lake Kawaguchi. A short descent south from the wooded summit opens onto a rocky viewpoint, where Mt. Fuji and the Southern Alps spread out beyond the lake below.
338.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Misaka Mountains; rocks just south of the top overlook Mt. Fuji and Lake Kawaguchi.

339.description_ja_long: 大分県西部に火山群が連なるくじゅう連山の最高峰は中岳の標高1,791mで、九州本土の最高点でもある。初夏はミヤマキリシマが山肌を桃色に染め上げる。
339.description_ja_short: 九州本土の最高点・中岳を擁す火山群。初夏は花に染まる。
339.description_en_long: The highest point of the Kuju Mountains, a volcanic group in western Oita, is Nakadake at 1,791 m, which is also the highest point on Kyushu's mainland. In early summer, Miyama-Kirishima azaleas dye the mountainsides pink.
339.description_en_short: A volcanic group whose Nakadake is the highest point of mainland Kyushu, dyed with azalea blooms in early summer.

341.description_ja_long: くじゅう連山の東の盟主・大船山は標高1,786m。船を伏せたような姿が名の起こりで、火口跡を彩るミヤマキリシマの大群落と山頂直下の御池の紅葉で名高い。
341.description_ja_short: 船を伏せた姿の大船山。ミヤマキリシマと御池の紅葉が見事。
341.description_en_long: Taisenzan, the eastern chief of the Kuju Mountains, stands 1,786 m. Named for its shape like an upturned boat, it is famed for great colonies of Miyama-Kirishima azaleas coloring its old crater and for autumn leaves at the Oike pond just below the summit.
341.description_en_short: Taisenzan, shaped like an upturned boat, famed for azalea colonies and autumn colors at the Oike pond.

342.description_ja_long: 河口湖の北東に三つの頂を並べる標高1,785mの山。古くは修験者が行き交う信仰の山で、最高点直下の屏風岩は岩登りの歴史を刻み、富士の展望は絶品と称される。
342.description_ja_short: 三つの頂と屏風岩を持つ、富士展望と岩登りで名高い信仰の山。
342.description_en_long: A 1,785 m mountain lining up three summits northeast of Lake Kawaguchi. Long a mountain of faith frequented by shugenja ascetics, its Byobuiwa cliff just below the highest point carries a history of rock climbing, and its view of Mt. Fuji is called superb.
342.description_en_short: A mountain of faith with three summits and the Byobuiwa cliff, famed for Fuji views and rock climbing.

343.description_ja_long: 奈良県、大峰山脈の鋭峰で標高1,780m。笙ノ窟など修験道の行場を抱き、見る方角で姿を変える岩峰は剱岳の八ツ峰にも例えられる。
343.description_ja_short: 修験の行場を抱く大峰の鋭峰。見る方角で姿を変える。
343.description_en_long: A sharp 1,780 m peak in the Omine Mountains of Nara Prefecture. Home to Shugendo training sites such as the Sho-no-Iwaya cave, this rocky peak changes its look with the viewing angle and has been likened to the Yatsumine ridge of Mt. Tsurugi.
343.description_en_short: A sharp Omine peak holding Shugendo training sites; its form changes with the direction you view it from.

344.description_ja_long: 新潟県南魚沼の霊山で越後三山の一座。頂稜には大日岳など八ツ峰の岩峰が連なり、鎖を伝う八剣巡りは今も修験の聖地として登拝者を集める。
344.description_ja_short: 八ツ峰の岩稜が連なる越後三山の霊山。八剣巡りは修験の聖地。
344.description_en_long: A sacred mountain in Minamiuonuma, Niigata, and one of the Three Mountains of Echigo. Rocky towers such as Dainichidake line its crest as the Yatsumine peaks, and the chain-assisted Hakken circuit still draws pilgrims to this holy ground of Shugendo.
344.description_en_short: A sacred Echigo Sanzan peak with the rocky Yatsumine crest; the Hakken circuit remains a Shugendo holy ground.

345.description_ja_long: 福島・栃木県境の男鹿山塊にある標高1,777mの山。明瞭な道がなく全山が深い樹林に覆われ、県境の山々の中でも最も登る人の少ない静寂の頂。
345.description_ja_short: 深い樹林に包まれた福島・栃木県境の山。静寂の道なき頂。
345.description_en_long: A 1,777 m mountain in the Ojika massif on the Fukushima-Tochigi border. With no clear trail and the whole mountain cloaked in deep forest, it is the quietest, least-climbed summit among the peaks along the prefectural border.
345.description_en_short: A trailless, silent summit wrapped in deep forest on the Fukushima-Tochigi border.

346.description_ja_long: 朝日連峰の北端に構える重量感あふれる山で標高1,772m。山頂の下には熊のなめし皮の形をした大鳥池が横たわり、怪魚タキタロウの伝説で名高い。
346.description_ja_short: 朝日連峰北端の重厚な山。怪魚タキタロウ伝説の大鳥池を抱く。
346.description_en_long: A massive mountain of great presence at the northern end of the Asahi Range, 1,772 m high. Below the summit lies Lake Otori, shaped like a tanned bear hide and famed for the legend of the mysterious giant fish Takitaro.
346.description_en_short: A hefty peak at the north end of the Asahi Range, holding Lake Otori and its legend of the fish Takitaro.

347.description_ja_long: 高知県東部、剣山地の西に立つ通称「物部の白髪」。冬に白く冠雪する秀麗な姿は土佐富士とも呼ばれ、笹原の広がる頂から三嶺や太平洋を望む。
347.description_ja_short: 土佐富士とも呼ばれる剣山地の秀峰。笹原と展望の山。
347.description_en_long: Known as the Shiraga of Monobe, this peak stands west of the Tsurugi Range in eastern Kochi. Its graceful form, capped white with snow in winter, earns it the name Tosa Fuji, and its bamboo-grass summit looks out to Miune and the Pacific Ocean.
347.description_en_short: A graceful Tsurugi Range peak also called the Tosa Fuji, with bamboo-grass slopes and fine views.

348.description_ja_long: 越中五箇山と飛騨白川郷の境に立つ標高1,764mの山。人形山と登路を分け合い、山頂には池塘とニッコウキスゲの群落があり北アルプスも望める。
348.description_ja_short: 五箇山と白川郷の境の山。池塘にニッコウキスゲが咲く。
348.description_en_long: A 1,764 m mountain on the border between Gokayama in Etchu and Shirakawa-go in Hida. Sharing its approach route with Ningyozan, it holds small ponds and colonies of day lilies on the summit, with views of the Northern Alps as well.
348.description_en_short: A mountain between Gokayama and Shirakawa-go, where day lilies bloom around summit ponds.

349.description_ja_long: 大分県九重町の南に立つ九重連峰の一峰で、町の最高峰。南に草原の西千里ヶ浜を広げ、北側には三俣山や飯田高原、由布岳までを一望に収める。
349.description_ja_short: 九重連峰の一峰で九重町の最高峰。草原を広げる大観の山。
349.description_en_long: A peak of the Kuju Range standing south of Kokonoe Town in Oita, and the town's highest point. The grassland of Nishisenrigahama spreads to its south, while to the north it commands a sweeping view of Mitsumatayama, the Handa Highland and even Mt. Yufu.
349.description_en_short: A Kuju peak and the highest point of Kokonoe Town, spreading grasslands beneath grand views.

350.description_ja_long: 北海道北見市と上川町にまたがる石狩山地の山で標高1,759m。無加川源流に平らな頂を横たえ、麓にはかつて日本有数の水銀鉱山が栄えていた。
350.description_ja_short: 石狩山地の平らな頂の山。麓に水銀鉱山の歴史を残す。
350.description_en_long: A mountain of the Ishikari Mountains straddling Kitami City and Kamikawa Town in Hokkaido, 1,759 m high. Its flat summit lies at the headwaters of the Muka River, and at its foot once flourished one of Japan's leading mercury mines.
350.description_en_short: A flat-topped peak in the Ishikari Mountains, keeping the history of a mercury mine at its foot.

351.description_ja_long: 蔵王連峰の南部、宮城県側に立つ一峰で山頂に刈田嶺神社を祀る。火口湖・御釜の好展望台で、江戸期には年間一万余の登拝者を集めたと伝わる。
351.description_ja_short: 火口湖・御釜を見下ろす蔵王の一峰。刈田嶺神社を頂に祀る。
351.description_en_long: A peak on the Miyagi side of the southern Zao Range, with Kattamine Shrine enshrined on its summit. A fine vantage point over the crater lake Okama, it is said to have drawn more than ten thousand pilgrims a year in the Edo period.
351.description_en_short: A Zao peak overlooking the crater lake Okama, with Kattamine Shrine enshrined on its summit.

352.description_ja_long: 群馬県西部に立つ標高1,757mの山。麓の温泉郷から浅間山を隠すことが名の由来で、二峰に分かれた頂は矢筈山とも呼ばれ、大展望を誇る。
352.description_ja_short: 浅間山を隠すことが名の由来の山。全方位の展望を誇る。
352.description_en_long: A 1,757 m mountain in western Gunma. It takes its name from hiding Mt. Asama from the hot-spring villages at its foot, and its summit, split into two peaks and also called Yahazuyama, boasts a grand panorama.
352.description_en_short: Named for hiding Mt. Asama from view, this two-peaked mountain boasts an all-round panorama.

354.description_ja_long: 愛媛・高知県境、石鎚山地に連なる一峰。西へなだらかな斜面を引き、見る方角では富士を思わせる。山頂からは瀬戸内海と土佐湾の両方を望める。
354.description_ja_short: 石鎚山地の郷土富士。瀬戸内海と土佐湾の両方を見渡す。
354.description_en_long: A peak in the Ishizuchi Range on the Ehime-Kochi border. Trailing a gentle slope to the west, it recalls Mt. Fuji from certain angles, and from the summit both the Seto Inland Sea and Tosa Bay can be seen at once.
354.description_en_short: A local Fuji of the Ishizuchi Range, overlooking both the Seto Inland Sea and Tosa Bay.

355.description_ja_long: 大分・宮崎県境に立つ祖母傾山群の主峰。神武天皇の祖母・豊玉姫を祀ることが山名の由来とされ、切り立つ岩峰を濃密な自然林が包む神々しい山。
355.description_ja_short: 神武天皇の祖母を祀る名を持つ霊峰。岩と原生林の山。
355.description_en_long: The main peak of the Sobo-Katamuki mountain group on the Oita-Miyazaki border. Its name is said to come from enshrining Toyotamahime, grandmother of Emperor Jimmu, and dense natural forest wraps its sheer rock peaks in a divine air.
355.description_en_short: A sacred peak named for the grandmother of Emperor Jimmu, a mountain of rock and virgin forest.

356.description_ja_long: 日高山脈の北部に位置する標高1,754mの山。かつて全山縦走の最終ピークとされ「日高最北端の山」と呼ばれた。尖った西峰が北の砦のように構える。
356.description_ja_short: 日高最北端の山と呼ばれた峰。鋭い西峰を砦のように従える。
356.description_en_long: A 1,754 m mountain in the northern Hidaka Mountains. Once regarded as the final peak of a full traverse of the range, it was called the northernmost mountain of Hidaka, and its pointed west peak stands guard like a northern fortress.
356.description_en_short: Once called the northernmost mountain of Hidaka, attended by a sharp west peak like a fortress.

357.description_ja_long: 日高山脈の主稜から西へ離れて巨体を横たえる標高1,752mの山。山名は川の名に由来しアイヌ語で「蟻」の意という。かつては遠い山の代名詞だった。
357.description_ja_short: 日高の主稜から離れて横たわる巨峰。名は蟻の意という。
357.description_en_long: A 1,752 m mountain laying its huge bulk apart from and west of the main ridge of the Hidaka Mountains. The name comes from a river and is said to mean 'ant' in the Ainu language. It was once a byword for a remote, faraway mountain.
357.description_en_short: A massive peak lying apart from the Hidaka main ridge; its name is said to mean 'ant' in Ainu.

358.description_ja_long: 大分県、九重連峰の北に堂々と構える山。どこから眺めても三つの峰に見えることが名の由来だが、実は四峰からなり、ミヤマキリシマが山肌を彩る。
358.description_ja_short: どこから見ても三峰に見える九重の山。花にも彩られる。
358.description_en_long: A commanding mountain in the north of the Kuju Range in Oita Prefecture. It is named for appearing as three peaks from every direction, though it actually consists of four, and Miyama-Kirishima azaleas adorn its mountainsides.
358.description_en_short: A Kuju mountain that looks three-peaked from anywhere, its slopes adorned with azalea blooms.

359.description_ja_long: 熊本・宮崎県境に立つ九州山地の最高峰で、熊本県の最高峰でもある。平家落人伝説の里、五家荘と椎葉に挟まれた山深い頂に広大な天然林が残る。
359.description_ja_short: 九州山地の最高峰。平家落人伝説の里に囲まれた奥山。
359.description_en_long: Kunimidake, on the Kumamoto-Miyazaki border, is the highest peak of the Kyushu Mountains and of Kumamoto Prefecture. Deep in the hills between Gokanosho and Shiiba, villages tied to legends of Heike refugees, its summit area preserves a vast natural forest.
359.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Kyushu Mountains, a remote summit surrounded by villages of Heike refugee legend.

360.description_ja_long: 南日高の女王と称される優雅な山で標高1,736m。深い谷に守られ「遥かなる山」と呼ばれ、厳冬期の初登頂には往復17時間の苦闘が刻まれている。
360.description_ja_short: 遥かなる山と呼ばれた南日高の女王。三つのカールを抱く。
360.description_en_long: Petegaridake, a graceful 1,736 m peak called the Queen of the Southern Hidaka, is guarded by deep valleys and known as 'the faraway mountain.' Its first ascent in the depths of winter is remembered as a grueling 17-hour round-trip struggle.
360.description_en_short: The Queen of the Southern Hidaka, once called the faraway mountain, cradling three cirques.

361.description_ja_long: 白山から北へ延びる県境稜線上の山で標高1,736m。飛騨岩、越中岩、加賀岩の三つの大岩壁に囲まれることが名の由来で、行者窟の伝承も残る。
361.description_ja_short: 三方を大岩壁に囲まれることが名の由来。白山北方の山。
361.description_en_long: Sanpoiwadake, a 1,736 m peak on the prefectural-border ridge running north from Hakusan, takes its name from the three great rock walls that surround it—Hida-iwa, Etchu-iwa and Kaga-iwa—and also preserves lore of the Gyojakutsu ascetics' cave.
361.description_en_short: Named for the great rock walls enclosing it on three sides; a peak on the ridge north of Hakusan.

362.description_ja_long: 富士五湖・西湖の北に立つ御坂山塊の高峰。富士山展望の山として知られ、古い峠道の通う十二ヶ岳への縦走路は変化に富んだ登りごたえで名高い。
362.description_ja_short: 西湖の北に構える御坂山塊の峰。富士山の展望で名高い。
362.description_en_long: Settogatake is a high peak of the Misaka Mountains rising north of Lake Saiko, one of the Fuji Five Lakes. Known as a mountain for viewing Mt. Fuji, it is also famed for the varied, rewarding traverse to Junigatake along ridges threaded by old pass trails.
362.description_en_short: A Misaka Mountains peak standing north of Lake Saiko, famed for its views of Mt. Fuji.

363.description_ja_long: 中国地方の最高峰で『出雲風土記』に火神岳と記された古き霊峰。伯耆富士とも呼ばれ、山頂部には特別天然記念物ダイセンキャラボクの純林が茂る。
363.description_ja_short: 伯耆富士とも呼ばれる中国地方の最高峰。由緒ある霊峰。
363.description_en_long: Daisen, the highest peak of the Chugoku region, is an ancient sacred mountain recorded as Hinokamidake in the Izumo Fudoki chronicle. Also called Hoki Fuji, it carries on its summit a pure stand of Daisen kyaraboku yew, a Special Natural Monument.
363.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Chugoku region, also called Hoki Fuji; a sacred mountain of long lineage.

364.description_ja_long: 福島県中部、安達太良連峰の最高峰で標高1,728m。主峰安達太良山の陰で知名度こそ控えめだが、頂に立てば素晴らしい展望が広がる静かな山。
364.description_ja_short: 安達太良連峰の最高峰。静かな頂に素晴らしい展望が広がる。
364.description_en_long: Minowayama, at 1,728 m the highest point of the Adatara range in central Fukushima, remains modest in fame in the shadow of the main peak Adatarayama, yet this quiet mountain rewards those who stand on its summit with a superb spreading panorama.
364.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Adatara range; a quiet summit opening onto a superb panorama.

365.description_ja_long: 五箇山と白川郷の境に立つ泰澄開山と伝わる山。春には手をつなぎ合った姉妹の姿の雪形が斜面に現れ、親孝行な姉妹の伝説とともに仰がれてきた。
365.description_ja_short: 姉妹の雪形が春に現れる伝説の山。泰澄開山と伝わる。
365.description_en_long: Ningyozan stands on the border of Gokayama and Shirakawa-go and is said to have been opened by the monk Taicho. Each spring a snow figure of two sisters holding hands appears on its slopes, and the peak has long been revered with their legend of filial devotion.
365.description_en_short: A mountain of legend where a snow figure of two sisters appears each spring; said to be opened by Taicho.

366.description_ja_long: 静岡・山梨県境、安倍川の東に連なる安倍東山稜の主要峰。上下二峰からなる双耳峰で、上十枚山の頂は南アルプス屈指の展望台として人気が高い。
366.description_ja_short: 安倍東山稜の双耳峰。南アルプスの大展望で人気が高い。
366.description_en_long: Jumaizan is a major peak of the Abe Higashi ridge running east of the Abekawa River on the Shizuoka-Yamanashi border. A twin-peaked mountain with upper and lower summits, its higher top, Kami-Jumaizan, is popular as a superb viewpoint of the Southern Alps.
366.description_en_short: Twin peaks on the Abe Higashi ridge, popular for grand views of the Southern Alps.

367.description_ja_long: 夕張山地の最高峰で標高1,726m。岩壁をめぐらせた男性的な山容は富良野から鋭く仰がれ、槍のように尖る頂と岩壁群が迫力ある景観をつくる。
367.description_ja_short: 夕張山地の最高峰。岩壁をまとった鋭く男性的な山容の山。
367.description_en_long: Ashibetsudake, at 1,726 m the highest peak of the Yubari Mountains, presents a rugged, masculine form ringed with rock walls that rises sharply when seen from Furano. Its spear-like pointed summit and surrounding crags create a scene of striking power.
367.description_en_short: Highest of the Yubari Mountains, a sharp, rugged peak clad in rock walls.

368.description_ja_long: 埼玉県秩父の鋸歯状の岩稜が際立つ日本百名山。イザナギ・イザナミ二神を祀ることが名の由来とされ、山中には狼の石像や霊神碑が信仰を伝える。
368.description_ja_short: 鋸歯状の岩稜が連なる秩父の霊峰。二神を祀る名を持つ。
368.description_en_long: Ryokamisan, in Chichibu, Saitama, is one of Japan's 100 Famous Mountains, marked by a striking saw-toothed rocky ridge. Its name is said to derive from enshrining the deities Izanagi and Izanami, and stone wolf statues and spirit monuments convey its faith.
368.description_en_short: A sacred Chichibu peak of saw-toothed rocky ridges, bearing a name that enshrines two deities.

369.description_ja_long: 日本海に浮かぶ利尻島がまるごと一つの頂となった標高1,721mの独立峰。アイヌ語で「高い島山」を意味し、古くは航海や豊漁を祈る海からの信仰を集めた。
369.description_ja_short: 島全体が一峰をなす最北の秀峰。海から仰がれた利尻富士。
369.description_en_long: Rishirizan is a 1,721 m independent peak formed by the whole of Rishiri Island rising from the Sea of Japan. Its name means 'high island mountain' in Ainu, and from early times it drew worship from the sea, where people prayed for safe voyages and good catches.
369.description_en_short: The northernmost graceful peak forming an entire island: Rishiri Fuji, long revered from the sea.

370.description_ja_long: 熊本・宮崎県境にそびえる九州山地南部の主峰で標高1,721m。山麓の市房神社へは樹齢七百年を超すという杉の巨木の参道が続き、御岳参りで賑わった信仰の山。
370.description_ja_short: 九州山地南部の主峰。杉の巨木の参道が続く信仰の山。
370.description_en_long: Ichifusayama, a 1,721 m peak on the Kumamoto-Miyazaki border, is the principal summit of the southern Kyushu Mountains. The approach to Ichifusa Shrine at its foot passes giant cedars said to be over 700 years old, on a peak of faith once busy with Ontake pilgrims.
370.description_en_short: Principal peak of the southern Kyushu Mountains; a mountain of faith approached through giant cedars.

371.description_ja_long: 谷川連峰の東、白毛門から続く稜線上の山。湯桧曽川を隔てて一ノ倉沢など谷川岳東面の大岩壁を望む格好の展望台で、朝日に輝く岩壁の眺めが名高い。
371.description_ja_short: 谷川岳東面の岩壁群を望む展望の山。朝日に映える稜線。
371.description_en_long: Asahidake lies east of the Tanigawa range on the ridge continuing from Shiragamon. Across the Yubisogawa River it is an ideal viewpoint for the great rock walls of Tanigawadake's east face, such as Ichinokurasawa, famed for the sight of them glowing in the morning sun.
371.description_en_short: A viewpoint peak facing the rock walls of Tanigawadake's east face; a ridge aglow in the morning sun.

372.description_ja_long: 奈良県天川村にある標高1,719mの霊峰。大峰信仰の中心で山頂に大峯山寺が建ち、西ノ覗などの行場が連なる。今なお女人禁制を守る修験道の聖地。
372.description_ja_short: 大峰修験の根本道場をなし、今も女人禁制を守る霊峰。
372.description_en_long: Sanjogatake, a 1,719 m sacred peak in Tenkawa Village, Nara, is the heart of Omine mountain worship. Ominesanji Temple stands on its summit amid a chain of ascetic training sites such as Nishi no Nozoki, and this Shugendo sanctuary still upholds its ban on women.
372.description_en_short: The core training ground of Omine Shugendo, a sacred peak that still upholds its ban on women.

373.description_ja_long: 雲取山から東へ延びる長沢背稜の最高峰で、埼玉・東京の都県境に立つ標高1,718mの山。秩父では黒ドッケと呼ばれたと古書に記される奥深い頂。
373.description_ja_short: 長沢背稜の最高峰。黒ドッケの別名を持つ奥深き静かな頂。
373.description_en_long: Toridaniyama, a 1,718 m mountain on the Saitama-Tokyo border, is the highest point of the Nagasawa ridge stretching east from Kumotoriyama. Old writings record that in Chichibu it was called Kurodokke, and its summit remains deep and far from the trailheads.
373.description_en_short: Highest point of the Nagasawa ridge; a deep, quiet summit also known by the name Kurodokke.

374.description_ja_long: 柳沢峠の東に連なる大菩薩連嶺の山。麓から仰ぐ岩峰が鶏のとさかに見えることが名の由来という。一帯は黒川千軒と呼ばれた甲州金の産地だった。
374.description_ja_short: とさかに似た岩峰が名の由来。甲州金の里を見下ろす。
374.description_en_long: Keikanzan, also called Kurokawayama, is a peak of the Daibosatsu range east of Yanagisawa Pass. Its name is said to come from its rocky summit resembling a rooster's comb when seen from below, and the area was once Kurokawa Senken, a source of Koshu gold.
374.description_en_short: Named for a rock peak shaped like a rooster's comb, looking down on an old Koshu gold country.

375.description_ja_long: 徳島県三好市、剣山の北西に横たわる標高1,713mの山。なだらかな稜線と山頂一帯の笹原が広がり、剣山地の山々の展望が楽しめる。塔ノ丸とも記される。
375.description_ja_short: 剣山の北西に延びる笹原の山。おおらかな稜線が魅力。
375.description_en_long: Tonomaru is a 1,713 m mountain in Miyoshi, Tokushima, lying northwest of Tsurugisan. Gentle ridgelines and fields of sasa bamboo grass spread across its summit area, offering fine views of the peaks of the Tsurugi Range. The name is also written To-no-maru.
375.description_en_short: A sasa-grass mountain stretching northwest of Tsurugisan, charming for its easy, open ridgeline.

376.description_ja_long: 山梨市の北にある奥秩父前衛の山で標高1,713m。山頂部の錫杖ヶ原にはレンゲツツジが咲き、夢想国師修行の伝説が残る。富士山や甲府盆地の眺めもよい。
376.description_ja_short: 錫杖ヶ原にツツジ咲く奥秩父前衛の山。富士の展望台。
376.description_en_long: Konarayama, a 1,713 m outlier of Oku-Chichibu north of Yamanashi City, blooms with renge azaleas at Shakujogahara near its summit and keeps a legend that the Zen master Muso Kokushi trained here. It also offers fine views of Mt. Fuji and the Kofu Basin.
376.description_en_short: An Oku-Chichibu outlier where azaleas bloom on Shakujogahara; a fine viewpoint for Mt. Fuji.

377.description_ja_long: 剣山の北に丸い山容をもたげる標高1,712mの峰。名の通り山頂一帯は一面の笹に覆われ、広々とした頂からは剣山をはじめ周囲の山々を一望できる。
377.description_ja_short: 一面の笹に覆われた丸い頂から剣山を間近に望む展望の山。
377.description_en_long: This 1,712 m peak raises its rounded form north of Tsurugisan. True to its name, the summit area is covered in an unbroken carpet of sasa bamboo grass, and from the broad, open top one can look out over Tsurugisan and the surrounding mountains in a sweep.
377.description_en_short: A round summit blanketed in sasa grass, a viewpoint looking straight across to nearby Tsurugisan.

378.description_ja_long: 白山連峰の南端に位置する岐阜県の山で標高1,709m。養老元年に泰澄が大日如来を山頂に祀って開いたと伝わり、山名もこれに由来する。白山の展望が見事。
378.description_ja_short: 泰澄開山と伝わる白山連峰南端の峰。頂から白山を一望。
378.description_en_long: Dainichigatake, a 1,709 m mountain in Gifu at the southern end of the Hakusan Range, is said to have been opened in the first year of the Yoro era, when the monk Taicho enshrined Dainichi Nyorai on the summit—the origin of its name. The view of Hakusan is superb.
378.description_en_short: A peak at the southern end of the Hakusan Range said to be opened by Taicho; Hakusan in full view.

380.description_ja_long: 徳島・高知県境にそびえる石灰岩の峻峰で標高1,708m。徳島で最も険しい山とされるが植物は豊かで、頂には四国に珍しいダケカンバの純林が広がる。
380.description_ja_short: 石灰岩の険しい峰。頂に四国では珍しいダケカンバの純林。
380.description_en_long: Ishidateyama, a precipitous 1,708 m limestone peak towering on the Tokushima-Kochi border, is held to be the most rugged mountain in Tokushima. Yet its plant life is rich, and a pure stand of dakekanba birch, a rarity in Shikoku, spreads across the summit.
380.description_en_short: A steep limestone peak crowned by a pure stand of dakekanba birch, a rarity in Shikoku.

382.description_ja_long: 愛媛県東部・赤石山系の最高峰。鉄分が酸化して赤く見える橄欖岩の岩峰で、固有種オトメシャジンなど四国随一の高山植物が岩場を彩る。標高1,706m。
382.description_ja_short: 赤い橄欖岩の岩峰に四国随一の高山植物が咲く最高峰。
382.description_en_long: Higashiakaishiyama is the highest peak of the Akaishi range in eastern Ehime. Its crags of peridotite, tinted red by oxidized iron, are adorned with Shikoku's finest alpine flora, including the endemic Otome-shajin bellflower. It stands 1,706 m.
382.description_en_short: A red peridotite rock peak of 1,706 m, where Shikoku's finest alpine flowers bloom.

383.description_ja_long: 蔵王連峰の南端に大きく張り出した三角錐の峻峰で標高1,705m。古くは御前岳と呼ばれたと伝わり、樹木のない山頂からは高度感あふれる展望が得られる。
383.description_ja_short: 蔵王連峰の南端に立つ三角錐の峰。裾野の美しい展望の頂。
383.description_en_long: This 1,705 m mountain is a steep triangular peak jutting out boldly at the southern end of the Zao range. Tradition holds that it was once called Gozendake, and its treeless summit offers an exhilarating panorama with a strong sense of height.
383.description_en_short: A triangular peak at the southern end of the Zao range; a summit of fine views over graceful skirts.

384.description_ja_long: 韮崎の北に裾野を広げる標高1,704mの山。八ヶ岳に似た姿から「ニセ八ツ」の異名を持ち、『日本百名山』の著者深田久弥が山行中に没した山として知られる。
384.description_ja_short: 八ヶ岳に似た姿の「ニセ八ツ」。深田久弥終焉の山と知られる。
384.description_en_long: Kayagatake, a 1,704 m mountain spreading its skirts north of Nirasaki, so resembles Yatsugatake that it is nicknamed 'Nise-Yatsu,' the false Yatsu. It is also known as the mountain where Kyuya Fukada, author of One Hundred Mountains of Japan, died while climbing.
384.description_en_short: 'Nise-Yatsu,' the Yatsugatake look-alike, known as the mountain where Kyuya Fukada met his end.

385.description_ja_long: 福島県中部に横たわる活火山で標高1,700m。高村光太郎『智恵子抄』の「ほんとの空」の山として親しまれ、荒々しい火口の沼ノ平がシンボルとなっている。
385.description_ja_short: 「ほんとの空」の山。沼ノ平の火口が刻む福島の名峰。
385.description_en_long: Adatarayama is a 1,700 m active volcano lying across central Fukushima. Cherished as the mountain of the 'true sky' in Kotaro Takamura's poetry collection Chieko-sho, it bears as its symbol the wild volcanic crater basin of Numanotaira.
385.description_en_short: The mountain of the 'true sky'; a famous Fukushima peak marked by the Numanotaira crater.

386.description_ja_long: 宮崎・鹿児島県境に連なる霧島火山群の最高峰は韓国岳で標高1,700m。遠く韓の国まで見渡せるほどの展望が名の由来と伝わり、大きな火口を頂に抱く。
386.description_ja_short: 韓国岳を最高峰とする火山群。頂に大きな火口を抱く。
386.description_en_long: Kirishimayama is a chain of volcanoes straddling the Miyazaki-Kagoshima border, crowned by Karakunidake at 1,700 m. The name is said to come from a view said to reach as far as the old country of Kara, Korea, and the highest summit holds a great crater.
386.description_en_short: A volcano group whose highest peak is Karakunidake, holding a great crater at its summit.

387.description_ja_long: 台高山脈南部の山で最高点は日出ヶ岳、標高1,695m。日本有数の多雨が深い森を育み、断崖の大蛇嵓や立ち枯れの正木ヶ原など独特の景観で知られる。
387.description_ja_short: 多雨が育む深い森と大蛇嵓の断崖で知られる三重県の最高峰。
387.description_en_long: A mountain in the southern Daiko Range whose highest point, Hidegatake, rises to 1,695 m. Some of Japan's heaviest rainfall nurtures its deep forests, and it is known for singular scenery such as the Daijagura cliff and the dead standing trees of Masakigahara.
387.description_en_short: The highest peak of Mie Prefecture, known for deep rain-fed forests and the sheer cliff of Daijagura.

388.description_ja_long: 白山から南へ延びる稜線上、福井・岐阜県境の山で標高1,691m。登山道はなく残雪期に登られる静かな峰で、西の台地にはブナ原生林と刈込池が横たわる。
388.description_ja_short: 白山から南に延びる道なき静峰。ブナの森と刈込池を抱く。
388.description_en_long: A 1,691 m mountain on the ridge running south from Hakusan, on the Fukui-Gifu border. With no trail, this quiet peak is climbed in the lingering-snow season, and on the plateau to its west lie a virgin beech forest and Karikomi Pond.
388.description_en_short: A quiet, trailless peak on the ridge running south from Hakusan, sheltering a beech forest and Karikomi Pond.

389.description_ja_long: 宮崎県五ヶ瀬町と熊本県の境に立つ標高1,685mの山。北東斜面には日本最南端として知られるスキー場が広がり、九州の山では珍しく雪と縁の深い峰である。
389.description_ja_short: 日本最南端のスキー場を斜面に抱く、九州の県境に立つ山。
389.description_en_long: A 1,685 m mountain standing on the border of Gokase, Miyazaki Prefecture, and Kumamoto. Its northeastern slopes hold a ski area known as Japan's southernmost, making it a peak with an unusually close tie to snow for a mountain of Kyushu.
389.description_en_short: A mountain on a Kyushu prefectural border, holding Japan's southernmost ski area on its slopes.

390.description_ja_long: 道志山塊の最高峰で標高1,681m。江戸期には僧妙心が山中で入定した信仰の山で、御正体山のほか御祖代山など数多くの異字表記が古文献に残る。
390.description_ja_short: 道志山塊の最高峰。妙心上人入定の伝えが残る信仰の山。
390.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Doshi Mountains at 1,681 m. A mountain of faith where, in the Edo period, the monk Myoshin entered his final meditation on its slopes; old documents preserve many variant written forms of the name besides Mishotaiyama.
390.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Doshi Mountains, a mountain of faith keeping the tale of the monk Myoshin's final meditation.

391.description_ja_long: 伊那谷と南アルプスの間に横たわる伊那山地の山で標高1,681m。北から仰ぐ姿が富士に似て伊那富士と呼ばれ、東西二つの頂から仙丈ヶ岳や木曽駒ヶ岳を望む。
391.description_ja_short: 伊那富士と呼ばれる双耳の頂。仙丈ヶ岳を間近に望む。
391.description_en_long: A 1,681 m mountain of the Ina Range, which lies between the Ina Valley and the Southern Alps. Seen from the north it resembles Mt. Fuji, earning the name Ina-Fuji, and its twin east and west summits look out on Senjogatake and Kisokomagatake.
391.description_en_short: A twin-summited peak known as Ina-Fuji, with close views of Senjogatake.

392.description_ja_long: 長野県南木曽町にある標高1,679mの山。御嶽山・木曽駒ヶ岳とともに木曽三岳に数えられた信仰の山で、花崗岩の巨石が乱立する頂に祠が祀られている。
392.description_ja_short: 木曽三岳に数えられた信仰の山。花崗岩の巨岩が連なる頂。
392.description_en_long: A 1,679 m mountain in the town of Nagiso, Nagano. Counted with Ontakesan and Kisokomagatake as one of the Three Peaks of Kiso, it is a mountain of faith, and a small shrine stands on its summit amid a jumble of huge granite boulders.
392.description_en_short: A mountain of faith counted among the Three Peaks of Kiso, its summit crowded with huge granite boulders.

393.description_ja_long: 上毛三山の一つで、標高1,674mの地蔵岳は赤城山の中央火口丘。頂から大沼と最高峰黒檜山を一望する。中世の地獄思想にちなむ名で、血ノ池など地名も残る。
393.description_ja_short: 大沼を見下ろす赤城山の中央火口丘。夏は花々が咲き競う頂。
393.description_en_long: One of the Three Mountains of Jomo. The 1,674 m Jizodake is the central cone of Mt. Akagi, and its summit overlooks Lake Onuma and Kurobisan, the highest peak. The name reflects medieval notions of hell, and place names such as Chinoike, the Blood Pond, remain.
393.description_en_short: The central cone of Mt. Akagi, looking down on Lake Onuma, with a summit where flowers bloom in profusion in summer.

394.description_ja_long: 岐阜・福井県境に立つ両白山地の山で標高1,674m。麓の石徹白は白山信仰の美濃側登山口として栄えた地。登山道はなく、残雪の尾根から静かな頂に至る。
394.description_ja_short: 白山信仰の里、石徹白から仰ぐ道なき残雪の峰。展望は雄大。
394.description_en_long: A 1,674 m mountain of the Ryohaku Mountains standing on the Gifu-Fukui border. Itoshiro at its foot flourished as the Mino-side gateway for Hakusan worship. There is no trail; climbers follow ridges of lingering snow to the quiet summit.
394.description_en_short: A trailless peak of lingering snow rising above Itoshiro, a village of Hakusan worship, with magnificent views.

395.description_ja_long: 丹沢山塊の最高峰で標高1,673mは神奈川県の最高点。山名には毘盧遮那仏を祀った「びるが岳」に由来する説がある。頂から富士山と幾重の山並みを望む。
395.description_ja_short: 神奈川県最高点の頂。富士を望み山並み幾重に連なる。
395.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Tanzawa massif; at 1,673 m it is also the highest point in Kanagawa Prefecture. One theory traces the name to 'Birugatake,' a peak that enshrined the Vairocana Buddha. The summit looks out on Mt. Fuji and ridge upon ridge of mountains.
395.description_en_short: The highest point in Kanagawa, its summit facing Mt. Fuji across layer upon layer of ridgelines.

396.description_ja_long: 岐阜県郡上市と高山市にまたがる飛騨高地の山で標高1,671m。古くは雲ヶ岳と呼ばれ、頂近くに棲む大鷲を退治して献上したという伝説が名の由来と伝わる。
396.description_ja_short: 大鷲退治の伝説が名の由来と伝わる、飛騨高地の静かな山。
396.description_en_long: A 1,671 m mountain of the Hida Highlands straddling the cities of Gujo and Takayama in Gifu. Once called Kumogatake, it is said to owe its present name to a legend that a giant eagle living near the summit was slain and presented to the lord.
396.description_en_short: A quiet mountain of the Hida Highlands, named, tradition says, for the legend of a slain giant eagle.

397.description_ja_long: 北海道、夕張山地南端の標高1,668mの山。岩の芦別岳と対をなす「花の山」と称され、ユウバリソウなど固有種を含む高山植物の宝庫として名高い。
397.description_ja_short: 固有種の花咲く夕張山地の名峰。かつて神の山と畏れられた。
397.description_en_long: A 1,668 m mountain at the southern end of the Yubari Mountains in Hokkaido. Called 'the mountain of flowers' in contrast to rocky Ashibetsudake, it is famed as a treasury of alpine plants, including endemic species such as yubarisou.
397.description_en_short: A famed flower peak of the Yubari Mountains with endemic alpine plants, once feared as a mountain of the gods.

398.description_ja_long: 世界自然遺産・知床半島の最高峰で標高1,661m。火山群の主峰らしい岩積みの頂からは、オホーツク海や国後島までも一望できる。
398.description_ja_short: 世界遺産・知床半島の最高峰。頂から国後島までも望む火山。
398.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Shiretoko Peninsula, a World Natural Heritage site, at 1,661 m. From its rock-piled summit, befitting the main peak of a volcanic group, the view sweeps across the Sea of Okhotsk and even as far as Kunashiri Island.
398.description_en_short: The highest peak of World Heritage Shiretoko, a volcano whose summit view reaches as far as Kunashiri Island.

399.description_ja_long: 浅間高原の東を限る尾根上の一峰で、曲がった鼻に似た山容がひと目でそれと知れる。日本武尊が水源を見出したと伝わる烏川の伝説が残る。
399.description_ja_short: 曲がった鼻に似た山容の峰。日本武尊の水の伝説を宿す。
399.description_en_long: A peak on the ridge bounding the eastern edge of the Asama highlands, instantly recognizable by a profile that resembles a bent nose. It preserves the legend of the Karasu River, whose source Yamato Takeru is said to have discovered.
399.description_en_short: A peak shaped like a bent nose, keeping a legend of Yamato Takeru and the waters he found.

400.description_ja_long: 信州最南端の遠山郷の山で標高1,654m。中央構造線がつくった大崩壊地・青崩峠を東に従え、一等三角点の頂から南アルプス深南部の山々を望む。
400.description_ja_short: 青崩峠を従える信州南端の山。頂から南ア深南部を一望する。
400.description_en_long: A 1,654 m mountain of Toyama-go at the southernmost tip of Shinshu. To its east lies Aokuzure Pass, a great collapse zone created by the Median Tectonic Line, and its first-order triangulation summit looks out on the deep southern reaches of the Southern Alps.
400.description_en_short: A mountain at Shinshu's southern edge attended by Aokuzure Pass, overlooking the deep south of the Southern Alps.

401.description_ja_long: 諏訪湖を望む標高1,651mの山。旧名を森山と伝え、東西二つの頂からは八ヶ岳を正面に、北・中央・南アルプスまで一望できる。
401.description_ja_short: 諏訪湖を望み、アルプスと八ヶ岳を一望する好展望の山。
401.description_en_long: A 1,651 m mountain overlooking Lake Suwa, said to have once been called Moriyama. From its twin east and west summits, Yatsugatake rises straight ahead, and the panorama reaches the Northern, Central, and Southern Alps as well.
401.description_en_short: A mountain of fine views above Lake Suwa, taking in the Alps and Yatsugatake in a single sweep.

402.description_ja_long: 富山県南部、飛騨との境に座る標高1,650mの山。前・中・奥金剛の三峰からなり、風衝草原の頂に藩主前田利保の歌碑が立つ古くからの信仰の山。
402.description_ja_short: 三つの金剛を連ね、草原の頂をもつ越中の信仰の名山。
402.description_en_long: A 1,650 m mountain seated in southern Toyama on the border with Hida. Made up of the three peaks of Mae-, Naka-, and Oku-Kongo, it is an old mountain of faith, and a poem monument to the domain lord Maeda Toshiyasu stands on its wind-swept grassland summit.
402.description_en_short: A noted mountain of faith in Etchu, linking its three Kongo peaks and crowned by a grassland summit.

403.description_ja_long: 平家落人伝説の里・五家荘の南に横たわる熊本の秘峰。台形の目立たぬ山ながら、麓のフクジュソウや頂のシャクナゲなど花の彩りに恵まれる。
403.description_ja_short: 五家荘の南に潜む秘峰。フクジュソウやシャクナゲが彩る。
403.description_en_long: A hidden peak of Kumamoto lying south of Gokanosho, a village steeped in legends of Heike refugees. Though an unassuming, table-shaped mountain, it is blessed with flowers, from fukujuso blooms at its foot to rhododendrons on the summit.
403.description_en_short: A secluded peak hidden south of Gokanosho, colored by fukujuso blooms and rhododendrons.

404.description_ja_long: 早池峰山と向かい合うピラミッド形の秀峰で標高1,645m。古くは前薬師とも呼ばれ、頂からは早池峰の雄姿や遠野の山並みの大観が広がる。
404.description_ja_short: 早池峰山と対峙する端正な三角峰。前薬師とも呼ばれた。
404.description_en_long: A shapely pyramid-shaped peak of 1,645 m facing Hayachinesan. In older times it was also called Maeyakushi, and from its summit a grand view unfolds of Hayachine's imposing form and the mountain ranges of Tono.
404.description_en_short: A trim triangular peak standing face to face with Hayachinesan, once also called Maeyakushi.

405.description_ja_long: 宮崎県延岡市、祝子川源流にそびえる山。遠望すると大きく崩れて見える姿が名の由来とされ、花崗岩の岩峰群と原生林が九州最後の秘境をなす。
405.description_ja_short: 花崗岩の岩峰群と原生林が広がる九州最後の秘境の山。
405.description_en_long: A mountain towering at the headwaters of the Horigawa River in Nobeoka, Miyazaki. Its name is said to come from its greatly crumbled look when seen from afar, and its granite rock spires and virgin forest form the last unspoiled wilderness of Kyushu.
405.description_en_short: A mountain of granite rock spires and virgin forest, forming the last hidden wilderness of Kyushu.

407.description_ja_long: 男女岳を最高点に男岳、女岳などを束ねる秋田県境の活火山。名は春の山肌に浮かぶ駒形の雪形にちなむといい、高山植物の宝庫として名高い。
407.description_ja_short: 駒形の雪形に名を負う活火山。コマクサ咲く花の名山。
407.description_en_long: An active volcano on the Akita border gathering several summits, with Onamedake as its highest point alongside Odake and Medake. The name is said to derive from a horse-shaped snow patch appearing on its spring slopes, and it is famed as a treasury of alpine plants.
407.description_en_short: An active volcano named for a horse-shaped snow patch, a celebrated flower mountain where komakusa bloom.

408.description_ja_long: 南会津にその名の通り七つの峰を連ねる山で標高1,636m。山鬼が大石を転がすという伝説が残り、一等三角点の頂は四方の展望に恵まれる。
408.description_ja_short: 七つの峰を連ねる南会津の山。山鬼の伝説が残る展望の頂。
408.description_en_long: A 1,636 m mountain in South Aizu linking, true to its name, a chain of seven peaks. A legend tells of a mountain demon rolling great boulders here, and its first-order triangulation summit is blessed with views in every direction.
408.description_en_short: A South Aizu mountain of seven linked peaks, keeping a demon legend and a summit with all-round views.

409.description_ja_long: 東大雪、クマネシリ山群の一峰で標高1,635m。十勝三股からピリベツ岳と双耳峰のように並び、オッパイ山の愛称で親しまれる展望の山。
409.description_ja_short: ピリベツ岳と双耳をなし、オッパイ山と親しまれる東大雪の峰。
409.description_en_long: A 1,635 m peak of the Kumaneshiri group in the East Daisetsu region. Seen from Tokachimitsumata it stands beside Piribetsudake like a pair of twin summits, a mountain of fine views affectionately nicknamed Oppai-yama.
409.description_en_short: An East Daisetsu peak forming twin summits with Piribetsudake, fondly nicknamed Oppai-yama.

410.description_ja_long: 祖母・傾縦走路の中ほど、大分・宮崎県境に座るおだやかな山。豊玉姫がまずこの峰に降り立ち、のちに祖母山へ移ったという伝説が名の由来と伝わる。
410.description_ja_short: 豊玉姫降臨の伝説に名を負う、祖母・傾縦走路の穏やかな峰。
410.description_en_long: A gentle mountain seated on the Oita-Miyazaki border midway along the Sobo-Katamuki traverse. Its name is said to derive from a legend that the goddess Toyotamahime first alighted on this peak and later moved on to Sobosan.
410.description_en_short: A gentle peak on the Sobo-Katamuki traverse, named for the legend of Toyotamahime's descent.

411.description_ja_long: 南日高三山の一つで標高1,631m。名はアイヌ語で「美しい山」を意味し、鞍部から頂まで300mの高度差は日高随一。登山道をもたない優美な鋭峰。
411.description_ja_short: アイヌ語で「美しい山」。道なき南日高の優美な鋭峰。
411.description_en_long: One of the three great peaks of the southern Hidaka Range at 1,631 m. The name means 'beautiful mountain' in Ainu, and the 300 m rise from the saddle to the top is the greatest in the Hidaka. A graceful, sharp peak with no climbing trail.
411.description_en_short: 'Beautiful mountain' in Ainu — a graceful, trailless sharp peak of the southern Hidaka Range.

413.description_ja_long: 加越国境、白山前衛に横たわる標高1,629mの山。兎のようななだらかな姿が名の由来とされ、山頂東の湿原に夏、ニッコウキスゲが咲き競う。
413.description_ja_short: 兎のように穏やかな加越国境の山。湿原の花と白山の眺め。
413.description_en_long: A 1,629 m mountain lying on the old Kaga-Echizen border among the outlying peaks of Hakusan. Its gentle, rabbit-like form is said to be the source of the name, and in summer day lilies bloom in profusion in the wetland east of the summit.
413.description_en_short: A mountain as gentle as a rabbit on the Kaga-Echizen border, with wetland flowers and views of Hakusan.

414.description_ja_long: 南アルプス深南部にひっそりと立つ静かな山。清楚なシロヤシオの名所として知られ、数年に一度どっと花をつける年はことのほか美しい。
414.description_ja_short: シロヤシオの花に彩られる南アルプス深南部の静かな山。
414.description_en_long: Sobatsubuyama is a quiet mountain standing in seclusion in the deep southern reaches of the Southern Alps. Known as a noted spot for graceful shiroyashio azaleas, it is exceptionally beautiful in the years, once every few, when the flowers bloom in profusion.
414.description_en_short: A quiet peak deep in the southern Southern Alps, adorned with shiroyashio azalea blossoms.

415.description_ja_long: 奥羽山脈中部、岩手・宮城県境に頂をおく古い火山で標高1,626m。初夏に現れる馬の雪形が名の由来とされ、全国屈指と讃えられる紅葉で名高い。
415.description_ja_short: 馬の雪形に名を負う奥羽の火山。全国屈指の紅葉に染まる。
415.description_en_long: Kurikomayama is an old 1,626 m volcano on the Iwate-Miyagi border in the central Ou Mountains. Its name is said to come from a horse-shaped snow patch that appears in early summer, and it is famed for autumn foliage praised as among Japan's finest.
415.description_en_short: An Ou volcano named for a horse-shaped snow patch, renowned for some of Japan's finest autumn colors.

416.description_ja_long: 円錐形の姿から津軽富士と呼ばれる青森県の最高峰で標高1,625m。岩木山神社の「お山参詣」に象徴される、津軽の農民の信仰を集めてきた霊峰。
416.description_ja_short: 津軽富士と呼ばれる青森の最高峰。お山参詣の信仰を集める。
416.description_en_long: Iwakisan, at 1,625 m the highest peak in Aomori, is called Tsugaru Fuji for its conical form. It is a sacred mountain that has long drawn the faith of Tsugaru's farmers, symbolized by the Oyama-sankei pilgrimage of Iwakiyama Shrine.
416.description_en_short: Aomori's highest peak, known as Tsugaru Fuji, revered through the Oyama-sankei pilgrimage.

417.description_ja_long: 位山の南西に連なる飛騨の山で標高1,625m。位山の男神をめぐり船山の女神と競い、勝って妻となった女神の山とする伝説が残る展望の頂。
417.description_ja_short: 女神の伝説を宿す飛騨の山。頂から御嶽や北アルプスを一望。
417.description_en_long: Kaoredake is a 1,625 m Hida mountain trailing southwest of Kuraiyama. Legend holds it is the mountain of a goddess who competed with the goddess of Funayama for the male god of Kuraiyama, won, and became his wife; the summit commands fine views.
417.description_en_short: A Hida mountain bearing a goddess legend, with sweeping summit views of Ontake and the Northern Alps.

418.description_ja_long: 大野盆地の北東に立つ火山で、県境の山を除けば福井県内の最高峰。兵火に焼かれた平泉寺の古経を山に埋めたことが名の由来と伝えられる。
418.description_ja_short: 古経を埋めた伝承に名を負う火山。火口跡に湿原を抱く。
418.description_en_long: Kyogadake is a volcano rising northeast of the Ono Basin, the highest peak within Fukui Prefecture apart from mountains on its borders. Its name is said to derive from old sutras of Heisenji temple, burned in war, that were buried on the mountain.
418.description_en_short: A volcano named for buried sutras of a war-torn temple, cradling a marsh in its old crater.

419.description_ja_long: 奥只見、只見町の山深くにそびえる峻険な峰で標高1,624m。朝日が最初に照らす頂という名をもち、山腹には手つかずのブナ原生林が広がる。
419.description_ja_short: 朝日が最初に照らす頂。ブナ原生林を抱く奥只見の峻峰。
419.description_en_long: Asahidake is a steep, rugged 1,624 m peak rising deep in the mountains of Tadami in the Okutadami region. Its name means the summit first lit by the morning sun, and untouched virgin beech forest spreads across its slopes.
419.description_en_short: The peak first lit by the morning sun, a rugged Okutadami mountain cloaked in virgin beech forest.

420.description_ja_long: 南アルプス深南部、気田川源流に笹の山頂をもたげる標高1,621mの山。名は麓の京丸集落から仰いだ高い山の意と伝わり、林道開通まで秘境だった。
420.description_ja_short: 笹に覆われた頂が静かな深南部の山。かつては秘境だった。
420.description_en_long: Takatsukayama is a 1,621 m mountain lifting its bamboo-grass summit at the headwaters of the Ketagawa in the deep southern Southern Alps. Its name is said to mean the tall peak seen from the Kyomaru hamlet below, a remote spot until a forest road opened.
420.description_en_short: A quiet deep-south peak with a summit covered in bamboo grass, once an untrodden hinterland.

421.description_ja_long: 岐阜・福井県境に座る越美山地の最高峰で標高1,617m。白山の開祖泰澄が開いたと伝わる信仰の山で、両白山地の名は白山とこの山に由来する。
421.description_ja_short: 白山と対をなす越美山地の最高峰。泰澄開山の伝承をもつ。
421.description_en_long: Nogohakusan, 1,617 m, is the highest peak of the Etsumi Mountains on the Gifu-Fukui border. A mountain of worship said to have been opened by Taicho, founder of Hakusan worship, it and Hakusan together gave the Ryohaku Mountains their name.
421.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Etsumi Mountains, paired with Hakusan and said to have been opened by Taicho.

422.description_ja_long: 岩手県北西部、秋田との境に広がる高原状の山で標高1,613m。オオシラビソの樹海に湿原と池塘が点在し、名は坂上田村麻呂の命名説など諸説ある。
422.description_ja_short: 樹海に湿原と池塘をちりばめた、たおやかな高原状の名山。
422.description_en_long: Hachimantai is a 1,613 m plateau-like mountain spreading along the Akita border in northwestern Iwate. Marshes and small ponds dot its sea of Maries' fir forest, and among several theories its name is ascribed to the general Sakanoue no Tamuramaro.
422.description_en_short: A gentle plateau-like mountain, its sea of forest sprinkled with marshes and small ponds.

423.description_ja_long: 熊本と宮崎の県境に横たわる標高1,607mの山で、津野岳の名でも呼ばれる。山頂には一等三角点が置かれ、みやざき百山にも数えられている。
423.description_ja_short: 津野岳とも呼ばれる熊本・宮崎県境の山。頂に一等三角点。
423.description_en_long: Eshiroyama is a 1,607 m mountain lying on the border of Kumamoto and Miyazaki, also known by the name Tsunodake. A first-order triangulation point stands on its summit, and it is counted among the 100 Mountains of Miyazaki.
423.description_en_short: A Kumamoto-Miyazaki border mountain also called Tsunodake, with a first-order triangulation point on top.

424.description_ja_long: 大分・宮崎県境にそびえる祖母・傾山地東の雄、標高1,605m。北から仰ぐ頂が斜めに傾いて見えることが名の由来で、岩峰群を連ねた山容を誇る。
424.description_ja_short: 頂の傾きが名になった祖母・傾山地東の雄。岩峰を連ねる。
424.description_en_long: Katamukiyama, 1,605 m, is the eastern champion of the Sobo-Katamuki Mountains on the Oita-Miyazaki border. Its name comes from the way the summit appears to lean when viewed from the north, and it boasts a skyline of clustered rocky spires.
424.description_en_short: The eastern giant of the Sobo-Katamuki range, named for its leaning summit and lined with rock pinnacles.

425.description_ja_long: 日高山脈南部にどっしり構える岩壁の山で標高1,600m。名はアイヌ語の「神の山」に由来し、南日高三山の中でも風格は抜きん出ているといわれる。
425.description_ja_short: 「神の山」の名を持つ南日高の雄。岩壁が際立つ風格の峰。
425.description_en_long: Kamuidake is a massive 1,600 m mountain of rock walls standing solidly in the southern Hidaka Mountains. Its name comes from the Ainu for 'mountain of the gods,' and its presence is said to stand out even among the three great peaks of southern Hidaka.
425.description_en_short: A southern Hidaka giant named 'mountain of the gods,' a stately peak defined by its rock walls.

426.description_ja_long: 新潟県南魚沼市と湯沢町の境に鋭く立つ谷川連峰の一峰で標高1,598m。角ばった山容から「上越のマッターホルン」と呼ばれ、頂は展望に恵まれる。
426.description_ja_short: 角ばった鋭鋒は「上越のマッターホルン」。展望の頂。
426.description_en_long: Daigentasan is a sharp 1,598 m peak of the Tanigawa Range on the border of Minamiuonuma and Yuzawa in Niigata. Its angular form has earned it the nickname 'Matterhorn of Joetsu,' and its summit is blessed with fine views.
426.description_en_short: A sharp, angular spire called the 'Matterhorn of Joetsu,' crowned by a summit rich in views.

427.description_ja_long: 富山・岐阜県境に横たわる標高1,596mのおおらかな山。頂上は池塘の点在する広い草原と高層湿原で、ニッコウキスゲの咲く頃はひときわ華やぐ。
427.description_ja_short: 頂に池塘と湿原が広がる県境の山。ニッコウキスゲの名所。
427.description_en_long: Shirakimine is a gentle 1,596 m mountain lying on the Toyama-Gifu border. Its summit is a broad grassland and high moor dotted with small ponds, and the scene is at its most festive when the Nikko day lilies come into bloom.
427.description_en_short: A border mountain whose summit spreads with ponds and moorland, famous for its day lilies.

428.description_ja_long: 山形・福島両県にまたがり、飯豊山地の周辺に位置する標高1,595mの山。名峰の連なる飯豊の懐にあって、ひっそりと静かにたたずむ一座である。
428.description_ja_short: 飯豊山地の周辺に静かにたたずむ山形・福島県境の一座。
428.description_en_long: Iimorisan is a 1,595 m mountain straddling Yamagata and Fukushima on the fringe of the Iide Mountains. Nestled in the bosom of Iide's chain of celebrated peaks, it stands quietly and unobtrusively, a serene presence apart from the crowds.
428.description_en_short: A quiet, secluded peak on the Yamagata-Fukushima border at the edge of the Iide Mountains.

429.description_ja_long: 白馬岳から日本海へ至る新潟・富山県境の稜線上でひときわ尖って見える標高1,592mの峰。昭和期に山岳会が拓いた栂海新道が頂を越えて延びる。
429.description_ja_short: 白馬岳と日本海を結ぶ栂海新道の途上に尖る県境の峰。
429.description_en_long: Inugatake is a 1,592 m peak that looks conspicuously sharp on the Niigata-Toyama border ridge running from Shiroumadake to the Sea of Japan. The Tsugami Shindo trail, cut by a mountaineering club in the Showa era, crosses over its summit.
429.description_en_short: A sharp border peak on the Tsugami Shindo trail linking Shiroumadake with the Sea of Japan.

430.description_ja_long: 熊本県にそびえる世界最大級のカルデラを抱く複式火山の総称。高岳・中岳など阿蘇五岳を連ね、最高点は高岳の標高1,592m。今も噴煙を上げ続ける。
430.description_ja_short: 世界最大級のカルデラを抱く活火山。阿蘇五岳の最高峰は高岳。
430.description_en_long: Mt. Aso is the collective name for a compound volcano in Kumamoto holding one of the world's largest calderas. Its five peaks, including Takadake and Nakadake, form the Aso Gogaku; the highest point is Takadake at 1,592 m, and it still smokes today.
430.description_en_short: An active volcano with one of the world's largest calderas; Takadake is the highest of the five Aso peaks.

431.description_ja_long: くじゅう連山の東端に横たわる黒岳の山塊で、最高点は高塚山の標高1,587m。落葉期の山肌の黒さが名の由来で、ブナやカエデの原生林が濃密に茂る。
431.description_ja_short: くじゅう東端の黒岳山塊。原生林の黒さが名になった。
431.description_en_long: This is the massif of Kurodake lying at the eastern end of the Kuju Mountains, its highest point Takatsukayama at 1,587 m. The name comes from the darkness of its slopes after the leaves fall, and dense virgin forests of beech and maple clothe it.
431.description_en_short: The Kurodake massif at Kuju's eastern end, named for the darkness of its virgin forests.

432.description_ja_long: 丹沢山塊北部、神奈川・山梨県境に立つ標高1,587mの山。富士を隠すほど堂々とした山容から「富士隠し」と呼ばれ、頂はブナの深い森に包まれる。
432.description_ja_short: 富士を隠すほどの偉容で「富士隠し」。ブナ茂る丹沢北の雄。
432.description_en_long: Omuroyama is a 1,587 m mountain in the northern Tanzawa massif on the Kanagawa-Yamanashi border. Its bulk is imposing enough to block Mt. Fuji from view, earning it the name 'Fuji-kakushi' (Fuji hider), and deep beech forest wraps its summit.
432.description_en_short: Called 'Fuji-kakushi' for hiding Mt. Fuji behind its bulk, a beech-clad giant of northern Tanzawa.

433.description_ja_long: 青森市の南にそびえる火山群の総称で、最高峰の大岳は標高1,585m。峰々の間に高層湿原が広がり、明治の雪中行軍遭難の物語と冬の樹氷でも知られる。
433.description_ja_short: 高層湿原と樹氷の火山群。明治の雪中行軍の悲話が残る。
433.description_en_long: Hakkodasan is the collective name for a volcanic group rising south of Aomori City; its highest peak, Odake, stands 1,585 m. High moors spread between the peaks, and the mountain is also known for a tragic Meiji-era snow march and its winter rime ice trees.
433.description_en_short: A volcanic group of high moors and frosted winter trees, remembered for a tragic Meiji snow march.

434.description_ja_long: 新潟と福島の県境に横たわる越後山脈の古い火山で標高1,585m。優美な曲線を描く頂に池塘が光り、鬼ヶ面山の断崖と田子倉湖の眺めが迫力を添える。
434.description_ja_short: 優美な稜線に池塘が光る越後の火山。眼下に田子倉湖。
434.description_en_long: Asakusadake is an old 1,585 m volcano of the Echigo Mountains lying on the Niigata-Fukushima border. Small ponds glisten on its gracefully curved summit, while the cliffs of Onigatsurayama and views of Lake Tagokura add drama to the scene.
434.description_en_short: An Echigo volcano with ponds gleaming on its graceful ridgeline, overlooking Lake Tagokura.

435.description_ja_long: 湯布院の盆地を見下ろして双耳の峰を突き上げる標高1,583mの活火山。古くから神のいます山と崇められ『万葉集』にも詠われた。愛称は「豊後富士」。
435.description_ja_short: 湯布院にそびえる双耳の霊峰。「豊後富士」と親しまれる。
435.description_en_long: Yufudake is a 1,583 m active volcano thrusting its twin peaks above the basin of Yufuin. Revered since antiquity as a mountain where gods dwell and sung of in the Man'yoshu anthology, it is affectionately known as 'Bungo Fuji.'
435.description_en_short: A twin-peaked sacred volcano towering over Yufuin, affectionately called 'Bungo Fuji.'

436.description_ja_long: 福島・栃木県境の帝釈山脈東部に立つ標高1,581mの双耳峰。福島側では荒海山、栃木側では太郎岳と呼び分けられ、二つの名を併せ持つことで知られる。
436.description_ja_short: 帝釈山脈の双耳峰。福島と栃木で異なる名で呼ばれる山。
436.description_en_long: Arakaizan (Tarodake) is a 1,581 m twin-peaked mountain in the eastern Taishaku Mountains on the Fukushima-Tochigi border. It is known for carrying two names at once, called Arakaizan (Tarodake) on the Fukushima side and Tarodake on the Tochigi side.
436.description_en_short: A twin-peaked mountain of the Taishaku range, called by different names in Fukushima and Tochigi.

437.description_ja_long: 八幡平から安比高原へ続く稜線上にあって、ひときわ目を引く円錐形の茶臼岳、標高1,578m。西麓の黒谷地湿原には池塘と高山の花が広がる。
437.description_ja_short: 八幡平の稜線に立つ円錐形の茶臼岳。山麓に湿原が広がる。
437.description_en_long: This is Chausudake, a strikingly conical 1,578 m peak on the ridgeline running from Hachimantai toward the Appi Highlands. At the Kuroyachi wetland on its western foot, small ponds and alpine flowers spread across the moor.
437.description_en_short: The conical Chausudake on the Hachimantai ridgeline, with wetlands spreading at its foot.

438.description_ja_long: 八幡平の頂稜の南に位置する秋田県の山で標高1,578m。なだらかな高原状の山並みの中で、ピラミッド型の整った姿がひときわ目を引く一座である。
438.description_ja_short: 八幡平の南にピラミッド型の姿がひときわ目立つ一座。
438.description_en_long: This 1,578 m mountain in Akita lies south of the summit ridge of Hachimantai. Amid the gently rolling, plateau-like ranges that surround it, its neatly formed pyramid shape makes it stand out conspicuously as a singular presence.
438.description_en_short: A peak south of Hachimantai whose neat pyramid form stands out among gentle plateau ridges.

439.description_ja_long: 霧島連峰の東に気高くそびえる高千穂峰、標高1,574m。天孫降臨神話の地とされ、頂には青銅の天逆鉾が立つ。西山腹に火口の御鉢を従えた成層火山。
439.description_ja_short: 天孫降臨神話の高千穂峰。頂に天逆鉾が突き立つ霊峰。
439.description_en_long: Takachihonomine rises nobly at 1,574 m in the east of the Kirishima Range. Held to be the site of the Tenson Korin myth of the gods' descent, its summit bears the bronze spear Amanosakahoko. The stratovolcano carries the Ohachi crater on its western flank.
439.description_en_short: Takachihonomine, peak of the heavenly descent myth, a sacred mountain crowned by the Amanosakahoko spear.

440.description_ja_long: 金沢の街から富士に似た姿を望める両白山地の峰で標高1,572m。「加賀富士」と呼ばれて室生犀星らの作品にも登場し、麓には五箇山の塩硝を運んだ峠道が通う。
440.description_ja_short: 金沢から仰ぐ「加賀富士」。文学作品にも描かれた名峰。
440.description_en_long: A 1,572 m peak of the Ryohaku Mountains whose Fuji-like form can be seen from the streets of Kanazawa. Called 'Kaga Fuji,' it appears in works by writers such as Muro Saisei, and an old pass road that carried saltpeter from Gokayama runs at its foot.
440.description_en_short: 'Kaga Fuji' admired from Kanazawa, a famed peak also depicted in works of literature.

441.description_ja_long: 丹沢山塊の中央に構える標高1,567mの峰。明治の三角測量で一等三角点が置かれ、山地全体の名「丹沢」を負うことになった。頂はブナの森に覆われる。
441.description_ja_short: 一等三角点が山地の名を定めた丹沢の中心峰。ブナの頂。
441.description_en_long: A 1,567 m peak standing at the center of the Tanzawa massif. A first-order triangulation point was set here in Meiji-era surveys, and the peak came to bear the name 'Tanzawa' for the whole range. Its summit is covered in beech forest.
441.description_en_short: Central peak of Tanzawa, whose first-order triangulation point gave the range its name. A beech-clad summit.

443.description_ja_long: 知床連峰で唯一の活火山で標高1,562m。純度の高い硫黄を噴き、江戸末期から採掘が行われた歴史を持つ。稜線には固有種シレトコスミレが可憐に咲く。
443.description_ja_short: 硫黄を噴く知床唯一の活火山。固有種シレトコスミレが咲く。
443.description_en_long: The only active volcano in the Shiretoko Range, 1,562 m high. It vents sulfur of high purity, which was mined from the late Edo period onward, and the endemic Shiretoko violet blooms daintily along its ridgeline.
443.description_en_short: Shiretoko's only active volcano, venting sulfur; home to the endemic Shiretoko violet.

444.description_ja_long: 四国カルスト北部、大川嶺連峰の最高峰で標高1,562m。強い北西風に笠を飛ばされることが名の由来という。ササ原を染めるミツバツツジの群落が見事。
444.description_ja_short: 風に笠を飛ばされるのが名の由来という大川嶺連峰の最高峰。
444.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Okawamine Range in the northern Shikoku Karst, 1,562 m. The name is said to come from strong northwest winds blowing travelers' hats away. The colonies of Mitsuba azalea that color its bamboo-grass fields are superb.
444.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Okawamine Range, named for winds said to blow travelers' hats away.

445.description_ja_long: 天塩川の源に構える北見山地の最高峰で標高1,558m。川名はアイヌ語の「梁のある川」に由来し、山頂部は大雪山以北で最大級の高山植物帯が広がる。
445.description_ja_short: 天塩川の源流に立つ北見山地の最高峰。花の頂を持つ。
445.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Kitami Mountains, 1,558 m, standing at the source of the Teshio River. The river's name derives from Ainu words meaning 'river with fish weirs,' and the summit area holds one of the largest alpine flower zones north of Daisetsuzan.
445.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Kitami Mountains at the Teshio River's source, with a flower-rich summit.

447.description_ja_long: 八甲田の山群のうち、青森市と十和田市の境に円錐形の端正な姿を見せる一座。ブナからアオモリトドマツ、ハイマツへと移る植生の垂直分布が鮮やか。
447.description_ja_short: 円錐形の姿が端正な八甲田の一座。植生の移ろいが鮮やか。
447.description_en_long: One of the Hakkoda mountains, showing a trim conical form on the border of Aomori and Towada cities. The vertical zoning of its vegetation is vivid, shifting from beech to Aomori fir and then to creeping pine as the slopes rise.
447.description_en_short: A shapely conical peak of the Hakkoda group, with vivid shifts in its vegetation.

448.description_ja_long: 知床半島の付け根に裾野を広げる秀麗な成層火山で標高1,547m。アイヌの人々にオンネヌプリ「老いた山」と呼ばれ、頂からはオホーツク海を一望する。
448.description_ja_short: 知床の付け根に裾野を引く秀峰。古名は「老いた山」。
448.description_en_long: A graceful stratovolcano spreading its skirts at the base of the Shiretoko Peninsula, 1,547 m high. The Ainu people called it Onnenupuri, 'old mountain,' and from its summit the Sea of Okhotsk spreads out in a single sweeping view.
448.description_en_short: A graceful peak trailing its skirts at the base of Shiretoko; its old name means 'old mountain.'

449.description_ja_long: 奥羽山脈中部、焼石連峰の主峰で標高1,547m。名は頂の焼けたような石に由来する説がある。姥石平の広大な花畑は東北でも屈指の規模と謳われる。
449.description_ja_short: 姥石平に大花畑が広がる焼石連峰の主峰。東北屈指の花の山。
449.description_en_long: The main peak of the Yakeishi Range in the central Ou Mountains, 1,547 m. One theory traces the name to burnt-looking rocks on the summit. The vast flower fields of Ubaishidaira are hailed as among the largest in all of Tohoku.
449.description_en_short: Main peak of the Yakeishi Range, with the vast flower fields of Ubaishidaira; one of Tohoku's finest flower mountains.

450.description_ja_long: 宮崎県西米良村にそびえる標高1,547mの峰で、市房山・天包山と並ぶ米良三山の一つ。一ツ瀬川を隔てて市房山と対峙し、春はアケボノツツジが彩る。
450.description_ja_short: 市房山と対峙する米良三山の一峰。アケボノツツジの山。
450.description_en_long: A 1,547 m peak rising in Nishimera, Miyazaki, counted with Ichifusayama and Tenpozan among the Three Mountains of Mera. It faces Ichifusayama across the Hitotsuse River, and in spring Akebono azaleas color its slopes.
450.description_en_short: One of the Three Mountains of Mera, facing Ichifusayama; a mountain of Akebono azaleas.

451.description_ja_long: 北海道・知床半島の山並みに連なる標高1,544mの峰。斜里町と羅臼町の二つの町にまたがり、半島を貫く山脈の一角として知床の山岳景観を形づくっている。
451.description_ja_short: 知床半島の山並みに連なる峰。二つの町の境に頂を置く。
451.description_en_long: A 1,544 m peak in the mountain chain of Hokkaido's Shiretoko Peninsula. Straddling the two towns of Shari and Rausu, it forms one part of the range that runs down the peninsula and helps shape Shiretoko's mountain scenery.
451.description_en_short: A peak in the Shiretoko chain, its summit set on the border of two towns.

452.description_ja_long: 北海道の山深く、石狩岳の北東に連なる標高1,541mの山。国境を分かつ意を思わせる名を持ち、人里から遠く離れた奥地に静かな頂を構えている。
452.description_ja_short: 石狩岳の北東に構える静かな頂。国境を思わせる名を持つ。
452.description_en_long: A 1,541 m mountain deep in the Hokkaido interior, trailing northeast of Ishikaridake. Bearing a name that suggests the parting of provinces, it keeps a quiet summit in remote back country far removed from any human settlement.
452.description_en_short: A quiet summit northeast of Ishikaridake, bearing a name that evokes old provincial borders.

453.description_ja_long: 愛媛・高知県境に立つ標高1,541mの秀麗な独立峰。通称は中津明神で、麓の中津の神の山が名の由来ともいわれる。夏は山頂で石鎚修験の祭礼が営まれ、展望も広い。
453.description_ja_short: 県境に立つ秀麗な独立峰。山頂で石鎚修験の祭礼が営まれる。
453.description_en_long: A graceful 1,541 m freestanding peak on the border of Ehime and Kochi. Commonly called Nakatsu Myojin, its name is said to derive from the god of Nakatsu at its foot. In summer a rite of Ishizuchi mountain asceticism is held on the wide-viewed summit.
453.description_en_short: A graceful freestanding peak on the prefectural border, where Ishizuchi ascetic rites are held on the summit.

454.description_ja_long: 岩手・秋田県境、裏岩手縦走路の中ほどに横たわる標高1,541mの楯状火山。周囲には遅くまで雪の残る湿原が点在し、雪解けとともに花々が咲き継ぐ雲上の別天地である。
454.description_ja_short: 裏岩手縦走路の中ほどの楯状火山。湿原と花に恵まれる。
454.description_en_long: A 1,541 m shield volcano lying midway along the Ura-Iwate traverse route on the Iwate-Akita border. Moors where snow lingers late are dotted around it, and as the snow melts, flowers bloom in succession in this paradise above the clouds.
454.description_en_short: A shield volcano midway along the Ura-Iwate traverse, blessed with moors and flowers.

455.description_ja_long: 新潟県中越にどっしりと裾野を広げる標高1,537mの古い火山。大岳・青雲岳・袴岳の三峰の総称で、牛頭天王の伝説を宿す神の山。冬に頂稜へ張り出す大雪庇で名高い。
455.description_ja_short: 三つの峰から成る中越の神山。頂稜の大雪庇で知られる。
455.description_en_long: An old volcano spreading broad skirts over Niigata's Chuetsu region, 1,537 m high. A collective name for three peaks, Otake, Aokumodake and Hakamadake, it is a divine mountain of the Gozu Tenno legend, famed for the huge snow cornices on its winter crest.
455.description_en_short: A sacred Chuetsu mountain of three peaks, known for the great snow cornices on its winter crest.

456.description_ja_long: 長野県飯田市の背後に三角錐の姿を見せる標高1,535mの山。名は山上の鞍部を風が吹き越えることに由来し、頂近くには重要文化財の白山社奥宮が鎮座する信仰の山。
456.description_ja_short: 飯田のシンボル。風の吹き越す名を持つ白山信仰の山。
456.description_en_long: A 1,535 m mountain showing a triangular form behind Iida City, Nagano. Its name comes from the wind blowing over a saddle high on the mountain, and near the summit stands the Okumiya of Hakusansha, an Important Cultural Property, marking it as a mountain of faith.
456.description_en_short: Symbol of Iida and a mountain of Hakusan worship, named for the wind crossing its saddle.

457.description_ja_long: 奥多摩と山梨の境に立つ標高1,531mの山。西・中央・東の三つの頂を連ねる姿が名の由来で、山腹には美しいブナ林が残る。古くは神々の集う鹿妻山と呼ばれたという。
457.description_ja_short: 三つの頂を連ねる奥多摩の名峰。山腹のブナの森が美しい。
457.description_en_long: A 1,531 m mountain on the border of Okutama and Yamanashi. Its name comes from the three summits it links, west, central and east, and beautiful beech woods remain on its slopes. In old times it was called Kazumayama, where the gods were said to gather.
457.description_en_short: A famed Okutama peak linking three summits, with beautiful beech forest on its slopes.

458.description_ja_long: 飛騨の中央に横たわる標高1,529mの霊山で、水を日本海側と太平洋側に分かつ分水嶺の山。朝廷に献じたイチイの笏が一位の官位を賜ったことが名の由来と伝えられる。
458.description_ja_short: 飛騨の分水嶺に横たわる霊山。イチイの笏に名を由来する。
458.description_en_long: A sacred 1,529 m mountain lying at the heart of Hida, on the watershed dividing waters between the Sea of Japan and the Pacific. The name is said to derive from a yew-wood scepter presented to the court, which was rewarded with 'ichii,' the first court rank.
458.description_en_short: A sacred mountain on the Hida watershed, named after a yew scepter presented to the court.

459.description_ja_long: 福井県大野市の南東にすっくと立つ標高1,523mの峰。その風格から大野富士と呼ばれ、泰澄の開山伝承を持つ信仰の山。ブナ林と山頂の大展望で日本百名山に数えられる。
459.description_ja_short: 大野富士と称される独立峰。ブナ林豊かな日本百名山。
459.description_en_long: A 1,523 m peak standing tall southeast of Ono City, Fukui. Its dignified bearing earned it the name Ono Fuji, and it is a mountain of faith said to be opened by the monk Taicho. Beech forest and a grand summit panorama rank it among the 100 Famous Japanese Mountains.
459.description_en_short: A freestanding peak called Ono Fuji, rich in beech forest; one of the 100 Famous Japanese Mountains.

460.description_ja_long: 群馬県南西部、御荷鉾山系の最高峰で標高1,523m。南から望めば古武士のごとくずんぐりと、北からはなだらかな三角形に見える。一等三角点の頂は遠く富士山も望む。
460.description_ja_short: 御荷鉾山系の最高峰。見る方角で姿を変え、富士も望む。
460.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Mikabo Range in southwestern Gunma, 1,523 m. Seen from the south it looks stocky like an old warrior, while from the north it appears a gentle triangle. Its summit, with a first-order triangulation point, even commands a distant view of Mt. Fuji.
460.description_en_short: Highest peak of the Mikabo Range, changing its look with the viewpoint; even Mt. Fuji is in sight.

461.description_ja_long: 渡島半島の日本海側にそびえる道南の最高峰で標高1,520m。名はアイヌ語で桜の皮の多い所を意味する言葉に由来するとされ、頂からは海の彼方に奥尻島まで見渡せる。
461.description_ja_short: 道南の最高峰。名はアイヌ語の「桜皮多き所」に由来。
461.description_en_long: The highest peak of southern Hokkaido, rising to 1,520 m on the Sea of Japan side of the Oshima Peninsula. The name is held to derive from an Ainu word meaning a place rich in cherry bark, and from the summit Okushiri Island can be seen far across the sea.
461.description_en_short: Southern Hokkaido's highest peak, named from an Ainu word for a place of much cherry bark.

462.description_ja_long: 日高山脈の南部、ペテガリ岳と神威岳の二つの巨峰に挟まれて立つ標高1,519mの峰。目立たぬ位置ながらピラミッド形の美しい姿を持ち、冬の初登頂は戦後まで残された。
462.description_ja_short: 巨峰の間に潜むピラミッド形の秀峰。道なき日高の奥山。
462.description_en_long: A 1,519 m peak in the southern Hidaka Range, standing between the two giants Petegaridake and Kamuidake. Though set in an inconspicuous position, it has a beautiful pyramidal form, and its first winter ascent was left unclaimed until after the war.
462.description_en_short: A beautiful pyramidal peak hidden between giants, deep in the trailless Hidaka Range.

463.description_ja_long: 南八甲田の最高峰で標高1,517m。東から望む姿が柘植の櫛に見えることが名の由来とされ、裾野には広大な湿原が横たわる。夏、雪解けの進む斜面は一面の花園となる。
463.description_ja_short: 櫛の形をした南八甲田の最高峰。裾野に湿原と花園が広がる。
463.description_en_long: The highest peak of the southern Hakkoda mountains, 1,517 m. The name is said to come from its likeness to a boxwood comb when viewed from the east, and vast moorland lies across its base. In summer the slopes, as the snow melts, become one great garden of flowers.
463.description_en_short: Comb-shaped highest peak of southern Hakkoda, with moorland and flower gardens spreading at its base.

464.description_ja_long: 福島・新潟県境の豪雪地帯に眠る標高1,517mの山。正規の登山道はなく深い薮に覆われ、雪が締まり天候の安定する春の残雪期にのみ、岳人がその頂を目指す。
464.description_ja_short: 深い薮に閉ざされた県境の秘峰。残雪期にのみ頂を許す。
464.description_en_long: A 1,517 m mountain sleeping in the heavy-snow country on the Fukushima-Niigata border. It has no formal trail and is covered in deep brush, so climbers aim for its summit only in spring, when the lingering snow firms up and the weather grows stable.
464.description_en_short: A hidden border peak sealed in deep brush, yielding its summit only in the season of lingering snow.

466.description_ja_long: 兵庫・鳥取県境にそびえる兵庫県の最高峰で標高1,510m。山頂に須賀の宮が祀られたことから須賀ノ山とも呼ばれた。ブナの森と山上の高層湿原が豊かな自然を伝える。
466.description_ja_short: 兵庫県の最高峰。ブナと湿原を抱く中国山地東部の雄峰。
466.description_en_long: The highest peak of Hyogo Prefecture, rising to 1,510 m on the Hyogo-Tottori border. It was also called Suganosen after the Suga shrine once enshrined on its summit. Beech forests and a high moor on its upper reaches convey its rich natural life.
466.description_en_short: Hyogo's highest peak, a mighty mountain of the eastern Chugoku range holding beech woods and moors.

467.description_ja_long: 高知県の山あいに絶壁をめぐらせて立つ標高1,506mの山。名は頂が稲に似た叢に覆われていたことによると伝わり、平家の落人伝説が残る。春はアケボノツツジが彩る。
467.description_ja_short: 絶壁をまとう土佐の山。平家伝説とアケボノツツジの峰。
467.description_en_long: Inamurayama is a 1,506 m mountain rising amid the hills of Kochi, ringed by sheer cliffs. Its name is said to come from a summit once covered in grass resembling rice plants, and legends of Heike refugees linger here. In spring, Akebono azaleas color its slopes.
467.description_en_short: A cliff-girdled mountain of Tosa, keeping Heike legends and spring Akebono azaleas.

468.description_ja_long: 熊本県、阿蘇五岳のほぼ中央で今も噴煙を上げる標高1,506mの活火山。火口の周囲には噴出物の広がる荒涼とした砂の世界が続き、地球の鼓動を間近に伝える山である。
468.description_ja_short: 今も噴煙を上げる阿蘇の心臓部。荒涼たる砂の世界が続く。
468.description_en_long: Mt. Aso is a 1,506 m active volcano in Kumamoto, still trailing smoke near the center of the five peaks of Aso. Around the crater stretches a desolate world of sand spread with volcanic ejecta, a mountain that conveys the very pulse of the earth at close range.
468.description_en_short: The still-smoking heart of Aso, where a desolate world of volcanic sand stretches on.

469.description_ja_long: 富士山の南裾にどっしりと構える愛鷹連峰の最高峰、越前岳で標高1,504m。山頂近くの富士見台は、かつて五十銭紙幣の富士の図の撮影地となったことで知られている。
469.description_ja_short: 富士の南に連なる愛鷹連峰の最高峰。富士の展望で名高い。
469.description_en_long: Ashitakayama's high point is Echizendake, 1,504 m, crowning the range that sits solidly on the southern skirts of Mt. Fuji. Fujimidai near the summit is known as the spot where the image of Fuji used on the old fifty-sen banknote was photographed.
469.description_en_short: Highest point of the Ashitaka range south of Mt. Fuji, famed for its views of Fuji.

471.description_ja_long: 宮城・山形県境に横たわる船形連峰の主峰で標高1,500m。船を伏せた山容が名の由来とも、船形権現の信仰によるともいわれ、山形側では御所山の名で親しまれている。
471.description_ja_short: 船を伏せたような県境の主峰。山形側では御所山と呼ぶ。
471.description_en_long: Funagatayama, 1,500 m, is the main peak of the Funagata range lying along the Miyagi-Yamagata border. The name is said to come from its form like an upturned boat, or from worship of Funagata Gongen, and on the Yamagata side it is fondly called Goshozan.
471.description_en_short: A border peak shaped like an upturned boat, called Goshozan on the Yamagata side.

472.description_ja_long: 阿寒湖の西で噴煙を上げる標高1,499mの活火山。アイヌ語で女山を意味し、雄阿寒岳と夫婦の山として対をなす。山名を冠した花々が砂礫の斜面を飾る日本百名山。
472.description_ja_short: 雄阿寒岳と夫婦をなす女山。噴煙上がる花の日本百名山。
472.description_en_long: Meakandake is a 1,499 m active volcano trailing smoke west of Lake Akan. Its name means 'female mountain' in Ainu, pairing it with Oakandake as husband and wife. Flowers bearing the mountain's name adorn its gravelly slopes, one of the 100 Famous Japanese Mountains.
472.description_en_short: The 'female mountain' paired with Oakandake, a smoking, flower-decked famous peak.

473.description_ja_long: 新潟県糸魚川の海谷山塊に構える標高1,498mの岩山。西と南に安山岩の大絶壁をめぐらせ、山腹に浮かぶ駒の形は義経が乗り捨てた馬が写ったものと伝えられている。
473.description_ja_short: 大絶壁を連ねる海谷山塊の岩峰。義経の駒の伝説を宿す。
473.description_en_long: Komagatake is a 1,498 m rocky peak in the Umidani massif near Itoigawa, Niigata. Great andesite cliffs ring its western and southern faces, and the horse-shaped patch on its flank is said to be the image of a horse that Yoshitsune rode and abandoned.
473.description_en_short: A rock peak of the Umidani massif lined with great cliffs, keeping the legend of Yoshitsune's horse.

474.description_ja_long: 徳島県東部、三つの川を分かつ分水嶺にどっしりと構える標高1,496mの山。全国有数の豪雨地帯にあり、山頂には雨を司る神を祀る雲早神社が静かに鎮座している。
474.description_ja_short: 三つの川を分かつ分水嶺の山。頂に雨乞いの神を祀る。
474.description_en_long: Kumosoyama is a 1,496 m mountain standing solidly on the watershed that divides three rivers in eastern Tokushima. Set in one of Japan's rainiest regions, it holds on its summit the quiet Kumoso Shrine, dedicated to a deity who governs the rain.
474.description_en_short: A watershed mountain dividing three rivers, with a rainmaking deity enshrined on its summit.

475.description_ja_long: 日本海に臨む増毛山地の最高峰で標高1,492m。名はアイヌ語で滝の上にある川を意味する暑寒別川に由来する。東麓には池塘を散りばめた雨竜沼湿原が広がっている。
475.description_ja_short: 増毛山地の最高峰。雨竜沼湿原を東麓に従える豪雪の山。
475.description_en_long: Shokanbetsudake, 1,492 m, is the highest peak of the Mashike mountains facing the Sea of Japan. The name derives from the Shokanbetsu River, Ainu for a river above a waterfall. At its eastern foot spreads the Uryunuma wetland, scattered with small ponds.
475.description_en_short: Highest of the Mashike mountains, a snowy peak with the Uryunuma wetland at its eastern foot.

477.description_ja_long: 札幌市の最高峰で標高1,488mを測る余市三山の主峰。名はアイヌ語のイオチ、蛇の多い所に由来するとの説がある。大正期からスキー登山の山として親しまれてきた。
477.description_ja_short: 札幌市の最高峰。大正以来のスキー登山で親しまれる。
477.description_en_long: Yoichidake, at 1,488 m the highest point in the city of Sapporo, is the main peak of the three Yoichi mountains. One theory traces the name to the Ainu word iochi, a place of many snakes. It has been loved as a ski-mountaineering peak since the Taisho era.
477.description_en_short: Sapporo's highest point, cherished for ski mountaineering since the Taisho era.

478.description_ja_long: 長崎県島原半島の中央、1990年からの大噴火で生まれた溶岩ドームの山。火砕流を繰り返した噴火の中で隆起を続け、平成新山として標高1,483mまで成長した。
478.description_ja_short: 平成の噴火が生んだ溶岩ドーム。島原半島に立つ火の山。
478.description_en_long: Unzendake, in the center of Nagasaki's Shimabara Peninsula, is a lava-dome mountain born of the great eruption that began in 1990. Rising steadily through an eruption that sent down repeated pyroclastic flows, it grew to 1,483 m as Heisei-Shinzan.
478.description_en_short: A lava dome born of the Heisei eruption, a mountain of fire on the Shimabara Peninsula.

479.description_ja_long: 福島県会津地方の中央にどっしり構える標高1,482mの山。名は大和朝廷の役人が太刀を佩いて峰を越えた「佩かせ」に転じたと伝わり、ブナの原生林が広がる。
479.description_ja_short: 太刀を佩いた古伝に名が由来する会津中央の山。ブナ原生林が深い。
479.description_en_long: Hakaseyama is a 1,482 m mountain standing solidly in the center of Fukushima's Aizu region. Its name is said to derive from 'hakase,' the girding of a sword by a Yamato court official who crossed the ridge, and virgin beech forest spreads across the mountain.
479.description_en_short: An Aizu mountain named from an old tale of a sword-girded official, deep in virgin beech forest.

480.description_ja_long: 岩手・秋田県境の火山で、秋田側では乳頭山と呼ぶ。南東に尾瀬に匹敵するといわれる高層湿原・千沼ヶ原を抱き、山麓には鶴ノ湯など古いいで湯が湧く。
480.description_ja_short: 秋田側の名は乳頭山。千沼ヶ原の湿原と山麓のいで湯で知られる。
480.description_en_long: Eboshidake is a volcano on the Iwate-Akita border, called Nyutozan on the Akita side. To the southeast it cradles Senshogahara, a high moor said to rival Oze, while old hot springs such as Tsurunoyu well up at the foot of the mountain.
480.description_en_short: Known as Nyutozan in Akita, famed for the Senshogahara moor and the old hot springs at its foot.

482.description_ja_long: 日高山脈南部に端正な三角錐を立てる標高1,471mの峰。名はアイヌ語で「火を消す川」の意とも海獣ラッコにちなむともいわれ、由来には諸説ある。
482.description_ja_short: 日高南部に立つ端正なピラミッド峰。名の由来には諸説が伝わる。
482.description_en_long: Rakkodake is a 1,471 m peak raising a shapely triangular pyramid in the southern Hidaka Range. Its name is variously explained as Ainu for 'a river that puts out fire' or as deriving from the sea otter, rakko; several theories about its origin are handed down.
482.description_en_short: A shapely pyramid peak in the southern Hidaka Range, its name explained by several theories.

483.description_ja_long: 南アルプス深南部に静まる標高1,470mの秘境の山。尾根にはシロヤシオの群生地があり、山麓の京丸には最後の住人が105歳で世を去った集落跡が残る。
483.description_ja_short: シロヤシオ群れ咲く深南部の秘境峰。麓に京丸の廃村跡が眠る。
483.description_en_long: Kyomaruyama is a 1,470 m mountain lying quietly in the deep southern reaches of the Southern Alps. Its ridge holds colonies of shiroyashio azalea, and at Kyomaru on its foot remain the ruins of a hamlet whose last resident died at the age of 105.
483.description_en_short: A hidden peak of the deep south where shiroyashio azaleas bloom; the lost village of Kyomaru sleeps below.

484.description_ja_long: 高知県中央部の山で、東の物部の白髪山に対し「本山の白髪」と呼ばれる。白く光る結晶片岩に由来する名を持ち、土佐藩が守った樹齢200年近いヒノキが全山を覆う。
484.description_ja_short: 白く光る結晶片岩に名を負う山。藩政期から守られたヒノキが覆う。
484.description_en_long: Shiragayama, in central Kochi, is called 'the Shiraga of Motoyama' against the Shiragayama of Monobe to the east. Named for its white-glinting crystalline schist, the whole mountain is cloaked in hinoki cypress nearly 200 years old, protected since Tosa domain days.
484.description_en_short: Named for white-glinting crystalline schist and cloaked in hinoki cypress guarded since domain days.

485.description_ja_long: 札幌市と京極町にまたがる標高1,464mの古い楯状火山。名はアイヌ語ムイネシリ「箕のような山」の意で、大正期から登山とスキーの山として親しまれた。
485.description_ja_short: 箕を伏せた姿に名を得た札幌近郊の高峰。羊蹄山の眺めが圧巻。
485.description_en_long: Muineyama is a 1,464 m old shield volcano straddling the city of Sapporo and the town of Kyogoku. Its name comes from the Ainu muine-shiri, a mountain like a winnowing basket, and it has been enjoyed for climbing and skiing since the Taisho era.
485.description_en_short: A high peak near Sapporo named for its winnowing-basket form, with a superb view of Mt. Yotei.

486.description_ja_long: 福井県大野市と池田町の境に立つ標高1,464mの山。継体天皇の妃・目子媛が足羽川の源を求めて登ったという伝えから目子ノ岳の古名を持ち、頂の展望が広い。
486.description_ja_short: 王妃目子媛の伝説に名を負う越前の山。頂から福井の高山を一望。
486.description_en_long: Hekosan is a 1,464 m mountain on the border of Ono and Ikeda in Fukui. It bears the old name Menoko-no-dake from the tale that Menokohime, consort of Emperor Keitai, climbed it seeking the source of the Asuwa River, and the view from the summit is broad.
486.description_en_short: A mountain of Echizen named for the legend of consort Menokohime, its summit surveying Fukui's high peaks.

487.description_ja_long: 月山と並び出羽山地に噴出した火山で、かつて出羽三山の一座に数えられた修験の霊山。ブナ原生林に包まれ、奥ノ院に葉山神社が祀られる。標高1,462m。
487.description_ja_short: かつて出羽三山に数えられた修験の山。深いブナの森が包む。
487.description_en_long: Hayama is a volcano that erupted in the Dewa hills alongside Gassan and was once counted among the Three Mountains of Dewa as a sacred peak of Shugendo. Wrapped in virgin beech forest, it enshrines Hayama Shrine at its inner sanctuary. It rises 1,462 m.
487.description_en_short: A Shugendo mountain once counted among the Three Mountains of Dewa, cloaked in deep beech forest.

488.description_ja_long: 愛媛県中央部、皿ヶ嶺連峰の最高峰で標高1,456m。麓には大蛇を退治した修験者・赤鬼法性院の伝説が残り、頂からは石鎚山や雲海の久万高原を見晴らす。
488.description_ja_short: 皿ヶ嶺連峰の最高峰。修験者の伝説を秘め、頂から石鎚山を望む。
488.description_en_long: Ishizumisan, 1,456 m, is the highest peak of the Saragamine range in central Ehime. Its foot preserves the legend of Akaoni Hosshoin, an ascetic who slew a giant serpent, and the summit looks out over Ishizuchisan and the cloud sea of the Kuma highlands.
488.description_en_short: Highest of the Saragamine range, keeping an ascetic's legend, with Ishizuchisan in view from the top.

489.description_ja_long: 福井県大野市の南に平らな稜線を延べる大山塊。機を織る山姥が棲んだという岩穴の話など越前で最も多くの物語を残し、源流にはミズバショウが咲く。
489.description_ja_short: 山姥の物語を数多く残す越前の山。源流にミズバショウが群れる。
489.description_en_long: Ubagatake is a great massif extending a level ridgeline south of Ono, Fukui. It preserves more stories than any other mountain in Echizen, among them the tale of a rock cave where a weaving mountain crone lived, and skunk cabbage blooms at its headwaters.
489.description_en_short: An Echizen mountain rich in mountain-crone tales, with skunk cabbage massing at its headwaters.

490.description_ja_long: 秋田県北秋田市に横たわる標高1,454mの古い火山。古来霊山と仰がれ、夏はヒナザクラなどの花畑、冬は樹氷が山上を飾る「花の百名山」として名高い。
490.description_ja_short: 花と樹氷に彩られる秋田の霊山。なだらかな山容の古い火山。
490.description_en_long: Moriyoshizan is a 1,454 m old volcano lying in Kitaakita, Akita. Revered as a sacred mountain since ancient times, it is celebrated among the 100 Famous Flower Mountains, its heights adorned with fields of hinazakura in summer and rime-covered trees in winter.
490.description_en_short: A sacred old volcano of Akita with gentle contours, colored by flowers and frost-rimed trees.

491.description_ja_long: 愛媛県東部、法皇山脈の一峰で標高1,453m。暴風雨の闇夜に山から飛んだ明るい星が船を導いたという伝説に名を負い、端正な姿は「伊予小富士」と称される。
491.description_ja_short: 船を導いた星の伝説に名を得た法皇山脈の峰。別名は伊予小富士。
