491.description_en_long: Akaboshiyama, 1,453 m, is a peak of the Hoo mountain range in eastern Ehime. Its name rests on the legend of a bright star that flew from the mountain on a stormy night and guided a ship to safety, and its trim figure is praised as the 'Little Fuji of Iyo.'
491.description_en_short: A Hoo-range peak named for the star legend that guided a ship, also called the Little Fuji of Iyo.

492.description_ja_long: 群馬県中央部で赤城山と向かい合う火山群の総称で、上毛三山の一つ。カルデラ湖の榛名湖を榛名富士や最高峰・掃部ヶ岳が囲み、南麓に古社・榛名神社が鎮まる。
492.description_ja_short: 湖を峰々が囲む上毛三山の一つ。南麓に古社・榛名神社が鎮まる。
492.description_en_long: Mt. Haruna is the collective name for a volcanic group facing Mt. Akagi in central Gunma, one of the three mountains of Jomo. Peaks such as Haruna Fuji and the highest, Kamongatake, ring the caldera lake Harunako, and the old Haruna Shrine rests at the southern foot.
492.description_en_short: One of Jomo's three mountains, its peaks ringing a lake, with the old Haruna Shrine at the southern foot.

493.description_ja_long: 大山の南東にそそり立つ標高1,448mの鋭峰。怪鳥が羽ばたく姿にも似た山容から名がついたといわれ、鏡ヶ成から仰ぐ姿は「山陰のマッターホルン」と称される。
493.description_ja_short: 大山の南東に立つ鋭峰。山陰のマッターホルンと呼ばれる。
493.description_en_long: Karasugasen is a sharp 1,448 m peak soaring to the southeast of Daisen. Its name is said to come from a silhouette resembling a monstrous bird beating its wings, and the mountain as seen from Kagamiganaru is praised as the Matterhorn of San'in.
493.description_en_short: A sharp peak southeast of Daisen, celebrated as the Matterhorn of San'in.

494.description_ja_long: 徳島県三好市に均整の三角錐を描き「中津富士」とも呼ばれる山。山頂には弘法大師像が祀られ、スイレンの自生する「黄金の池」が静かに水をたたえている。
494.description_ja_short: 中津富士と呼ばれる端正な峰。頂に黄金の池が水をたたえる。
494.description_en_long: Nakatsusan draws a well-balanced triangular pyramid in Miyoshi, Tokushima, and is also known as Nakatsu Fuji. A statue of Kobo Daishi is enshrined on the summit, where the Golden Pond, home to wild water lilies, quietly holds its still water.
494.description_en_short: A shapely peak called Nakatsu Fuji, with the Golden Pond resting quietly on its summit.

495.description_ja_long: 長野市・千曲市・麻績村の境に立つ標高1,447mの山で、筑北三山の一つ。「ひじり曇れば雨となる」と伝わる雨乞い信仰の山で、頂には360度の展望が開ける。
495.description_ja_short: 雨乞い信仰を伝える筑北三山の一峰。頂は360度の大展望。
495.description_en_long: Hijiriyama is a 1,447 m mountain on the borders of Nagano City, Chikuma and Omi, one of the three Chikuhoku mountains. A peak of rainmaking faith, as in the saying 'when Hijiri clouds over, rain will come,' its summit opens onto a 360-degree panorama.
495.description_en_short: One of the three Chikuhoku mountains, tied to rainmaking faith, with a 360-degree summit view.

496.description_ja_long: 北海道の上川町と遠軽町にまたがる山。ユニークな名はアイヌ語「我ら・射る・木・がある・処」に由来し、旅人が矢を放って運を占った大木の伝えを宿すという。
496.description_ja_short: 矢を放って運を占った大木の伝えをアイヌ語の名に宿す北の山。
496.description_en_long: A mountain straddling the towns of Kamikawa and Engaru in Hokkaido. Its unusual name comes from the Ainu for "place of the tree we shoot at," preserving the tale of a great tree at which travelers loosed arrows to divine their luck.
496.description_en_short: A northern peak whose Ainu name preserves the tale of travelers shooting arrows at a great tree to divine their luck.

497.description_ja_long: 金沢市南部、犀川の源流域に頭をもたげる山で、名は3つの峰を連ねる姿に由来する。手つかずの自然が残り、5月にはホンシャクナゲが岩尾根を彩る。
497.description_ja_short: 三つの峰に名を負う犀川源流の山。春はシャクナゲが岩尾根を飾る。
497.description_en_long: A mountain lifting its head at the headwaters of the Saigawa River in southern Kanazawa, named for its form of three linked peaks. Untouched nature remains here, and in May rhododendrons brighten its rocky ridges.
497.description_en_short: A Saigawa headwaters peak named for its three summits; in spring rhododendrons deck its rocky ridge.

498.description_ja_long: 福井県大野市の岐阜県境近くに盛り上がる標高1,442mの大山塊。源平合戦に敗れた落人が頂に住んだという伝説に名を負い、尾根をヒノキとシャクナゲが飾る。
498.description_ja_short: 平家落人の伝説に名を得た県境の山。尾根にシャクナゲが咲く。
498.description_en_long: A large 1,442 m massif swelling up near the Gifu border in Ono, Fukui. It owes its name to a legend that fugitives defeated in the Genpei War lived on its summit, and hinoki cypress and rhododendrons adorn its ridges.
498.description_en_short: A border mountain named for a legend of Heike fugitives; rhododendrons bloom along its ridges.

499.description_ja_long: 岩手・秋田県境、真昼山地の主峰で標高1,439m。かつてマタギが分け入った獣多き深山で、稜線の草原をニッコウキスゲの大群落が黄に染める花の山でもある。
499.description_ja_short: マタギが歩いた真昼山地の主峰。稜線にニッコウキスゲの大群落。
499.description_en_long: The 1,439 m principal peak of the Mahiru Mountains on the Iwate-Akita border. Once a deep, game-rich wilderness entered by matagi hunters, it is also a mountain of flowers, where vast colonies of daylilies dye its ridgeline grasslands yellow.
499.description_en_short: Principal peak of the Mahiru Mountains once roamed by matagi hunters, with vast daylily colonies on its ridgeline.

244.description_ja_long: 志賀高原の中心に座る火山で、山頂は志賀山と裏志賀山に分かれる。渦を巻いて流れた溶岩の跡を留める珍しい山で、周囲には四十八池や大沼池など池と湿原が点在する。
244.description_ja_short: 志賀高原の中心の火山。渦巻く溶岩跡と池めぐりの山。
244.description_en_long: Shigayama is a volcano seated at the heart of Shiga Kogen, its summit divided into Shigayama and Ura-Shigayama. A rare mountain preserving traces of lava that flowed in swirls, it is surrounded by scattered ponds and marshes such as the Shijuhachi Ponds and Onuma Pond.
244.description_en_short: The volcano at the heart of Shiga Kogen, with swirling lava traces and a circuit of ponds.
